lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, got a few minutes to serve your country?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh... sure...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Cool. Help me slice this birthday cake into 300 million slices.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Five million, three hundred and twenty-two thousand, five hundred and forty.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Five million, three hundred and twenty-two thousand, five hundred and thirty-nine.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer. Mortimer!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Five million, three hundred and twenty-two thousand, five hundred and thirty-eight.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, counting the seconds until Labor Day isn't going to make it get here any quicker.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, they've made huge advances in engineering these things.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It was rated #1 by Consumer Reports! In the luxury category, no less! Don't you like luxury?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: The answer's still no.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's so easy to maneuver, you'd hardly even know you were -

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: For the last time: I don't care how easy or luxurious it is, I am not pushing you around in that stroller.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You coming over tonight for must-see TV?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But it's summertime. Those sitcoms are in re-runs.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What sitcoms? I'm talking about the season finale of Skating With The Spoiled Housewives Of Cannibal Island.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'll pass, thanks.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, but if Ariel can turn a triple lutz, she gets to eat Brendalynne - off of Brendalynne's own Swarovski platter! Are you sure you wanna miss that?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That dual-core laptop looks pretty nice, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'd never get a dual-core myself.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? Why's that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Out of respect for Aaron Burr.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well that movie was certainly a "transforming" experience.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: My favorite part was where the director transformed two hours into the biggest waste of time since arguing on the Internet was invented.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you see the part where they transformed $400 million into a brain pacifier?! SO AWESOME.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Dude, are you going to the vending machine? If you bring me back some mini-pretzels, I'll be your best friend!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, friendship isn't about the things one can "get" from another. It's about trust, respect, patience...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Is borrowing a dollar anywhere in there? 'Cause your best friend needs to do that, too.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, my dreams of being a big-shot theatre director are shot. Stupid reviews. SO MUCH FOR ART, I GUESS.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You have to admit that asking an audience to bring their own SCUBA gear is a little over the top.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's called "Shakespeare in the Waterpark" for a REASON, Monte!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whoa! I just barely avoided getting beaten up by a mobster in a dive bar!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, you're playing the Wootquest game on Woot today too, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, I'm really a lucky guy. Lucky to have friends who are always ready to come to my aid when I'm in a jam, who always spring into action when I need -

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm not going to clean your ear scabs again.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why not? Didn't you hear how lucky I am?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte! You've gotta join Google+ so we can be friends there!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I didn't add you on Friendster. I didn't add you on Myspace. I didn't add you on Facebook. Why would I add you on Google+?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because THIS one has CIRCLES!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte! You've gotta join my room on Turntable.FM!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Didn't we just go through this yesterday with the stupid Google thing?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, but this is different! It has MUSIC!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So you can viciously judge people's tastes INSTANTANEOUSLY!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you hear the news?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What news? What happened?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't know. I just ask that so in case something DOES happen, I won't seem so uninformed.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, Mortimer. How was your weekend?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh man, it was AWESOME.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Good, good.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Aren't you gonna ask WHY it was awesome?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I find that, in general, I'm happier when I don't ask.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Holy crap it's hot outside! I can't wait for winter.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But you spent the whole winter complaining about the cold and wishing for summer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, in an ideal world, the seasons would only be about 10 days long so I wouldn't get sick of them.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm not buying a new TV until I can get one that's at least 960 inches.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You mean 96 inches.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But that wouldn't even fit into your house.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, I'm a consumer, not an engineer. If the industry wants my business, they'll figure something out.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: "Sport shirts", Woot? Seriously? What happened to you guys? Ever since-

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, could you please keep your complaining down to a dull roar?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I'm not complaining. I'm rehearsing.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Rehearsing? For what?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The Woot movie. I'm up for Disgruntled Commenter #2.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: TGGIF, huh, Monte?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think you put an extra G in there, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I was thanking Gilbert Gottfried. You ask me, that guy doesn't get enough credit.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wow, I've got to get my hands on those Climate Cubes.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I know. Pretty cool.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I didn't know equipment like that was even available to the general public.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, yeah. I'm a bit of an amateur meteorologist myself. In fact, I'm one of KWUT-TV's volunteer Weatherscouts. When you watch the news, you might see one of my barometric pressure readings!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So wait a minute - these Climate Cubes don't actually let you control the weather?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, did you find the secret Bag of Crap on the site today?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What?! There's a Bag of Crap?! Where?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You have to hunt around.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm not falling for this again. You're lying, aren't you?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Only one way to find out.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: CURSE YOU, MONTE!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm thinking about going back to school.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, really? That's admirable. You'll really open doors for yourself with a degree. What are you going to study?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Degree? No, I just think it'd be awesome to be a grown-up in the 7th grade. I would RULE that junior high!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, what are you doing with my phone?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Check it out! I Googled a bunch of area codes in the Midwest. I've been calling people all day and saying, "Hot enough for ya?"

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And why do you need to use MY cell phone?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't want them to have MY number. The Midwest is full of jerks.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why do you say that? Lots of our customers live there. We have FRIENDS there!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, so far everyone I've called has been really angry.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte, wanna help the cause? My kid's selling cookies for school.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You don't have kids. Believe me, I'm grateful for that fact, but you don't have kids.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Okay, fine, I'M selling cookies. For a good cause. Just buy a couple already.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's the cause? These are just frosted sugar cookies from the grocery store bakery. And they're $5 a piece?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, this will go a lot smoother if you just stop asking questions and make with the cookie buyin'.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: All right! Now I can replace the defective GPS I bought from that big-box store.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why don't you just take the defective one back to the store?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I was gonna, but I couldn't remember how to get there.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, snazzy case! I didn't know you had an iPad.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't. Turns out these things make perfect bacon caddies.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Isn't that whole bacon thing a little played out now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's why I carry it in a case. Fatty, fried pork is the only thing I value more than my hip cred.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've decided to join a support group for my gambling addiction.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, Mortimer, I'm very proud of you. It's a big step to admit you have a problem and seek help by banding together with others who are battling those same demons.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, what? You mean, this group isn't going to support my gambling habit by giving me some extra cash?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, they're going support you by listening.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: BUT I CAN'T WAGER "LISTENING" AT THE CRAPS TABLE!!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Before you even ask, Monte - yes, you are reading that framed certificate correctly.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You were the "poet of the month" in fifth grade?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uh-huh. My mom found it in her attic and mailed it to me. But listen, I don't want this to affect our friendship. Just because I'm an award-winning author doesn't mean that I expect some additional respect. I just want to be treated like a normal guy, you know?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How modest of you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do you think "Mortimer, Laureate" would fit on my business card?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How was your weekend, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh. Lame. Wasted all Saturday afternoon playing stupid Ultimate Frisbee.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, and you didn't enjoy it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not at all! It's not an ultimate Frisbee, like a Frisbee with lasers and chainsaws and stuff coming out of it. It's just some dumb sport called Ultimate Frisbee. It's like catch with points.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well I actually think it's quite fun. It's competitive, but it's not as ruthless or barbaric as contact sports. And nothing's as satisfying as when you get that flick of the wrist just right and the disc soars perfectly through the air, without a hint of wobble... Are you even listening? You're imagining a frisbee that shoots lasers, aren't you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Some of the lasers would be red, and some would look like lightning bolts. But the most powerful lasers would probably be invisible.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Your phone seems to be going off quite a lot today, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, I signed up for this thing where I get inspirational quotes from famous historical figures periodically texted to me.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How interesting! Isn't it great that we can use the technology of today to reconnect with our past, that even while we look ahead to the future, we can remember where we came from? Any particularly insightful quotes you'd like to share?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh I don't read the quotes. I usually just set my phone to vibrate, put it on the back of my chair, and then it's like history is giving me a back massage.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Good news, Monte! I think the new receptionist, Maggie, is starting to take quite a shine to me!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, I hate to break it to you, but her telling you to get away from her desk before she took a stapler to your eyes isn't the best sign.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But it's the WAY she said it. She didn't call me creepy this time, and she didn't even throw anything. I think I'm finally wearing her down!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, Monte, can I borrow five bucks? There's this game in the break room I've been trying to beat and I need more quarters.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Management put a game in the break room?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, but it's REALLY hard. You only get one try each time, but I've nearly cleared the chips and stale pastries from the board.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh. Right. Tell you what, if you bring me back something from one the candy levels, you've got a deal.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know what I miss the most about childhood? Summer vacation.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Dude, DEFINITELY. Three months off would be SO great, you know?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How about you? Do you miss anything about being a kid?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sure! Lots of things! Mostly, though, I miss knowing where my dad was.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So I might check out the Bacon thing tomorrow. Want to come with?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Before I say yes, let me clarify. Is this the show at the art museum with all the paintings of popes?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Art museum? I didn't even know we HAD one of those!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Right, of course. And is it a scholarly debate about the real author of Shakespeare's works?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Schol- hey, get OVER yourself, Monte! I'm talking about the World Bacon Brunch at the Bacon Bits Truck Stop And Diner just past the interstate! Canadian AND American and I think some from Mexico too! All you can eat!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think I have plans.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm excited about the 5G iPhone rumors.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, why do these phones always have Roman numerals in their name?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, "G" isn't a Roman numeral.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, but "i" is. BOOM! Now I'm the smart one for a change!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I... I don't know what to say.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Maybe now you'll show a little respect for my paper on the luminous aether.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No? Are you sure? I mean, I can pay. Not just pay, but PAY. Can't we work something- Hello? Hello?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Who had the good sense to hang up on you just now, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: This stuck-up medical receptionist. You believe I can't find a single doctor willing to help me stay cool in this heat by injecting freon into my veins?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, it's good to know there are still some things money can't buy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've heard people talk about healthcare rationing, but I never thought it would affect me. Thanks a lot, Sweden.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm thinking about going camping this weekend.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: In this heat? That sounds awful, but I'll bring plenty of booze to help us cool off.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It wasn't an invitation.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, who the heck else would you take camping?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't know... someone I like?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'll bet you a thousand bucks the Cowboys are going to win the Super Bowl this year.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Didn't they already win it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uh, yeah. Five times. '71, '77, '92, '93, and '95.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, I don't think they should win another one until everyone else has had a turn.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What kind of socialist nightmare do you live in, man?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: (slurp, glorph) Aaah! Hey, Monte, you ready for the greatest mixed drink of your life?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What... what on Earth... that color...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know! I call it a melon-mint mudslidetinigarita! Here, let me pour you one!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No. Really. No thanks.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You're right - the only way to enjoy a melon-mint mudslidetinigarita is straight from the pitcher! Open up!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't know what's supposed to be so great about making phone calls over the Internet.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, here we go with another episode of Monte's Fuddy-duddy hour. Shall I get you a bicarbonate of soda, Grandpa?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, my Grandpa probably never had a single dropped call in his entire life.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And he probably paid out the nose every time he wanted to call Thailand or Denmark, too!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But - but I don't know anyone in Thailand or Denmark -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Exactly! Now if you'll excuse me, I'm going to have virtual lunch with my friends Sarawong and Esben.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, you wanna take a bike ride? It's a really nice day out.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nah, I'm gonna play this Xbox bike game instead. It's better than a real bike ride.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: W-what? How can you say that? It doesn't give you any exercise, you don't get out and interact with your community, you don't get that satisfying feeling of the warm sun evaporating away a hard-earned sweat -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, like I said, better than a real bike ride.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How'd the hot date go last night?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, uh, Sherry's nice, but I don't think it's going to go anywhere. We're looking for different things right now.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: She's looking for a guy who's not me, and I'm looking for a girl who's not looking for a guy who's not me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you get my email about how I brought in donuts? I thought it would be a nice way to make Monday a little less of a bummer.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, thanks! Where are the donuts?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Haha! I didn't bring any!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But why - what -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You just brightened my Monday by falling for it! Thanks a lot, sucker!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, man, why'd they have to schedule a Woot-Off on a day when I have so much work to get done?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: For some reason I never seem to have that problem.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: This has to be the worst Woot-Off in the history of all time.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Didn't you say that last month?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't know. Who can remember last month?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whew, glad that Woot-Off is over. I mean, what a workout!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: A workout? You think buying things on the Internet is a workout?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm a very aggressive clicker.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've been thinking about buying a hybrid car.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's great, Mortimer! With their gas and electric motors, they save gas and help protect the environment!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Gas and electric? No, I was thinking half-car, half-alligator.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's Saturday, what do you say we go out on the town?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No way! Last time you invited me out to a club it was BO-RING! No music, no bar, just a bunch of unattractive nerds sitting around talking.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That was my book club, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Boy am I glad there's still another day left before work on Monday!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But, it's Sunday. We go back to the office tomorrow, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Shh! If I pretend to be genuinely confused about what day it is, it'll seem like I'm crazy, and firing a crazy person for skipping work is discrimination!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Your plan is flawless.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh man, when are they going to add a dislike button to Facebook? I mean, really!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes, just what we need, more negativity on the internet.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I dislike your sarcastic tone, Monte!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, didn't you get those cedar grilling planks last time?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, and it was a huge mistake.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? How come?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: They taste terrible. And they rip up your mouth and throat on the way down. I couldn't even finish one.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm sorry, I forgot who I was talking to.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Seriously, I drowned them in barbecue sauce, and still, yuck.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, look at you, Mortimer. What are you so dressed up for?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because you know what's back? Swing dancing.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Seriously? I hadn't heard about a new swing revival. Since when?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Since I forgot to do laundry and this zoot suit is all I had clean.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sure you don't want me to pick you something up? I'm meeting my guy after work.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No. I told you, it's not my thing.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK. Your loss. I'm telling you, this stuff will blow your mind.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Remind me again: why, exactly, do you want HIGHER-fructose corn syrup?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I got a jones, man. The legal stuff just doesn't do it for me anymore.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, Monte, you see that truffle butter on Wine.Woot today? You think that would be good on grilled poultry?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, sure, yeah. Wow, that'd be delicious. My mouth is watering just- hey, where are you going?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Up to the roof. Gotta check my pigeon traps.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, maybe that Gaddafi guy was just misunderstood.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? What are you saying? He was a brutal dictator, a deranged madman, an oppressive-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know, I know. You're right. I guess it's just hard for me to totally dislike anyone who shares my crush on Condoleezza Rice.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You been reading the Woot blog? Looks like those Woot writers are having a good time at the Penny Arcade Expo in Seattle.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Better them than me. I couldn't stand to be around all those gamer freaks. Yuck.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, Mortimer, don't you spend hours a day playing video games?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah. And would YOU want to crowd into a convention hall with 65,000 people like me?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I see your point.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I would like soccer if it was more like foosball.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How so? More scoring?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, played by tiny little men on a table in my house. Come on, tell me you wouldn't want to see that.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: GEEZ, I hate these suit and tie affairs, you know? So uncomfortable.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Would you keep it down, doofus? Just have a tiny sandwich and be quiet.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What, could they not afford full-size sammies or something? No wonder everyone's so morose.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm sorry your high standards are not being met by this funeral.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, at least I won't have to go far after I die of boredom.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yo, Monte! I need you to do me a favor.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh. What is it now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I need you to write "Mortimer will be an hour late tomorrow" on a sticky note and stick it on your monitor.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, you're going to be late tomorrow?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I was late today. Just make sure the boss sees the note when he comes by. I told him I told you about it and you forgot.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, can you believe this week's Woot video podcast? Some people have no pride or dignity.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're picking on a guy who dressed as a dog for the sake of comedy?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah! I mean, have a little self-respect, y'know?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Didn't you once drink a bottle of corn syrup and end up needing someone to drive you home from work because you were so sick?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah but that was for a DARE, not a podcast.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: When I die, I want to have super awesome last words on my tombstone.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, okay. Like some elegant observation, or more of a pithy quote?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I was thinking more like my Uncle Eddy's last words.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Which were?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: "Don't tell ME that I can't punch a lion in the nose!" There was more, but they were hard to understand between the screams and chewing.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: For the last time, I'm not going to guess the random famous person you're imagining in your head.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh come on! Take ONE guess! Do you want a hint?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Fine. John Popper.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: HOW DID YOU KNOW THAT?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's ALWAYS John Popper with you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm thinking of investing in the sock market again.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, that can be a- Wait. Did you say sock market?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah. You can make a lot of money flipping socks.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm getting that feeling like I'm having a stroke again.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I had that same feeling two years ago when the toe-sock bubble burst. Look for a proven performer, like a solid black tube sock, to carry you through.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do you ever get the feeling you're being watched?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Can't say that I do.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So, never? Not at home, in your house?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No. Why? Do I seem like the paranoid type?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No. I just wanted to make sure my hiding spot was good.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm glad that people started making realistic video games.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Realistic?? It seems like all the games are about zombies, or aliens, or -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, that's better than Tetris. Why would shapes kill other shapes? It's ridiculous.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, you haven't done any work all day. What gives?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh come on, Monte. It's only a matter of time before we're all replaced by robots.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ah, I see. You're demoralized by the idea that you could be replaced by a machine.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I'm just trying to set an example for the robots. The bosses aren't gonna program them to goof off, so they'll have to learn it somewhere.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Say, have you been watching the U.S. Open?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, I have, actually! Isn't it exciting?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Boy, I'll say! But I was a little shocked to find out the U.S. wasn't 24 hours. And how did I miss that the U.S. was closing in the first place?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: We were locked in and didn't even know it! You think someone would have made an announcement or something.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do you ever worry about the Yellowstone Caldera?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's that, some kinda Bigfoot?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, it's an enormous volcano underneath Wyoming. If it erupts it'll kill us all.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Bigfoot could kill us all. He just chooses not to.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's not Bigfoot!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Or is that just what he wants you to think?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, I guess. Except in other parts of the world, where it's 11-9-10.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, we'll show them in a couple of months. They won't have any choice but to do it our way on 11-11-11.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, today I just wanted to say... well, we've got nothing at all in common, but... I still respect you as a fellow American.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wow, thanks buddy. I feel the same way about you. Hey, you want some of these brownies I'm eating?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Aren't those my brownies? The brownies I made myself?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, that's why I figured you'd want some. We're in this together, right? Me and you against the world?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ... hand me that corner one, please.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You got it, pal.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, you're kinda into 19-century French politics, right? Who do YOU think would win in a knife fight between Adolphe Thiers and Louis Jules Trochu?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: A knife fight between- hey, did you just look all that up so you could mess with me?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ha ha, yeah! I don't know who any of those people are! Pretty sweet joke, right?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Touche.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What? Speak English!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Another Woot-Off, huh? Man, that's gotta be so much work.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Totally. The warehouse folks, the buyers, the copywriters - they all bust their butts to make the Woot-Off happen.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I meant the guys who change the orange lights.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Late again, huh? You better stop hitting that snooze button in the morning.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I only hit it once.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Then why didn't your alarm clock wake you up again?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because when I hit the snooze button, I used a hammer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My neighbors called the cops when I tried setting up my man cave!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Under what pretense? They have no legal right to dictate how you arrange your own things.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know, right? I had just started to light the dynamite I was using to make the actual cave part.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, are there some stuck-up people around here or what? You should see the looks I get when I ride my bike through the sidewalk.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, the sidewalk is reserved for pedestrian traffic, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, did I say sidewalk? My bad! I meant "bathroom."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Whoa! Is that a wine sale on the weekend?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I just saw it, too, and had to drink all the wine I had to make room for it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, how much wine was that, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Six bottles. Say, what are you and your car up to, Monte? I hear the emergency room is beautiful this time of year.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're up early this Sunday morning, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Got a lot to do today.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh yeah? What are you up to?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, first on my to-do list here: sleep in. Oh wait... NOOOO!!!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, all I did was download a pack of animated smiley GIFs and it killed my computer.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, now you know to be careful when downloading things from untrustworthy sites.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: More like "now I know to be careful to get a computer that isn't allergic to happiness."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do you believe in the concept of sin, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Totally. Just look at the world around us.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I know what you mean. War, oppression, suffering...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I was thinking more like trail mix with chocolate chips in it. I just can't help myself.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You bought three packs, huh? Do you really need 15 t-shirts?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Totally. One to wear for each day of the week, and one to shower in for each day of the week.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You wear t-shirts in the shower?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well what do you wear? Collared shirts?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'd really like to get my hands on whoever took the last cup of coffee. That's the third time this week I've had to make a new one.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I know. People treat the coffee machine like you wouldn't believe. Just the other day I had to scoop out big handfuls of grounds because somebody didn't use a filter.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, what's a "filter"?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: When are you going to tell me who your big office crush is?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It doesn't matter. I'm not that into her anymore.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why not? What happened?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Let's just say you should never decide on a soulmate until you take a good look at her cubicle décor.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Aha! It's the girl with the poster over her desk that says "LIFE IS SHORT, BUY THE SHOES!" I knew it!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How could I have been such a fool?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Don't you find that Saturdays in September have a certain poignant quality?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What, now?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Days like today, I mean. The freedom of Saturday mingles with the imminent end of summer for a bittersweet, melancholy -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: WHOOOAAA! Did you see the hit that State's D-backs laid on that wideout? This calls for another Jell-O shot!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Maybe it's just me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Boy, there are some real babes on this online dating site.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, yeah? Let me see - hey, that's not an online dating site. It's Foreign Policy Quarterly.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So you're saying I shouldn't send a love letter to this "Angela Merkel" person?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't believe it! Our so-called friend Milo just sent me an email saying I was "toxic" and he no longer wanted anything to do with me! "Toxic", he says!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, Milo talked about that a lot on the trip to Costa Rica we took last month.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wha-? You guys took a trip to Costa Rica without me? What did that two-faced little creep say about me?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mostly he's just tired of you making fun of his second face.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, come on! That's like the funniest birth defect I've ever seen!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, I was thinking about going rollerskating tonight...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No can do, Monte. I only rollerblade. That way, if I get into a knife fight along the way, I have a weapon.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You've never actually seen rollerblades, have you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I hate lattes. All that foamy milk, ugh.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? Personally, I love how soft and fluffy milk gets when it's steamed.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's just the thing! The rest of the world gave up steam power long ago! When are lattes going to wake up and start using gas or electricity? This is the 21st century, people!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Come on, Monte, enough with the silent treatment! It was just a prank.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How many times do I have to say I'm sorry, huh?! A million jillion?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, I never would've even tried that prank if I knew you couldn't tell the difference between lip balm and Superglue.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why so down today, Monte? Other than the whole "being at work" thing, I mean.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Tammy broke up with me last night.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Bummer, dude. Did she give you the old "it's not you, it's me" speech?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Actually, it was the "it's not you, it's your creepy co-worker who keeps showing up on our dates" routine.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: UGH! That old line? You're better off without her, dude.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Check it out, Monte! It's my offical Mortimer App for phones! Here, give it a shot.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You have your own vanity app now? What's it... It's not doing anything.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: EXACTLY! You have to pay money to make it work and, even then, getting it to do anything useful is a total crap shoot!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So, it's just like you, only portable. It's so stupid, it may actually be genius.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My developers are working on a "Who's Got the Best Lunch to Steal" add-on, too. I'm going to be so RICH.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Go on. Tell the nice man you tackled that you're sorry.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sigh. I'm sorry I jumped on you and reached inside your pockets but I thought you were stealing Monte's car.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And next time?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Next time, I'll try to take notice of the bright orange vest that says "VALET."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And here's your stupid tip money back that I stole for licorice, geez. Can I be done apologizing now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, Monte, how many jokes about how rough Mondays can be do you think have been told, like, ever?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Enough that you can probably skip it today. In fact, I encourage you to do so.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh good. 'Cause, man, Mondays, you know? Am I right?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Big announcement today! You excited? I said YOU EXCITED?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, it'll be interesting to finally see what awesome features the new phone will have -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Phone? What phone? I'm talking about the announcement of where the 2014 Beer Pong World Cup will be held!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh. I should've guessed.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ESPN keeps saying South Padre Island is a lock, but I've got a feeling Chapel Hill is gonna pull it out.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: WE-HENS-DAY, oh yeah, I'm dreaming about Wednesday, bayee-bayay -

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, Mortimer, could you keep the noise down to a piercing shriek?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sorry, genius knows no decibel limits! It's about time somebody wrote the ultimate Wednesday song, and I'm that somebody! What does Wednesday mean to you, Monte?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, right now it means my loudmouth officemate is getting on my nerves.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: LOUDMOUTH officeMATE a-getting on my NERVES, and I ju-hust - hey, do you know anything that rhymes with "nerves"?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, Mortimer. If you're not doing anything this weekend, you're welcome to swing by my place for Broctoberfest.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you say Brat-toberfest? I love bratwurst!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, "Broctoberfest." I'm going to celebrate the beginning of broccoli season by trying out all kinds of awesome broccoli recipes!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh. I see. Would it be mean to tell you what a sad, strange little monkey you are? I mean, if it's true, you'd want to know, right?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The day I've been waiting for is here! A Flip HD camera on Woot and a blender on Sellout!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Please, Mortimer, before you spend any money, let me try just one more time to convince you that your idea just isn't going to-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: NOPE! Not listening! Nothing you can say can stop Project Margarita's-Eye View!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But see, once the blender gets going, the camera's just gonna get chewed up by the-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Aw, you're just jealous you didn't think of it first.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, here's that bag of yard waste you asked me to bring to the office.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, thanks. Here, I'll take that.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So what are you going to do with - HEY! Watch it! You're dumping it all over the floor!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, I WAS going to make a big leaf pile for us to play in, but there aren't any leaves in here at all! This whole bag is grass clippings!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Great idea. Now there are grass clippings all over the place. What fun.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah. Remember this the next time you're procrastinating about raking up your leaves.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Boy, these Occupy Wall Street people are so misguided.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why do you think so? Because their anger is misplaced? Because their analysis is wrong? Because they don't have a clear program or list of demands?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, because of all the streets in Manhattan to camp out on, they picked the most boring one. Give me a call when it's time to occupy Broadway. THAT sounds like fun.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, did you see that this week's video Wootcast is an interview with Ken Jennings?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Mm-hm.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And that you can see the full interview on the Woot blog?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, yeah.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, what did you think of it? I thought it was funny and insightful - how about you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, what? Look, when you keep babbling to me about- about- whatever it is you're talking about, it's REALLY hard for me to concentrate on drawing this picture of Cobra Commander.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ha ha, funny hat! That helmet covered in spinning orange lights is the perfect headgear for this Woot-Off.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: There's a Woot-Off today? I just thought it was a cool hat.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: There've been some nice deals in this Woot-Off, but I'm holding out for a Jet Ski.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, Mortimer, you might not want to do that. It seems unlikely that a Jet Ski will show up in this Woot-Off.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why do you say that? How do you know? WHO DO YOU HAVE ON THE INSIDE?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You watching any of the MLB playoffs, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah! I'm cheering for the Brewers. Without them, society would have no beer!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Actually, they're not real brewers...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And don't get me started on the Cardinals. You're telling me those birds look like that without PEDs? Yeah, right.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, I broke down. I preordered the iPhone 4S. But I still don't understand why you would need more than one.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, what? You mean because of the "S"? That's just part of the name. It's not plural.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Of course I understand that. I'm not a complete idiot.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do you need to use my computer to log in and cancel your order?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I mean - yes, please.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Got a great business idea for you to invest in, Monte: We rent out an old sports stadium and do mega-bumper cars!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That sounds expensive. First for the stadium, and then we'd have to buy a bunch of cars.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not if we make it BYOC!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: BYOC? Bring your own... oh.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, really, BYOC&B. They'd have to bring the bumpers, too.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm thinking of taking fencing lessons.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's excellent, Mortimer. A lot of people think it's all action and sword fighting, but there really is a lot of art and subtlety to it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sure, sure, as long as the classes are on a pirate ship. That's a deal breaker for me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How big is too big when it comes to rims for my Escalade? Or is there even such a thing as "too big"?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You don't have an Escalade, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, I don't shoot you down when you talk about things you don't have, like friends.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: C'mon, Monte! It's time to fight the power! OCCUPY RIB STREET!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Rib Street? What?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: For too long, that barbecue joint down the street has withheld delicious dry-rubbed meats from us until we agree to pay their exorbitant prices! We can take them DOWN, Monte!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So you want to protest for free ribs?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Who WOULDN'T? OCCUPY RIB STREET!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Can you try occupying your desk for once?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Will you PLEEEEASE go see that new version of The Thing with me in the theater?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No way. I'm not all that into horror movies, and I'm definitely not into cruddy re-makes of good films. Just go yourself.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I can't just go to a movie by myself!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because the theater manager insists I have a chaperone with me at all times.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, right. I forgot about that debacle with the Sno-Caps.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You want a slice of pizza?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sure, thanks! Hey, wait a sec, this pizza is cold.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Duh. Most things that come out of the refrigerator are.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So, um, how old is it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You'll have to ask whoever ordered it and left it in the office fridge. Now are you gonna eat pizza or keep asking me stupid questions all day?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I know this is a silly question, but are you going to do any work today?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No can do, Monte. My team needs me.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Your team? Are you talking about the World Series?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yep! See, when I slack, my team always does well, and I don't want to jinx them now.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I see. And which team are you rooting for again?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I don't like to pick sides. Makes the other team feel bad.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh. Oh man. That's... that's a lot of water.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I just wanted us to get some practice in!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Were those company devices? Oh man oh man oh man.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Jeez, is this what I get for trying to help?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: For one thing, you are SO off the Halloween party-planning committee.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: HOW WAS I SUPPOSED TO KNOW THAT THE APPLES YOU BOB FOR ARE FRUIT?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know what's awesome about Sundays?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Not having to go to work to hang out with annoying co-workers who are always up in your business?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Exactly. You can do it in the comfort of your own home! So what time should I come over?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, a new Woot site! I'm so excited about Home.Woot!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Eh. Whatever.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: New daily homegoods deals featuring top-shelf brands at the lowest prices anywhere, and all you can say is "whatever"?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've got more substantial things to occupy my time than filling my house with a bunch of fancy tablecloths.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, I get it. You got evicted again.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Here's a copy of my lease! Show me where it says I can't use the apartment to make my own fireworks!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Pumpkin time! Let's carve, buddy!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No way. Not after last year. Seventeen stitches, remember?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, the knife slipped. It could've happened to anybody.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It slipped because you were trying to carve a pumpkin and watch that Facts of Life Halloween special at the same time.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You can never let anything go. You're just like Blair.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And when I got out of the car and looked at the handle...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: There was a bloody hook hand hanging from it, right?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Exactly! And that's totally why I'm late for work today.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Right. Well at least it wasn't because you had to drop off a dead prom queen at the cemetery like yesterday.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow. You've certainly gone all out this year, haven't you, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Huh? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I mean all these skulls you've got on your desk! Pretty gruesome Halloween decorations, buddy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Halloween decorations?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ... I'm just going to go back to my desk now.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You do that, buddy. You do that.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Toilet paper, balloons full of paint, eggs set out to rot... Getting ready for a few tricks this Halloween?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What? No, these are the treats.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're giving these out to children?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah! On Halloween, my house goes from "the place where the crazy guy lives" to "The Arsenal."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm sure your neighbors will appreciate that.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What can I say? I love working with young people.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just playin' this sweet new "Banana Brothers" game on my Xbox.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, cool. Does it have a two-player mode?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: If it does, I'll never know.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh. Okay then.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte, I figured we could go in on that his-and-hers French press set for sale on Home.Woot today.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, thanks! That's pretty thoughtful.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You can have the "hers" because you're such a girl! Ha ha!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ah, because being a girl is such an insult.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's what beer commercials taught me. A lack of manliness is pathetic.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Says the guy who just gave a pink coffee press to his buddy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Braaaaaains! Braaaaaiii- Oh, come on. Where's your costume?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: This is it. I'm wearing it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Who the heck are you supposed to be?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Noted linguist and political commentator Noam Chomsky!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You're like the physical opposite of fun. Fun touches you and explodes out of existence.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, you were looking for brains...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Aw, man. My stomach. Ugh.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Too much candy last night, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah. I'll say. Hey, pass me that bag of bite-size Butterfingers.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Are you sure that's such a good idea right now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hair of the werewolf, man.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, you'll never believe it! I found twenty bucks in my basement!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, that's lucky. What are you gonna... Mortimer! Your hands are covered in blood!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, deer's blood.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? What were you doing with deer's blood?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I told you, I found twenty bucks in my basement! Guess my roommate went hunting last weekend.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And what I don't understand about the Thanksgiving story is the pillgrams.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How it could possibly be legal to send medication via telegram? You know?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That is so stupid, and you are stupid because everyone besides me is stupid.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Is that really what I sound like?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Dude, I gotta say, at first I thought this was a lame idea... but I'm loving this role reversal game.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do you have any Thanksgiving plans, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do I ever! I'm thinkin' frozen pizza and a two-liter.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Aw, Mortimer! Do you want to come over to my place? We could always make room for one more.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's the idea, dude! What time should I - wait, who's this "we"? Do I need to bring another pizza?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Boy, I could really use a garment steamer myself.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? You never seemed like the type to worry too much about your clothes being wrinkled.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, what is that supposed to mean?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It means I've never even seen you wear pants.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why do side sleepers get their own special pillows? Nobody ever made a pillow for the way I sleep.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Don't you sleep hanging upside-down by your toes? How would anyone even design a pillow for that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It would be a nice gesture. That's all I'm saying.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Here, I made you a modernist sculpture. It's for your bedroom.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um... isn't this a video baby monitor?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: To the untrained eye, maybe. To an artist, it's a statement about the authorial gaze.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Remember, for maximum art, put it on your nightstand, pointed right at you.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No. You made me promise not to give it to you, no matter how much you begged. So the answer's still no.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Come on, I just need it for a minute. A second. Half a second! Please!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No. I'm keeping your mouse for the duration of this Woot-Off so you can't buy anything. That's what you wanted me to do.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I just need it long enough to buy this one thing. Then I'm kicking it. For good. For real this time. I swear.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You do know that even if you bought it, that mouse wouldn't get here in time for you to use it for this Woot-Off, right?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Would it kill you to just let an addict have his delusions?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, Monte, do you know anything about computers? I think I have a virus or something.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sort of. What's the problem?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: All these stupid pop-ups, that's what! "Where are the files I need?" "I need that project, stat!" "Are you there?"

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, I think that's just other co-workers trying to instant message you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, how do I turn it off? I'm never going to win this Flash game with all this distraction.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: All I'm saying is that if someone's going to restrict access to them, they should say so in the "Free Donuts" e-mail.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Dude, those were supposed to be for everyone.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Read the e-mail! There's no "Don't stand next to the box and eat them until you're sick" rule!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I just don't think the plot is very believable, that's all.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: They've had their property stolen from them. Aren't birds entitled to justice just like everyone else?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But to fling yourself against walls and rocks? Being angry is one thing. Being stupid is totally different.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you know that there are rules to playing darts?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, of course. It's more than just aimlessly throwing darts at a board, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, there's a board?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What do you think of my new cufflinks? I think they make me look distinguished.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Those are cufflinks? I thought cufflinks were a weapon, like brass knuckles.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: This is why you look things up rather than just assuming you know the definition.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Boy, Monte, you're lucky I don't have a pair of cufflinks in my hand right now...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Someone stole my cell phone!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh no!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think I'll call it on my desk phone and give that thief a piece of my mind!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh don't do that. It's immature.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, do I hear something ringing in your back pocket?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No... Yes... It's my butt. I HAVE A MEDICAL CONDITION!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I love frozen meals! So quick and easy!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm not a fan. All the sodium and preservatives. Not to mention, it's kind of depressing waiting next to the microwave.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You microwave them? Monte, you're a genius!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why are you so still mad about yesterday? A day off is supposed to be relaxing.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: A "day off"? You locked us in a closet! We couldn't do our jobs!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It wasn't just "a closet". It was the Team-Building Retreat Closet.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Gosh, have you been in the kitchen? It stinks in there.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know! And of course, this has to happen the day I bring Thanksgiving leftovers to share with the office!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, how do you have leftovers a week before Thanksgiving?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: There was a Thanksgiving last year, too. Duh.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I just got a 12-string guitar! Get ready for 12 times the shredding!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Don't you mean twice the shredding? Since normal guitars have six strings?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Only you would bring math into a conversation about rock and roll.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've decided I'm going to start biking to work.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's great, Mortimer! I'm glad to see you want to lead a healthy lifestyle.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, totally. Now, if you wouldn't mind signing this petition to move our office to this empty storefront next to my house...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You're still mad, aren't you?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Of course I am! I only ever get that pumpkin-spiced bread once a year, and you ate it all!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: If it makes you feel better, pumpkin-spiced stuff is kinda played out.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: My mother made that bread specially for me!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I'm not saying it wasn't tasty. I'm just saying it's played out.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, you've got to calm down, man.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Is it gone?! Oh jeez, it was so huge and horrible! And such horrible beady eyes!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You've never actually seen a real turkey, have you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Turkey? That was no turkey! It wasn't golden brown or full of stuffing or anything! THAT was a beast from hell itself!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Right. Maybe we'll have ham this year.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And that thing hanging from its beak! What was that?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just look at it all, Monte. Isn't it beautiful?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, the company really outdid itself on the Thankgiving lunch this... Mortimer, are you crying?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's just so... so... so free!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Come on. Say it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? You're really going to make me do this?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: SAY IT.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh... I'm thankful for a friend like you, Mortimer. I guess. If I have to be.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: SQUEE! And I am welcome for you!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That doesn't... You know what? Forget it. Happy Thanksgiving, buddy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I was so excited this morning when I saw I was the first one in line. Then it just never moved. For hours. I finally gave up.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, what store was that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Store? I was trying to renew my license plates.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Today I finally saw the sign that tells me, unmistakably, that the Christmas season has arrived.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: The lights on all the houses? The sales in all the stores? That little snow flurry we had this morning?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, last year's egg nog is done fermenting. You ready to get festive?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, we get it! Robots can clean floors! Now when are they gonna come out with some robots to do my other chores?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Like brush my teeth! I'M WAITING!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Of all the dumb ideas, "Cyber Monday" is the Top 1 Worst of All Time.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because it encourages people to sit at home and further cocoon themselves at their computers rather than get out in the world!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's kind of a big deal for us. You know, on account of our selling stuff. On the Internet.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, well, I was never wild about THAT idea either.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, guess what? I'm gonna keep this "stupid names for days" thing going all week!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? Are you still upset about Cyber Monday? It's over. Let it go, man.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No way, pal. SAY HELLO TO MY LITTLE FRIEND!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You have five seconds to tell me what's in that jar and get it the hell off my desk.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: IT'S TOEJAM TUESDAY, AND YOU'RE LIVIN' IT!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do you ever feel like there's someone up there? Y'know, watching over us?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't really like to discuss religion with co-workers, Mortimer. It's a guaranteed recipe for hurt feelings.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Religion? I'm talking about the drop ceiling. I swear, sometimes I see one of the tiles move.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No way! You've gone too far this time! This is INSANITY!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I take it you're upset about the party?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: "Generic Non-Denominational Winter Celebration?" What the heck does that even MEAN?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, not everyone in the office celebrates the same holidays, and I wanted all of us to be able to-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Come together in unity to acknowledge the fact that you're lame?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey! You can't just knock all my stuff off my desk like that!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sure I can. I'm the Thane.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm the Thane. The Thane of Woot.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm not going to explain to you again that the rules of Skyrim don't apply to real life.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow. That stupid Spider-Man Broadway musical actually broke a house record for box office revenue. Who'd have thought?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Mmm hmm.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I figured you'd be more interested than that. You're big into comics.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm kinda busy here.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Doing what?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Finishing this She-Hulk: Everyone's Green with Envy script.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Holy crap! I can't believe he responded!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Liam Gallagher! He emailed to say he's totally into writing the score for my She-Hulk Broadway musical! This is fantastic!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What?! How did you even get Liam Gallagher's email address?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I just spammed a bunch of email addresses until one responded. See, look!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, do you think Liam Gallagher's email address is really I_Hate_Noel@yahoo.co.uk?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Terrible news, Monte. My She-Hulk musical has officially gone from "troubled" to "canceled."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? But I had such high hopes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Me, too. But it turns out that the "Liam Gallagher" who replied to me was a phony.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No! What tipped you off?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: When I opened the "demo tracks" he sent me, it just made a fart noise. And then my email started sending Viagra spam.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Y'know, that's about how I thought this would end.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I can't believe Coke would confuse everybody with those white cans! How dare they introduce a temporary design that vaguely resembles the Diet Coke can?!?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: If you take a second to look at the label, it's pretty easy to tell which is which.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's not enough that I buy the stuff, and drink the stuff - now I have to read about it, too?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Only a couple of days left for the Shirt.Woot Holiday Gift Guide. Picked up any hoodies, long-sleeve tees, or other stuff yet, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Dude, why are you talking like that?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Because I'm so excited about seeing classic Shirt.Woot designs on new items like tote bags, aprons, and greeting cards! How about you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Stop freaking me out. You sound like email sales copy or something.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ...You're just not going to play along with this, are you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: As long as those stuck-up Shirt.Woot people refuse to print my "Darth Santa" T-shirt design, no.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Friday! One more day, and then it's a weekend full of nothing but fun and relaxation!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, so you've got all of your holiday shopping done?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ...You just had to bum me out, didn't you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Gaaah! What was I thinking, going to the mall today?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Pretty bad, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It was a zoo! A circus! A total madhouse!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So, kind of like a zoo full of insane animals jumping through hoops and doing tricks?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, I guess... actually, when you put it that way, it sounds pretty cool. Hey, you want to go to the mall?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What about this: Instead of a Woot-Off, we do a Woot-Out? We take two products each day for a week and make them fight to the death!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: The products would fight? I don't even know how that would work.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It should work just fine if we sell mostly sharks and knights that week.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look! They made a gingerbread-flavored soda!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's ginger ale, Mortimer. Seriously, have you never heard of ginger ale?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Maybe the problem isn't with me, Monte. Maybe the problem here is with what you expect me to know.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know what I love doing?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Going to a sporting goods store, cracking open some new tennis balls, and just breathing in that smell.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't know where to start with how weird and kind of gross that is.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So I take it you don't want to be the first test subject of Love-All for Men, my new tennis-ball-inspired cologne?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte! Come quick! I'm about to win a free iPad!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's just a scam, Mortimer. Somebody's trying to trick you into giving away your credit card information. You didn't tell them anything like that, did you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: C'mon! Do you really think I'd be dumb enough to tell some guy on the Internet that my Visa number is 4716 5288 8899 3083?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Y'know what Batman needs?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: A new, timeless villain. Like The Joker, but newer. And with royalties that go to me.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And you think you can create this new villain?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Totally! So far I have The Smoker, The Poker, and The Yolker. The last one only commits egg-related crimes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, it's happening. We've waited our whole lives for this!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Tom Cruise is working on a sequel to Top Gun! And you KNOW he's going to need to cast some devilishly handsome monkeys as the hotshot young pilots, so he can pass the baton both on-screen and in real life!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That doesn't seem very likely.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do you have to crap on everything?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Look, in my experience monkeys and the aerospace industry have sort of an uneasy relationship.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How goes your quest to get an audition for the Top Gun sequel that might not even happen?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Great! I got a lead! Turns out Tom Cruise is super into whatever church he goes to. I figure all I have to do is join and I'm in!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mmmkay, well make sure you don't miss any work.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Off I go! See ya, buddy!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh huh, see y- Wait. What did you just say you're going to do?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Eh, whatever. I'm not convinced.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You don't think I've made a good case that you shouldn't commit your life and fortune to the Church of Scientology under false pretenses to try and land an audition for Top Gun 2, a movie that may or may not get made?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: See, you just talk and talk, and you lose me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You are unbelievable, you know that? The lengths to which you'll go to try and keep me from realizing my dream.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, one of these days you'll thank me for not taking you to join the Church of Scientology in some half-baked scheme to meet Tom Cruise.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sure. Maybe I'll thank you when I'm NOT the hottest new thing in Hollywood after starring in Top Gun 2. OH WAIT I'M ALREADY THERE AND I HATE YOU.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, do you have room at your place for a broken photocopier? I need to get it out of my garage. It's yours for free if you want it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, I don't have room. And if I did, I wouldn't want to fill it with a broken photocopier.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, just thought I'd ask. Well, I gotta go. I need to run to the stationery store on my lunch break.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What do you need from the stationery store?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Like, 12 rolls of wrapping paper. You're not gonna BELIEVE what I'm giving you for Christmas!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, please, if you're planning to give me a broken photocopier -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: NO HINTS! What do you want to do, ruin the surprise?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: In this case, yes, that's exactly what I want to do.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm telling you, Mortimer, do NOT give me a broken photocopier. If you want to give me a gift, make a donation to a charity or something.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, sure. I'll call one right now and see what they want. Hello? Is this the Helping People Organization That Helps People? I'd like to make a donation. Oh, that's what you want? I sure will! You're welcome!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Let me guess -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: They told me to give you a broken photocopier.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, that "charity" you "called" doesn't exist.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, I'm just trying to help the HPOTHP, not interrogate them.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I won't give you any more hints about your present. But I hope you've got A LOT of room under the tree.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Please, Mortimer. I'm asking you one more time: In the name of monkey decency, do not bring that broken photocopier to my house.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, OK, fine, Scrooge. I won't. But I can't speak for Santa Claus...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I hope you've got a REALLY big chimney.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Christmas Eve! Just one more day until I can give you THE BIG PRESENT!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Here's yours. A day early.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oooh, an envelope! Is there a gift card in here? You know how much I love the Macaroni Grill - wait, what's this?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: An injunction. You are hereby ordered to keep that broken photocopier 100 yards away from my property at all times or go to jail.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wow. That's... not very festive.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Neither is the downtown lockup.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer! Why are you wheeling that thing up my sidewalk?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's your present! No stupid injunction is going to keep me away!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I told you, if you tried to dump that broken photocopier on my property, I'd call the police! I don't care how beautifully it's wrapped!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Will you just shut up and open it?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Fine, but only so the cops can see- hey, wait, it's a box. What's in here? Wha- two Segways! Inscribed with our names! So we can ride together! Mortimer, this is the most incredible gift ever!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And you almost ruined it. Merry Christmas, you crotchety old stick-in-the-mud. Now let's ride!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And now the post-holiday depression sets in. Back to the grind. Back to reality. Sigh.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte -

lines.speaker: monte
