lines.text: Those glittering dreams of joy, hope, and brotherhood lie discarded like so much crumpled wrapping paper. The weather hasn't changed, but it feels so much colder now.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte! I'm trying to ask you if you want to go to lunch and help me spend this gift card from Pizza Trough.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, of course I do! Thanks! But why didn't you say something before I wasted all that perfectly good moaning?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm sure there's plenty more where that came from. Now let's eat.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte! Get over here right now and help me analyze how well I fulfilled my 2011 New Year's resolutions! Now, I say!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, you could have just asked me nicely, you know.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But that would contradict #4: "Be more assertive."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How do you think I did on this 2011 New Year's resolution: "Listen more attentively to others"?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, in all honesty, Mortimer, there are times when I don't feel as if you're really paying attention to anything I'm trying to-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, sounds good! Yay, me! Now let's move on...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Here's a New Year's resolution I really struggled with last year: "Eat healthier."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, that's a tough one for everyone. Maybe you could try keeping more baby carrots and celery at your desk to snack on.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, you're right. Fresh veggies can be just as delicious as junk food under the right circumstances.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, what "circumstances" did you have in mind, exactly?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: When they're dipped in Cool Whip.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I appreciate that you asked me to help you check off your 2011 New Year's resolutions. But I can't tell you anything about #7.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, you mean "Make out with a cast member of Glee?" Yeah, check it off.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? Are you serious?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, I modified that one down to "Make out with someone who has seen Glee."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, still, congratulations. All year long, I had no idea there was a special someone-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And then I modified that down to "Ask someone who looks like she has probably seen Glee which way the mall escalators are." I've found that the secret to success is flexibility.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So how did I do on my 2011 New Year's resolutions?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, looking over the list, I have to say the results were mixed. But there is one more on the list here. It's kind of hard to read... maybe "gravy light"?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, right! Luckily there's still time for that one!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't even understand what it's supposed to mean. HEY! STOP IT! YOU'RE GETTING GRAVY ALL OVER- oh. Now I get it. "Gravy fight."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Gravy New Year, everybody! Gentlemen, start your boats!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So what are you looking forward to in 2012, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, definitely the election. It's all I can think about.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? I thought you didn't care about politics.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Politics? I'm talking about the election for Mr. Monkey Hunk of the Year. I think I have a real shot to ride on that float in the Monkey Pride Parade this year.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ah, I should've known.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Is there some other election going on?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Today's battery charger will really help with one of my New Year's resolutions.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, you're resolving to use less energy this year?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I'm resolving to cultivate a "green" image to help me pick up chicks.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, the planet appreciates it either way.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, the holidays are just barely over and I'm already swamped with work.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, I kind of went on a downloading binge over the holidays and now I have to clean up all the MP3 tags. It's taking me all day!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, man! Today's shirt! Check it out!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, yeah. That's cute.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Cute? It's hilarious!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I mean, yeah, Abe Lincoln wearing the Cat in the Hat's hat. They both wore tall hats. Kinda funny.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: They both... whoa, I didn't even GET that part! It's even funnier now!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ungh. Ungh. UNGH. UNNGGGGHHH. UNNNGGGGGHHH-

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer! What are you doing, and why is it so loud?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just some stomach clenches. Part of my new desk-side workout routine.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, it's pretty gross.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Then you definitely won't want to be here for the gastrointestinal flexes.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Whoa, nice jacket. That looks expensive.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It probably is!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You don't know?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nope! Came in the mail yesterday, courtesy of our newest fan on the site!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You gave your address to a Woot fan? I don't know if that's a great idea.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whatever. You're just jealous because MonkeyStalker618 is probably a cute girl.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You look terrible, Mortimer. What happened?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I couldn't sleep at all. MonkeyStalker618 showed up at my house.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, no!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Turns out it IS a cute girl, but she's also nuts. She stood outside my house screaming "I WANT ONE!" over and over.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Did you call the police?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You can call them for help? I've only ever had them called ON me.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How goes it with the stalker?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: FAN. She's a fan. And I think we've reached a compromise and she's leaving me alone.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, really? People like that usually don't listen to logic.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, the way I see it, my peace and quiet is well worth sending her a vial of blood every month.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: The Woot writers are going to the Consumer Electronics Show this week! I can't wait to follow along on the Woot blog!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Must be nice to go to Las Vegas on the company dime. Lucky for them they've got the rest of us to stay back here and get the REAL work done.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And, uh, what exactly are you working on right now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: An in-depth, detailed forecast of trends in celebrity SUV decor. Looks like TV screens in the steering wheel will stay popular again this year.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Boy, how about that game last night, huh?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'll say! An all-time classic! Congrats to the new national champs! We were in your corner all the way!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Totally! I'm so excited after that win, it's hard to concentrate on writing next week's Monkeychats!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Those containers look nice, but I think the dangers of BPA are way overblown.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? Bisphenol A has been linked to possible neural and cognitive defects in young children, you know.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait - so all along, people haven't been talking about Belgian Pale Ale?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Weren't you cold, coming to work today in no shoes? It's freezing outside.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Eh, a little, at first. But after a while I couldn't even feel it. My toes even have this kind of warm, burning sensation now.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, I think that could be frostbite.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I guess that explains why my feet are that deep blue color. I thought it was just a tattoo I forgot about.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So, what, you don't think the Nightmare On Elm Street series is better?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No one's disputing that, Mortimer, but...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So why won't you sign my petition?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I just feel there are better, more reality-based issues than officially declaring every Saturday the 14th "Freddy Day."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Fine. You'll get yours when you go to sleep tonight.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So, Mortimer, you want to talk about the elephant in the room?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Please, Monte. I think the self-hatred that causes you to lash out at me is best left to your therapist.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Thanks for the insight, but I was referring to the actual elephant outside your cubicle.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Huh? Oh, that. I'm trying to ignore it until it just goes away.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: "Winning an Oscar."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: "Finding the Plot."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: "Something Other Than Mediocrity."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: "Making Tom Cruise Likable."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: See? I told you making up subtitles for the next Mission: Impossible movie would be a blast.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm guessing I have you to thank for my new cubicle decorations?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yep! I wasn't really feeling like making a "Mondays suck" joke, but I found thousands of comic strips that could do it for me!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes, this Monday's definitely going to suck, since I'll have to spend it scraping comic strips off my desk. And chair. And keyboard. And monitor.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you see that cat who drinks coffee and eats lasagna? What kind of an owner is that? I'm thinking about calling Animal Welfare.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: WORST WOOT-OFF EVER!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What are you talking about? It just started like thirty seconds ago.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm taking a personal day tomorrow, so I have to get all my whining done tonight.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, it sure is quiet around here with Mortimer taking a personal day.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Maybe... a little too quiet.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No. No, it's just the right amount of quiet.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So, uh, did you come in to the office yesterday?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uh, did you?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ...Yes?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well then, me, too! That's why we saw each other at the office yesterday! Right? RIGHT?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Right. OK. But then, um, why are we having this conversation right now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: We aren't.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So all I'm saying is, based on both credit sequences there's a real case that Thundarr The Barbarian was the direct sequel to Space:1999.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But wasn't Thundarr set two thousand years in the future? So what goes between the two? Adventure Time?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ... Man. You just changed EVERYTHING.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, I just called the pizza place to see where my lunch was and they said someone cancelled it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, yeah. Yeah. I did that.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because this week we play... The Hunger Games.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You have cookie crumbs in your fur.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I ate those before The Hunger Games started.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: The thing is, I don't actually think The Hunger Games is about food.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, not the book, at least.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't like how you said that, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm just wanting to test a theory about how far people will go to get the first Thin Mints of the season. It's practically science!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Is that a bus full of Girl Scouts outside?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: For your own protection, it's best you don't ask questions from this point on.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Okay, tell me what you want on your sandwich. The Hunger Games are over. I sent the girls home.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Thank goodness. What changed your mind?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I did some research and apparently there's this movie called Battle Royale? Like, the exact same thing as my plan, only without the Thin Mints.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And you watched it and understood the point was that man's inhumanity to man can only be stopped when we all work together as one?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That, plus I don't want to be called a lame-o rip-off artist who's in it for the money. When I construct an elaborate scenario involving the ruthless manipulation of dozens of innocent lives, I want it to mean something.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Too bad not everyone shares your integrity.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: EXTREME ENCHILADACAM! EXTREME ENCHILADACAM!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Would you get that camcorder out of my face? I'm trying to eat here.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: EXTREME ENCHILADACAM! EXTREME ENCHILADACAM!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I hate to do this, but... there. See how you like having enchilada sauce smeared all over the lens.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The joke's on you! That just makes it more EXTREME!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, my new TouchPad is here! Awesome!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, but check my new tablet out. Never needs charging, holds hundreds of documents, super-intuitive touch-sensitive navigation, and I can draw or write directly on the surface with a simple, cheap stylus available at almost any store.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Isn't that just a pad of lined paper?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It doesn't have Wi-Fi, but the Internet is pretty stupid anyway.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm just saying, it would be hard to have to share your husband with the world like that. To always be there, waiting for him, putting your own life on hold while he's out changing the world. And then to never even get any recognition for it. My hat's off to her. I couldn't do something like that.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: For the last time, there was no Mrs. Coffee.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: See? That attitude is exactly what I'm talking about.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What is that smell? Are you... are you making nachos at 7 in the morning?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yep! Lots to do before the big game today! Hey, take those Oreos out of the deep fryer, wouldja?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, sure. But isn't the game next Sunday?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, not that game. The Pro Bowl! The elite showcase for the greatest players in the game today!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Except that half of the chosen players don't bother to show up.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's OK. Half of my friends won't bother to show up for the party.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Of course, the building staff had to pick right now to close the bathroom for maintenance. But I showed them, heh heh...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? Sorry, I'm kinda busy right now, trying to check this surveillance footage.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Surveillance footage?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah. With all those office supplies going missing lately, we installed some hidden cameras in the supply closet. Now I'm going to see what they've recorded so far.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh. Uh. Wow. That's cool. Hey, could you hand me that roll of paper towels? I need to go clean something up.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh. This is without a doubt the most UNBEARABLE part of my job. SIGH.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What is?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Filling out these dumb reports about my purchases with my company credit card.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, come on. It takes like 15 minutes a month. You'd want the same consideration if it were your money being spent.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Who said anything about money? I'm talking about a credit card.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: SIGH. GRUMBLE. WHINE.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Will you please knock it off? What's so hard about filling out a credit card report once a month?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: They want all these stupid receipts and stuff. It's STUPID.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Some might say it's stupid that a fully grown adult can't keep track of a few receipts.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, genius, the guy running the elephant rides at the zoo doesn't even GIVE receipts. Who's stupid now?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You used your company card to pay for elephant rides at the zoo?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It was a team-building exercise.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But you were alone.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And now I've built myself into such a great team, I don't need anybody else. It was money well spent.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Somehow I think you'll have trouble explaining that to the accounting office.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's not my fault they don't understand advanced managerial theory.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, they bought it. I don't have to pay back the money for those elephant rides I bought with the company card.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow. How'd you manage that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just went in there and made my case. Dropped a truth bomb on 'em. Told 'em what was what. People respond to a firm, forceful personality.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? Because I heard you have to dress up as an elephant for the CFO's daughter's birthday party and give all the kids rides.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm sorry, the details of that agreement are confidential.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So look, I couldn't get everything together in time. I gotta postpone my Super Bowl party.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? But Mortimer, the Super Bowl is today! How can you postpone it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Um, duh! DVR!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh! People keep talking about the Super Bowl! Didn't anyone read the email I sent about waiting for my party?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But Mortimer, it's all over the news, on the radio, everywhere. That's hard to ignore.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know what else is hard, Monte? MAKING A 47-LAYER CHEESE DIP!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, did you seriously make a 47-layer cheese dip for your belated Super Bowl party? Why 47 layers?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I wanted one layer for each person I invited on Facebook.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's surprisingly sweet of you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I like to make people feel special, by way of cheese.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You got any stamps, Monte?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sure. What do you need them for?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm mailing everyone their layer of the 47-layer cheese dip now that the Super Bowl party's cancelled.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, can you even do that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know what, Monte? If sending gelatinous cheese via post is wrong, I don't want to be right.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I... I don't feel so good. I feel weird, or something.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't know. I ate half of a leftover burrito out of the fridge, then left the wrapper on that guy Marty's desk. The guy whose burrito it was saw the wrapper and started yelling at Marty. Long story short, Marty wound up shoving the guy. He fell and cracked his head and had to go to the hospital. Marty got fired on the spot. He was crying as security marched him out the door. Actually crying. And I started to get this really... weird feeling. I don't like it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, I think that feeling is what the rest of us call "remorse."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Really? Is this how remor-URRRRRP! Oh, no, there it goes. It was just gas from the burrito. Man, you missed a great fight!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, everyone here is so down lately. Have you noticed?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Huh. Now that you mention it, I guess morale is a little low.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: These people need inspiration. They need motivation. They need... A SUPERHERO!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Quick, Monte! Find a spider we can nuke in the microwave. I have an idea!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Any luck with getting superpowers?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Negative. Apparently you can't just buy radioactive isotopes.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Bummer. I guess that's the end of that.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Pfft. I don't need powers! I can be like that archer guy in the Avengers. Or that one guy with the cowl...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Batman?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, thanks. I don't really care for baseball.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I need a superhero persona. Co-workers are a lazy, unproductive lot...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You would know.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ...so my disguise needs to strike terror into their hearts.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That reminds me. HR called about your self-evaluation. Something about "blatant lies."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's it! I shall become an Annual Review!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Dude, why is your cape in the trash?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm out of the superhero business, Monte. ANNUAL REVIEW-MAN NO MORE!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Aw, sorry, buddy. What happened?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Turns out telling people they "meet expectations" but aren't getting a raise isn't very inspiring.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: If it helps, you've totally exceeded my expectations. This is the most work you've done in weeks.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Thanks, dude. That means a lot.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Happy Valentine's Day, Mortimer! I got you a special Valentine's Day heart assortment!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Thanks! What a coincidence - that's what I got you!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, thanks... but what's this stuff dripping from this candy?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Candy? No, no - that's a pound of mixed beef hearts, pork hearts, and chicken hearts! It's a Valentine's Day tradition at my house!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh. Would you consider trading me some candy?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sorry! Like my mom used to say every Valentine's Day: "No give-backs! Called it!"

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My fellow Americans, I want to thank you for gathering here today for this historic occasion-

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Who are you talking to? It's just me. I work here. And what's so historic about it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: -as I announce my candidacy for President of the United States of America!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Terrific. Before you leave for the Oval Office, can I have my phone charger back? You borrowed it like a week ago and my phone is almost out-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm sorry, all interview questions must go through my press officer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: As I've walked the campaign trail these past several hours, I've heard the deep concerns of the American people. I've heard a nation crying out for solutions.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Actually, that was me crying out for my phone charger.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And as I stand before you today, I pledge to you: A Mortimer administration will ensure that every American, no matter his or her station in life, has equal access to his or her coworkers' phone chargers.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So things around here won't change much, then.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, the Mortimer campaign hasn't forgotten the favor you did us by lending us that phone charger.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Good, now if I could just have it back -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You stood by us when we were nowhere in the polls, when everybody was counting this campaign out. And this campaign takes care of its friends.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, you're still nowhere in the polls, but anyway, yeah, if you could just give that back-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Actually, I've got something better in mind. How would you like to be the United States Ambassador to Italy?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Can I get my phone charged there?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's right: For your support of the Mortimer 2012 campaign, I'm prepared to nominate you for Ambassador to Italy in my administration.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Look, when I loaned you that phone charger, you said you'd give it back in an hour. It's been over a week now. I just need to charge my phone.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh. I see. I get it. I see what's going on here. Another campaign made you a better offer.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, for the last time, I need that charger.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's sad. You can't trust anybody in politics these days.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I was just thinking something very similar.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, I'll level with you. I built my political reputation as a straight-shooter.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So when do I get my phone charger back?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm not the kind of candidate who's afraid to tell hard truths.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So when do I get my phone charger back?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And the harsh reality, my fellow Americans, is this: I broke your phone charger.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Finally. Next time you break something of mine, can we skip the fake presidential campaign?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So you launched a fake presidential campaign just to hide the fact that you broke my phone charger?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, when it comes to distractions, go big.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But a presidential campaign? It just seems like such a deranged, pointless, self-centered- ooooh, I see.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It does make a certain sense, doesn't it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey! WOOT-OFF! WOOT-OFF!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, before we get started with that: How did you break my phone charger, anyway?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, that. I was trying to infuse it into some vodka.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ...OK, that might be the stupidest thing I've ever heard.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Really? You're telling me you can't imagine a market for a vodka that can also charge your phone?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ...OK, that might be the most brilliant thing I've ever heard.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Check it. I got one of those sweet wing suits to go sky diving.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Who just GIVES you these things? Shouldn't you have to prove you can sky dive NORMALLY before you get wings?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: All it takes is a credit card.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I thought your cards were all maxed out.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, but this one has generous "overdraft protection." Whatever that means.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, absolutely not. No way.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Come on! It'll be fun!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, it won't be. Jumping out of an airplane and careening through the sky on a thin filament of synthetic fabric is not going to be fun for me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well SOMEONE has to operate the camera for my sweet extreme sports highlight reel.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What if you crash and horribly injure yourself?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: We should only BE so lucky! You get way more money for a blooper reel.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How'd the extreme skydiving go?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't want to talk about it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yikes! What happened to your leg?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: WHAT KIND OF MORON STILL HAS AN AERIAL ANTENNA FOR HIS TELEVISION IN 2012?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I decided I need a less extreme hobby. I'm taking up the bass guitar.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, that seems like a quiet and non-irritating hobby that won't grate on my nerves at all.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, I was being sarcastic.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh. I was gonna say, like 80% of the reason I got this thing was to annoy you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Boodly doodly dweebldy dwa, pa pow pa boodly doo-

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: As much as I realize I'm going to regret asking this, is there any way you could actually PLAY your bass guitar rather than sing how you think it would sound?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Actually, so far I'm finding the bass is much easier to play if you don't touch the strings.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know what always bugged me about Daredevil? He had echolocation powers or whatever but never made the noise for the echo!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What, like the way bats squeak? Kind of ruins the intimidation factor, doesn't it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You're telling me if you look up to see a guy in red spandex chittering like a squirrel and leaping around the rooftops you're not going to be intimidated?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Point taken.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How come you never talk about superheroes YOU like?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Come on. Who's your fave? Superman?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, to be honest, I always thought Aquaman had the coolest powers.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You- I- There's- It's- Aquaman? It's like you took a class on how to maximize your dorkiness.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, aren't you the one who loves those Triple-Meat Fryplugs from Burger Hole?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Me? Are you kidding? I don't even know what they are.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know: those delicious wads of processed pork, beef, and chicken, deep-fried in a crispy batter, only from Burger Hole. You sure you don't eat those?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Eeeew. I'm positive.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, that's a relief. They just announced a nationwide Triple-Meat Fryplug recall. Looks like they found some tainted with e. coli.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, uh... really? Were the tainted ones found around here, by any chance?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, Burger Hole yanked those Triple-Meat Fryplugs out of all their restaurants around here. You can't be too safe with e. coli. But it's not your problem. You'd never eat something so artificial and unhealthy anyway.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, no, of course I wouldn't...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Say, you're looking a little sick there, buddy. You feelin' alright?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, OK, fine! I DO eat Triple-Meat Fryplugs! I hate myself for it, but so help me God, I LOVE Triple-Meat Fryplugs! Now get me to a hospital, I can feel the e. coli squirming in my gut right now!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, there's no recall. I just made that up.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You... you... I was going to say you sicken me, but I think I sicken myself.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I can't believe you invented that story about an e. coli recall just to get me to admit I love Triple-Meat Fryplugs. And I can't believe it worked.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just keeping you honest, friend.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But isn't a certain hypocrisy a necessary part of civilized life? Shouldn't we all aspire to the highest ideals even when we ourselves fall short of those ideals? Isn't there some value to upholding standards in public, whether or not we can meet those standards in private?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Eh, I'm too hungry to think about it. You wanna go to Burger Hole?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: The Triple-Meat Fryplugs are on me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, what's the difference between "sensual" and "sensuous"? Which one means "sexy"?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, well, originally it would have been "sensuous", but modern usage has generally evolved toward-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, OK, forget it. I'll go with "powerfully erotic".

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What are you doing, anyway?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just editing my Wikipedia page.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, don't be naive. You think they'd build something that complex just to use it as a toy?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Believe what you want. But I'm telling you, that robotic lion cub is not collecting your DNA samples while you sleep.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Huh. OK. I see. So Interpol got to you, too, huh?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, look at you, all dressed up to play with your model NASCAR set. I didn't know you were such a race fan.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I'm not. I just like wearing corporate logos on my clothes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Thanks for helping me out, Monte. I didn't know you were into RPGs!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm not. You said we had to playtest the new "Wooters & Crappers" RPG module before we can sell it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Right. So first you have to pick a class.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I feel like we're way past the point of having any class whatsoever.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, Monte, if you're not going to take this Wooters & Crappers role-playing game seriously...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, fine. I'll be an elf.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Right, good. But our campaign takes place in Woot world, so we have to rename elves to something Woot-related.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, elves are delicate and dainty, they have squeaky voices, they're maybe a little effeminate-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Perfect! We'll call them "Montes."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Your adventurer stops dead in his tracks as you encounter a Forum Troll!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Forum Troll?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Before you can react, the Forum Troll uses "homophobic slurs with thinly veiled threat of violence"!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It does what now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte takes three points of damage and a -1 modifier to all self-esteem rolls!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, I summoned a moderator and dispatched the forum troll. Is there any treasure?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You find a T-shirt.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Nice. I'll put it on. Does it help my armor?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ha HA! It's cursed! It shrinks in the laundry, exposing your belly and making you look ridiculous! -1 to Charisma!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So for you, this game is essentially just an exercise in sadism.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Silence, slave! The Dungeon Master speaks!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte, you want to go grab a Four Loko?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Can't tonight. Gotta get some rest for Spring Harvest 2012 tomorrow.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Huh, cool. I didn't know you farmed.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, I don't. Spring Harvest is the largest annual convention of people involved in the spring industry and their fans.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Remember when I said "cool" a second ago? I take that back.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you really spend all day talking about springs at a spring convention?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes! Spring Harvest 2012, day 1 - or SH2k12d1, to the fans - was fantastic.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, there are enough spring-related activities to fill multiple days?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Three, in fact. The highlight is always the networking mixer on day 3.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, is that where you can SPRING yourself into a new career?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Not with a pun like that, no. We're enthusiasts, not idiots.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Missed you at work today. How was the second day of Spring Harvest 2012?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: As fascinating as you would expect from the world's largest gathering of fans and professionals in the spring industry.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't doubt it for a second.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You really missed this amazing panel called "Click Everywhere: The Retractable Pen and Its Impact on the American Breast Pocket." Did you know that in 1937-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just want to let you know that I've already stopped listening, in case you wanted to save the energy.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, I'm bummed that Spring Harvest 2012 is over. But at the networking event I got offered a job at a great new spring-manufacturing start up.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, you mean you're leaving?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No. Unfortunately it would require a significant pay cut, so I turned it down.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wow, Monte! I'm flattered it was our friendship that kept you here!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But that's not what I said...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sometimes I replace what I hear with compliments. It's the only way I can get through life.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What are you eating there, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, just some of this KRAVE gourmet jerky. Want a piece?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, thanks. I don't know how well it'll go with the yogurt I brought in for breakfast.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Actually, it's surprisingly delicious.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How do you know- Hey! What are these, these chunks? I don't believe it! You mixed jerky in with my yogurt!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Gourmet jerky, that is. Nothing but the best for my pal.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, I see you're using whiskey stones to keep your drink cool, too.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What? Oh, no. That's a sample I have to take to my urologist. I passed those in the bathroom earlier.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I see. Say, do you have a Sharpie?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Here you go. Why?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It suddenly seems very important that I write my name on this glass.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uh-oh! You dropped some potato-chip crumbs! I'm calling in a drone strike!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do you HAVE to say that every time you turn on that robot vacuum?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, I'm not the one who created the problem. I'm just cleaning it up. Drone strike, commence!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I guess this is what I get for eating at my desk.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, my latest home-brewed beer is ready to enjoy, just in time for St. Patty's Day. Have one on the house!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ooh, thanks! Can't wait to taste thi- ECCHHH! What is that flavor? Is that - is it -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You guessed it, pal! This batch of Mortimerbrau is flavored with real Brussels sprouts!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ugh! That's disgusting!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, you're the one who's always going on and on about Belgian beer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Boy, this March Madness is getting really messy!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, my bracket is just destroyed. But that's basketball.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Basketball? I'm talking about my allergy-induced sinus dementia. Could you pass me a lizard? I need to purple my nosebone.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, come on... the Wi-Fi on my laptop just does NOT work all of a sudden.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, I found this website that says Wi-Fi signals cause brain damage. So I opened up the case of your laptop and broke the Wi-Fi thingie.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? That's insane! Did you break the Wi-Fi adapter on your laptop, too?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No way! I don't believe every wild claim I read on the Internet. It's just an experiment. If I get brain damage before you do, we'll know there's something to the idea.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, Doctor, I think the results are in.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Woot-Off today! Are you as excited about it as I am?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Bleh. Every one just seems to lower the bar even further than the last one.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, I don't think so. But if you find them so disappointing, why don't you just stop following them?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, right! That's just what they want me to do! The minute I stop paying attention, they'll post something I really want!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's ridiculous. "They" have no way of knowing whether you're watching or not. Anyway, don't you think Woot would want to maximize the number of people seeing their best deals so they could - Hey, are you listening to me?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What? Oh, sorry, I was buying this bulletproof iPad cover. I just managed to snag one before it sold out! This is the best Woot-Off ever!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm sick of these high gas prices! I need somebody to be mad at!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, it's not a simple issue, and there's no one person to blame. Gasoline prices are largely determined by the global oil market, a complex web of independent actors, acting according to his or her own interests. Ultimately, rising gas prices reflect the increasing worldwide demand for oil.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's not what the guys at my Hummer Racing Club say!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: This seriously might be the happiest day of my life.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? Why?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The government must have read my letters. They're finally shooting Ashton Kutcher into space!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Actually, he's going as a tourist. He bought a ticket.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, so he's coming BACK? ALIVE?! This seriously might be the worst day of my life.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Dare I ask what you're working on?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, just a billion-selling young-adult fiction epic. All the money's in your Twilights and your Hunger Gameses and your Henry Porters. I'm "massaging" those books into one I can sell.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's Harry Potter.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oooh, nice! I'm free to use "Henry Porter" myself, then! Movie deal, here I come!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Here we go, buddy! I need your proofreading skills for draft one of Henry Porter and the Hungry Twilight of Games.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What makes you think I'd ever proofread that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The same reason you do everything you do with me: morbid curiosity.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What font is this, Wingdings?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, I finished proofreading your book-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whoa, seriously? I knew you could read fast, but you finished the whole novel?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, it's 20 pages of profanity, doodles of naked ladies, and slow-cooker recipes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Right, but did you LIKE it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've had this pulse massager going for hours and I can still feel my pulse. What a ripoff!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Okay, first, that's not what it's for. Second, why don't you want to feel your pulse?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Duh, Monte! So I can pretend I'm dead in order to collect my life insurance and never work again.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm not sure you understand life insurance...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, I do! It's like the insurance company is betting that I won't die. And I intend to win that bet!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Boy, this kids' music thing is really taking off. I wonder how I could get a piece of that action.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Write fun, engaging songs that both kids and their parents can enjoy?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Actually, I was thinking "cross-dressing baby." Kids, get ready for Lady Goo-Goo!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, you wanna go cruising after work? I got my car lowered!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Whoa, isn't that expensive?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Only if you call 20 bucks expensive.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: 20 bucks? Are you sure this wasn't something shady?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No way. I watched the guy take all the tires off himself. I'm low-ridin' now!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How about this idea: peer-to-peer location-specific analog voice chat?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, isn't that just what the rest of us call "talking"?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Don't be so Web 1.0, Gramps. By the way, you owe me 3 bucks for this peer-to-peer location-specific analog voice chat session.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Come on out from under your desk, Mortimer. You're being ridiculous.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ridiculous? You can see the evidence for yourself! No one liked it! No one liked it at all!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: People are busy this time of day. I'm sure people will like it when they get home.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I put my heart and soul into writing that status update and what did I get? Nothing. NOTHING!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh. Here, look, I just clicked the "like" button. Are you happy now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: YOU HAVE TO LIKE IT BECAUSE YOU'RE MY FRIEND.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Lemme get this straight, it's a website where people "pin" pictures of the things they like?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: From what I gather, yes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So if I wanted to pin up a bunch of pictures of, say, you, I could do that.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, sure, I guess.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Seems like a lot of fuss to me. Aren't the hundreds of pictures taped to the walls of my empty, candle-lit apartment good enough any more?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, who was these lady I saw with at you [insert var="LOCATION.LADY"]?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No [MONKEYNAME], not with lady - its was ERROR: parameter 'PUNCHLINE' not found;&131#sp;

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Boy, I'll be glad when this Woot writers' strike ends.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: [FATAL ERROR: output not permitted]

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Question.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Straightforward but exasperated acknowledgement.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Exposition concerning my skewed views of the office refrigerator.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Vain attempt at correcting your views through logic.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hilarious misunderstanding that only serves to reinforce my misconceptions.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh of disappointment at what my daily conversations have become.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: WENDY'S?! Are you KIDDING me?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What are you all worked up about?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wendy's has supplanted Burger King as the #2 burger chain in America!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And you're upset because you prefer Burger King?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm upset because nobody told me about the nationwide burger cook-off! MY VOICE WILL NOT BE SILENCED!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, wow. This is fascinating!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What? What is it?! I WANNA KNOW!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: The National Archives has released the 1940 census online! So neat!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh, I keep forgetting you run on some kind of "reverse fun system."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: C'mon, you don't think it's fun to learn about history?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: IF THE MISSOURI STATE BOARD OF EDUCATION CAN'T MAKE ME LIKE HISTORY, MONTE, THEN THERE'S NO WAY YOU CAN!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, what a rip. This garment steamer is supposed to steam wrinkles out, but look! This outfit looks exactly the same as before!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do they really have to specify that it doesn't work with suits of armor?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yes! If I'd known that before, I would've sprung for the un-dented armor!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: HOW DARE YOU SAY THAT TO ME?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm - I'm sorry, I didn't mean -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: HOW CAN YOU JUST SIT THERE AND LOOK ME IN THE FACE AND SAY THAT TO ME?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I really didn't mean to upset you -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: DO YOU HAVE ANY IDEA HOW PAINFUL THAT IS FOR ME? HOW MUCH IT HURTS TO HEAR?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm sorry. I just meant that it was cool that you knew somebody who was in Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom. Obviously, it was very uncool what they did with your uncle's brains.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, can you help with something I'm writing? How well do you know Emily Dickinson?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ooh, I took a whole semester course on her work sophomore year! What did you want to know?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, so if she was a werewolf, what would she -

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't know why I ever get my hopes up with you.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ummm... dare I ask why there's an airhorn on your desk?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I use that to drown out anything I don't want to hear. Was there anything else you wanted to say?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um... no, I guess not.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, good.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, come on! Say something uptight and scoldy! I'm dying to use this thing!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, what's this? A gift? For me? From my "secret admirer"? What is it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I guess you'll have to open it and find out!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, boy! I can't wait to see- Oh. Huh. A deflated volleyball. That's, uh, something.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What a coincidence! I just so happen to have brought my air compressor into the office today! You're in luck! For you, rentals start at just $9.50 a minute with a 20-minute minimum!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, I think I'll pass.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And hurt the feelings of whoever so thoughtfully picked out that volleyball just for you? Wow. You think you know somebody...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, have you signed up for SocialVoid yet? It's the social networking site I'm starting for people who hate social networking.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Then no, I won't be joining it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: HaHA! Got you! The only way to join SocialVoid is NOT to join!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I guess wasting your own time wasn't enough. You had to waste a few seconds of mine, too.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wasting my time? SocialVoid is up to 6,842,784,809 members!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer? You've been sitting completely still all morning. What's wrong?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Friday the 13th, Monte. As long as I don't move or do anything at all, nothing unlucky can happen to me.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, it would be really terrible to be, oh, I don't know, forced to sit still in one place for 24 hours.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Exactly! That's the kind of stuff I'm trying to avoid!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do you think dogs who live underwater get mad at the audio industry for stealing the word "subwoofer"?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do you ever listen to the questions you ask and feel ashamed of yourself?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I asked you first.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh! I hate Sundays! So much work to do!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? But it's the weekend. You don't have to go into the office.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Exactly! So I have all these out-of-the-office replies to write.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, our email client will do that automatically...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh sure, let the computer do it. While I'm at it, maybe I'll just let computers TAKE OVER THE WORLD!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What is - what the - are you smoking in the office? And is that Scotch in that glass? It's 8:30 in the morning!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, we are in the advertising business. So I'm remaking myself in the image of the best possible role model for an admonkey like me. You now share an office with the Don Draper of the 21st century.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, really? Does Don Draper pick bugs out of his fur and eat them?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's the "21st century" part. Just wait, pretty soon everybody'll be doing it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, dare I ask what all these sheep are doing in our office?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Stop whining and help me cram them into this enormous envelope. I'm paying my taxes with sheep this year.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sorry, that won't work. It's not legal to pay your taxes in livestock.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's not what my tax law advisor says.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What tax law is he talking about? Ancient Mesopotamia's?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, if it was good enough for Nebuchadnezzar, who is the IRS to say no?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I heard today was Conan O'Brien's birthday!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Aw, that's nice. He seems like a nice guy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, and he was great in that movie with Wilt Chamberlain.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That wasn't actually-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Every time I smash a whole bunch of mirrors, I think of him.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Speaking of which, don't go in your dressing room with bare feet.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do you wish to satisfy your beloved all night long? BEST LUXURY ROLEX REPLICAS the shocking secret that could take inches off your waist OVERNITE!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Is it me, or do you sound a little crazier than usual today?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've rented my voice out to a Russian mafia spam syndicate. Pretty easy money.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, that's the most -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Please, when I'm working, call me "Gerhard Camacho".

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No way! You couldn't possibly win again! You must have cheated!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, don't start this again.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: THE FIX IS IN! THE FIX IS IN! Did you have those cards hidden up your sleeve? Is that how you did it?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Please, I -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You disgust me. You really turn my stomach, you know that, cheater? HUH, CHEATER? DOES IT MAKE YOU FEEL GOOD TO PUT ONE OVER ON YOUR TRUSTING FRIEND?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You really know how to take all the fun out of Crazy Eights.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, man, my head is so foggy this morning. Just can't wake up. You know, after partying all day yesterday. Whew.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? You mean yesterday, as in April 20? As in, you know...?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's right: the feast of St. Agnes of Montepulciano. It can get pretty wild at my house.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My Earth Day resolution is not to burn any more plastic.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Had you been burning plastic before?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No. I prefer to keep my goals achievable.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Clearly.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Or, better yet, already achieved.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Eww! Gross! Somebody spilled some - some mayonnaise or something all over the plastic utensils in the kitchen!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's tartar sauce. And I happen to have some stainless-steel flatware right here.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, great, thanks. I'll take a fork-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not so fast. It'll cost you a $3 rental fee per utensil, plus a $1 deposit. You get that back if you return it clean.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait a minute: first you befoul the utensils in the office kitchen, and now you're making money by renting out clean utensils?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How else am I supposed to pay for that tartar sauce?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, ol' buddy ol' pal, have I got an investment opportunity for YOU!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But this is hot! The next big thing! Guaranteed bonanza! I'm talkin' biofuel!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's significantly less hare-brained than most of your ideas.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And this kind of biofuel is easy to extract! I've already started filling these jars with it!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But those jars are empty - oh, no. No.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Get out of here! Get away from my desk and take your horrible jars with you!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, you left before we could finish yesterday's shareholder meeting, and I-

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Blindsiding your coworker with Mason jars you've been farting into for the past week is NOT a "shareholder's meeting." It might be assault.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not until they're opened.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just hear me out! Have you ever considered how much energy you're literally farting away every day? If we could just monetize that stream-

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: For the last time, no! That isn't even biofuel!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What do you mean? It's made by the body, BIO, and it's gas, FUEL.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Okay, as impressed as I am that you managed to put those word roots together I still have to say no to this idea.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, then, backup plan: Would you be interested in investing in a fart jar disposal facility?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, fine, I'll ask. What's that stuff on your face?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Eye black. You know, to reduce glare.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, did they let you back on the softball team?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, this is for work. I'm trying to incorporate more physical activity into my daily routine. So I've started juggling. But I was worried I might lose my laptop in the sun when I juggle with it. So I just smear this on my cheeks every day and presto! Problem solved!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, I'll never accuse you of opting for easy answers.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm just saying: it would be nice, after all the money they've made from us as consumers, to get a little acknowledgement.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sure, yeah, but why would they bother? The kids' furniture industry knows that if we monkeys want appropriately sized furniture, we have no choice to keep buying their stuff. It's not like there's anybody out there making furniture explicitly designed for monkeys.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Buddy, you just had the idea that's going to make us both rich.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, no... me and my big mouth...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Furniture for monkeys! This idea can't miss! And I'm offering you the opportunity to get in on the ground floor!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Which sounds good until you notice the elevator's going down.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Aw, c'mon! Tell me what's wrong with that idea!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: For one, monkeys are notoriously poor credit risks. Like, say, you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What, now it's my fault the government took the dollar off of the banana standard? Stop blaming the victim here.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How's the monkey furniture business plan going?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not great. I met with a financial advisor, some venture capitalists, a furniture designer, a marketing team, and a factory manager, and they all made me aware of one crucial overlooked factor that could seriously hinder profitability.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm way too lazy to do all the crap you have to do to run a successful monkey-furniture business. Wanna play some video games?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Can you believe it's May already?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, yes. It's what the calendar says.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, but, like, can you BELIEVE it's MAY already?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You say this every month. On March 1st: "Can you believe it's March already?" On April 1st: "Can you believe it's April already?" What time would it have to be for today to just, you know, feel like the right time?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: A month ago. It should always be a month ago today.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What do you think you're doing here?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I got such a good deal on this mixed case of wine, I set up a wine stand. I'm my own boss now!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You can't just sell wine on the sidewalk. You need a license, and you need to pay taxes, and -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What, are you a cop?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ARE YOU A COP? You have to tell me now or it's entrapment. I've watched enough NYPD Blue to know that.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: All those health articles are lying. "Superfoods" aren't doing me any good.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What did you try?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I put a Twinkie in the microwave with a spider. I ate the whole thing like six hours ago, but I still can't shoot webs or climb walls.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You really should try reading more than just the headlines.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: WHOA! Hey! Stop swinging that fluorescent light tube around! And take off that blindfold!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No! Not until I've learned to see without using my eyes! How can I bring peace to the galaxy if I can't find the peace within?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Seriously, quit it before you slice my head open!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My training is more important than any one of us, especially you. When you see my light tube swinging at you, just get out of the way.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're going to have to learn to - WATCH IT! - walk without working legs if you don't knock it off, you idiot!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sounds like you need some peace training, too. Good thing I've got an extra blindfold.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Whoa. You're looking rather... bloated.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ay... Los churros... Asi que muchos churros.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know you can eat churros on other days, right? You don't have to eat ALL of them just 'cause it's Cinco de Mayo.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Estoy reventando, mi amigo! Traiga un cubo para mi, por favor.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, look, I kinda took French instead of Spanish, so if you're about to do what I think you're about to do, try to keep most of it in your sombrero.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Me arrepiento de nada! Si he de morir, quiero morir gordos y descarados!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I cannot believe you got us kicked out of the theater. We stood in line for, like, two hours to see it!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: PUNY USHER HECKLE MORTIMER! MORTIMER ONLY USE CELLPHONE TWICE! MORTIMER SMASH USHER!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You threw your Junior Mints at that teenager's head, then ran to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: MORTIMER DRINK TOO MUCH SODA! MORTIMER HATE PUNY BLADDER!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: UH OH!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ooo, Mortimer, I don't like the sound of that.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, no, it's nothing. But hypothetically, what if I was watching "Game of Thrones" on the mail server's computer?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, that might slow everything down so the mail went out late.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But nobody would notice, right? Like, nobody actually READS our mail.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Nobody but the thousands of subscribers, I guess. And our boss.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ...uh oh.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do you ever get the feeling that life is order wrong the in moving?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whoa. I did just now, yeah.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: cosmic filter, and chewed up, and spit out again?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Or, like, maybe, everything we say gets caught in some

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sigh... looks like our email server needs whackin' again. Hand me my IT stick.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: These email problems have to stop! My public gets cranky when they don't get enough of me.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And cranky when they get more than enough of you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I have a very finicky public.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Did it work? Did this Daily Digest email send OK?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Who knows? Email is like a rifle: You fire it off into the ether and you have no real way of knowing whether it hit its target until you hear the screams.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, yeah, exactly like that... I think I'll go see if there's anywhere to sit in the hallway...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, check out my snazzy new elliptical machine!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Nice, but what is that goo stretched across the handles?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I use it to make taffy while I'm working out.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ah. Problem and solution, all in one convenient package.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm efficient that way.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Want a mint?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sure. Thanks, my breath was getting a little- GAAAGGHH! IT BURNS! IT KILLS MY TONGUE! Ptheh! Water! Water! What was that thing and why did you let me put it in my mouth?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You, my good man, have just had the honor of being the first customer to try Dr. Mortimer's Curiously Overwhelming Wintermints! You have the look of a man who has just been Bludgeoned By Freshness (TM)! Shall I put you down for a case?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes, a case of chemical burns in the mouth and throat.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I won't say the secret ingredient is lye. But I won't say it's not, either.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Today, I celebrate the woman who gave me everything, who picked me up when I was down, who wiped my tears when I cried, who held my hand until I was steady enough to walk on my own. Today I say, thank you for being there.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Awww. That's so sweet. I'm sure your mom appreciates being appreciated.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I was thinking Bea Arthur, born on this day in 1922. But my mom's pretty cool, too.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, boy. You're wearing a costume again. Still chasing that dream of being a superhero?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, not anymore.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Then why the mask, and cape, and gloves, and boots, and underwear worn on the outside of your pants?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've got my eyes set on a more realistic goal: superhero sidekick. You don't need any powers. You get to live in some rich guy's mansion rent-free. And you do things like start the car or call the cops while the superhero takes all the damage.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I've always suspected that you and I had different standards for "reality."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Now I just need to get adopted. Do you know any billionaires who seem like they might lead secret lives?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What are you doing, and why does it reek in here?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm using this permanent marker to customize the Random Shirts I bought. See, now this one says AT LEAST IT WAS CHEAP.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So you bought some t-shirts sight unseen, it turned out you weren't that into some of them, and now you're wasting your precious time ruining them with a marker - and to you that's "cheap"?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The receipt doesn't lie, pal. Hey, check this one out: MY OTHER SHIRT IS "THE BINGE." Pretty funny, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'll see your nickel. I'm in.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, last down card here, deuces are wild, trips to wi-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: EEEEEYAH EEYAH EEYAH! YEEEEEH EEEYAH!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, you really need to work on your "poker scream."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's with the bulging pockets? And do I smell jalapenos?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: SHHHH! Not so loud! You're going to get me busted for being a nacho smuggler!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're walking around with stolen nachos?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No! I'm not a thief. I bought them fair and square, then smuggled them from the convenience store to the office.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, you know it's not actually illegal to bring nachos into the office, right?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know. But being a smuggler is so much cooler than being a guy who eats convenience-store nachos for lunch.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's with the jeans?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Casual Friday, man. I'm letting it all hang out. Dressing down. Loosening up. Living a little.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But normally we don't wear pants at all.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do you have to ruin this for me, too?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I seven the trashcan.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh... OK, fine. I eight the trashcan.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hahahaha! You ate the trashcan!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, when you invited me over for dinner, I thought the conversation would be a little more stimulating.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, if you're going to eat my food, the least you can do is indulge my taste in apres-dinner amusements. Hey, there goes the microwave! The chimichangas are ready!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: If you could travel anywhere in the world, where would you go?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Gosh, it's hard to say. Vienna would be wonderful - all that culture and history. I've always wanted to see the fjords of Norway. And then there's Buenos Aires. Or Tokyo, maybe. Or the Greek islands. What about you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's easy: the lady monkeys' locker room at the Bronx Zoo.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why do I even bother?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whoa, hey, you've got Buckyballs! In two colors! Cool! There are so many things you can do with them!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, they're pretty neat, but just make sure you don't-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You can build stuff, or use them as whiteboard magnets, or just knead them in your hand, or even, watch, check this out -

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey! Did you just swallow some of those? I was trying to tell you not to! They can really hurt you! Where's my phone? Call 911!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I was gonna say "or freak out your co-worker by pretending to swallow them."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Thanks for stopping by.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, these Woot-Offs are getting stale. I think, to mix it up, this time our "I Want One" button should reject people once in a while.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Reject people?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah! Like, it'd say, "Sorry, but in life, you don't get EVERYTHING you want!"

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Having trouble with the vending machine again?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I PAID GOOD MONEY FOR THOSE FUNYUNS, AND THEY'RE JUST HANGING THERE FOR THE NEXT GUY!!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh! I feel like I'm gonna throw up!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sorry to hear that, Mortimer. Something you ate?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, a burger I had for dinner last night. I guess it did taste a little funny, now that I think about it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hmmm, maybe you didn't grill it long enough.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: "Grill," Monte? Who am I, my grandpa? No, I cook my burgers the modern way: throw them onto a pile of burning plastic.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do you have a quarter I could borrow, Monte? I'll pay you back.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sure. And don't worry about paying me back. It's just a quarter.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Great. In that case, I need ten bucks for lunch.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? I thought you only needed a quarter.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That'll teach you to encourage me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know the old farmer's adage, "Three cats on the deck, it's gonna rain like heck"?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No. In fact, I'm positive those words have never been said in combination.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You got me-I just made it up! Today is National Adage Coinage Day.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That statement is also false.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Aw shucks. You're smarter than a wizened Kentucky marmot, Monte.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, do you want to go to that nerd bar tonight? It's trivia night.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Trivia night? Definitely! I'm there! What time should we meet up?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, right, like I'd go to that. I just wanted to make sure you wouldn't be at the cool bar. Can't have you embarrassing me in front of the ladies.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh... do you have to be so mean?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, mature male primates are natural rivals for mates. All the vodka & Red Bull in the world can't change nature.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, did you see? Woot's totally different today! It was a big shock at first, but I think I'm starting to get into it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Pffft. Not me, man. I'm taking a stand against familiar websites that wreck everything and call it a renovation.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You mean like when you sawed up my dining-room set to "reconfigure it"? And then gave up and left me with a pile of splintered wood? And then tried to bill me for your interior design services?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That was totally different. There were no computers involved.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, where are you going with the water cooler?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, you said you didn't like how I rearranged the break room, so I'm putting it back the way it was before. Anything's better than listening to you sing another song.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Good! Except, maybe, leave the water cooler. It comes in handy after those show-stopping musical numbers. And I kinda like the posters, too; they're cute. But other than those things, yes! Put it back the way it was before!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But that's pretty much everything.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nuh-uh! I want you to make it feel like it did before!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I'm fine. I'm OK. Don't worry about me. I'll be just fine. Really. I'm doing great. I'll be fine.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, something tells me you're upset.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Me? Why? It's not as if my best friend had people over to watch TV last night and didn't invite me. Or wait... it's EXACTLY like that! Judas! Turncoat! Betrayer!

lines.speaker: monte
