lines.text: OK, Mortimer, I'm sorry. I should've invited you. I just really wanted to watch something on C-SPAN, and I know how fidgety you get during Congressional hearings. So I made the mistake of not inviting you, and I feel terr-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: THAT's what you were doing? I take it all back! Not inviting me was the nicest thing anyone's ever done for me!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, well, uh... thanks? I guess...?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You've been unusually non-abrasive today. Why so quiet?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, when a man reaches my position, when he's achieved all that I have and found that there's still something... missing, his mind turns to larger questions. Eternal questions. Questions of transcendence.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, Mortimer, I never knew you had this philosophical side. What questions, specifically?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Like how come nobody has ever made an amphibious Segway. What kind of a world do we live in where that could happen?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I wind up asking myself that every time we have one of these conversations.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Outta my way! I'm jammin'! Power slam! Eat my elbow pad!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ah, I see you've discovered Roller Derby.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm gonna break the monkey barrier! Roller Derby's not just for rockabilly chicks anymore!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: If I ever did Roller Derby, my stage name would be Skatio Africanus. You know, after Scipio Africanus, the Roman general who defeated Hannibal during the Second Punic Wa-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: STOP! STOP! Your nerdiness is so overwhelming, it's even ruining Roller Derby!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer? Mortimer!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Tasmania.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Take off those headphones! I'm trying to talk to you!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Snood. Unconditional. Nero.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: MORTIMER! Whatever you're listening to, please stop talking back!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Would you stop pestering me? I'm trying to do an audio crossword puzzle here.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Vouch for it, fella: I'm fully 'tigued of the decreps who just can't fath the way my generation chatters.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't understand what you just said.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Don't be such a micronoodle! I'm speeching about old wattlefaces like you, who just won't hug the sensashe vocab of today. The whole "generation gap" sitch whistles, fella. It megawhistles!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: First of all, we're the same age. Second of all, that's not actual youth slang.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Of course a total welking screwb like you would say that. Get tubed!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've got to say, the whole thing with Venus was pretty overrated.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? I thought it was amazing. It was a once-in-a-lifetime event!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It was more of an oh-no-my-eyes-are-burning event for me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: This is ridiculous! Are you watching this crap they're showing at E3?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, not really. I don't pay attention to E3.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Exactly! They keep unveiling all this "family entertainment" junk. No one cares!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Maybe families care.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Families don't watch E3. Gaming addicts who have to remind themselves to eat and bathe watch E3. Now make with the explosions!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Lunch time, buddy! What did ya bring?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Salad.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Salad and what?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Just salad.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That doesn't make sense. Salad is what you eat to distract you while you're waiting for the real food to show up.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh man, I'm excited!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Excitement? I LOVE EXCITEMENT! What's going on?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm almost due for an upgrade! I'll finally get a new BlackBerry!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: BlackBerry? Hahahahaha, good one.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm not joking. I really like them.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, sure. People still use BlackBerry phones and everyone's website is still on Geocities and Smashmouth is still on the radio. Whatever, knucklehead.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte, what would be your worst case scenario? Hypothetically, I mean.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I guess a comet striking the earth with enough force to obliterate life as we know it. Why?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hmm, that would be bad. And you're sure that's the worst thing you can think of?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think so. Where is this going?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So nowhere did you mention "lovable coworker accidentally spills oil drum full of maple syrup in your office," right?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: All right! Sport.Woot! Can't wait to start grabbing some of those fitness, sports, and outdoors deals!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Huh. I didn't think you were the active type.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I'm not. But that gear will help me with what's really important: making sure the ladies think I'm the active type.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I see. Self-improvement through self-delusion. Interesting.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, it's really sad that you feel the need to mock me just because I'm trying to make my life better.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OLE OLE OLE OLAAAAAAY! Onward, mighty Mortimovka! Glory to the monkeyland! GOOOOOOOL!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Will you keep it down? What's with the face paint, anyway? And... is that beer I smell? Are you really drinking beer this early in the morning? At work?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm supporting my ancestral homeland, Mortimovka, in the Euro soccer championships! The Orange Monkeys are this year's team of destiny!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: There's no such country as Mortimovka.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's what makes this so great. I can have all the fun of being a soccer fanatic without having to watch any of the games! Now where did I put that vuvuzela full of pale ale?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, do you have a match? Or a lighter?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You mean, so you can set that little referee effigy on fire? No.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: He's lucky that's all I'm doing to him after he robbed the Mortimovkan team of victory yesterday!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I have to say, for an imaginary country, Mortimovka has some really passionate soccer fans. And the knots on that noose are really well-done.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Of course they are. Noose-tying is an ancient Mortimovkan folk art.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, what are you doing for lunch today?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer? You up for lunch?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ...Sorry, but I'm not going to play your headgames. You're just trying to weaken my resolve before the big Mortimovka vs. Montemonchia soccer match tomorrow.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, so I have a fake country playing in the Euro soccer championships, too, huh? Are we any good?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And now you're taking advantage of our friendship to pump me for information. Really classy. I should have expected as much from a filthy Montemonchian.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ha ha! In your face, Montemonchian vermin! May the glory of Mortimovka kick you in the stomach for a thousand years!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sounds like your fake country beat my fake country in the Euro soccer championships.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Damn right! Mortimovka's stirring 1-0 triumph proved once and for all our inherent superiority to the degenerate, lowlife, slothful, dishonest, cowardly Montemonchians!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sounds like an exciting game.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It was, but the important thing about these tournaments is how they promote international friendship.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Pretty crazy news about Matt Rutledge leaving Woot, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, crazy... like a fox.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But that doesn't even make sense.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, what doesn't make sense is making small talk while a power vacuum is waiting to be filled! In times of crisis, frightened people turn to a strong leader! Wooters are crying out for an iron paw to seize the helm! That's why I'm moving into Matt's office right now and refusing to leave until I'm granted dictatorial powers over the entire company!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, sure. But why do you have all those baggies?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The fight to restore dignity and honor and glory will be a long one! And let's just say my will is stronger than my bladder.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Check out what an awesome son I am: for Father's Day I gave my Dad a certificate for a year of oil changes.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's it? Your Dad sweats and sacrifices and denies his own dreams, all for you - and all he gets is his oil changed?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, the certificate lets him change the oil in my car.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer! I can't believe you would use Father's Day as an excuse to get free favors for yourself!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, it's what he asks for every year.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh. Well, I guess that does sound like a Dad.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: *ungh* Are you... are you just gonna sit there or are you gonna give me.. a hand... with these sandbags?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, sure, sorry! Here, let me - wait, why are we bringing sandbags into the office?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well... *huff* management denied my request to work from the beach every day this summer... so I thought I'd... turn the office into a beach.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, I've got an idea: let's not do that. It sounds really messy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You think this is messy? Just wait until we get the two dozen seagulls I ordered.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, I need a new hard drive. This one is totally full of music, movies, and TV shows I've downloaded.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, how could you? You wouldn't steal a pair of jeans, or a car, or a burrito.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Dude, you know a site where you can torrent burritos?!? Send me an invitation! Please!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's totally not the point I was trying to -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do they have quesadillas, too? Or enchiladas? Or, oooh, fajitas?!? Why didn't you tell me this sooner? This changes everything!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Can you believe it? It's the first day of summer already!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, you make every day feel like the first day of summer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because of my sunny disposition and personal warmth?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, because it always feels like the longest day of the year.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, no. That orange vest can only mean one thing: somehow, you got appointed office Safety Czar. Gold help us all.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Now, now, Monte, the term "Czar" is so grandiose. I'm just a humble servant.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, that's a good sign, I guess...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You can just call me "Safety Pharoah".

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Now that I'm the office Safety Pharaoh, a few things are going to change around here.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, boy. Here we go.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: To protect your eyes, all employees are now limited to one hour of screen time per day. For the other seven hours, you'll just have to find a way to get your work done without looking at your monitor. What do you think about that?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think I'm about to find out how many copies of my resume I can email out in an hour.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: MONTE! Are you using the copy machine without eye protection?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, you're taking your appointment as the office Safety Pharaoh way too seriously.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You dare question the decision of the Safety Pharaoh? I sentence you to 50 lashes with one of those ergonomic gel wrist-rest things!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So your plan is to hurt us to enforce the rules that are supposed to keep us from getting hurt? That doesn't make any sense.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm not following you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I swear, the Safety Pharaoh's work is never done...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, can I use some of those staples?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not until I'm finished filing the sharp points down. It wouldn't be safe.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, I think I can be trusted to use a staple without hurting myseOW! MY HAND! MY HAND! WHAT DID YOU DO THAT FOR?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: To fulfill my duty as Safety Pharaoh to teach you about office safety. Now stop bleeding all over the place. It's unsanitary.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What? What is this? Half a dozen dry erase markers left overnight with their caps off?!? It's an outrage!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, I'm speechless. I just hope this horrible sight doesn't give me nightmares.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not only has it dried out the markers, but now we're breathing in the fumes. As office Safety Pharaoh, I pledge that this shall not stand!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, making you Safety Pharaoh was a brilliant decision by the company.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Thank you for the vote of confidence, Monte.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, all the time you waste on stuff like this keeps you from messing anything up by doing your real job.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Binder clips? BANNED! Too pinchy! Three-hole punch? BANNED! Too punchy!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, I say this as a friend but also as an advocate of sanity: you've got to resign as office Safety Pharaoh. The power is doing dark things to you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's dangerous talk, Monte. And you know how I feel about things that are dangerous in the office.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But don't you see? The only way to eliminate 100% of safety hazards in the office is to eliminate the office.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why... you're right! The office: BANNED!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, stop complaining. You should be thanking me for rescuing you from that deathtrap of an office. Besides, it isn't that hot out here.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I just can't believe the company bylaws give the office Safety Pharaoh the power to close the office.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'll admit, even I was surprised by that one. But hey, now we get to work in the parking lot. That's fun.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, do we at least have any water out here? That's a safety issue, right?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Beats me. I'm the office Safety Pharaoh, not the parking lot Safety Pharaoh.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, what are you doing? You can't go in there!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm not working in the parking lot anymore. I'm going back to my desk.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But it's not safe! Staples! Pencils! Pencil sharpeners! It's a killing field in there!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm saying what nobody else will: the Safety Pharaoh has no clothes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, if you're not gonna respect the monkey, at least respect the office.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Friends. Co-workers. Subjects. Thank you for joining me here today.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, it's just me here.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It is with great sadness that I announce my resignation from the Safety Pharaoh's throne.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Aw, man. Now we have to go back to getting work done.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I will always cherish the memories of my reign. But I have found that it's true what Mark Twain said: heavy lies the monkey that wears the fluorescent orange vest.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow. I'm too happy about this to even point out how wrong that quotation is.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How did you survive the Great Internet Outage of 2012?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, let's see... I read a book for the first half-hour or so. Then I called my Grandma, I hadn't talked to her in ages. My next-door neighbor needed help hanging some curtains, so I did that, and we wound up talking for a while. Turns out he's a really interesting guy. Used to live in Thailand - man, he had some stories. After that, I came home and folded some laundry that had been sitting there for two days. Then I went to bed early and got the first good night's sleep I've had in weeks.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, you got a lot done.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know, it was horrible! Let's hope it never happens again.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, it's Keep America Weird week on the Woot blog. They'll be posting about some of the stuff that makes America not only the greatest country on Earth, but the weirdest.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So what's so great about that? I've heard that weird people cost the American economy $79 trillion per year.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's ridiculous. Where did you hear that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: From this guy in my Kabbalist Emu Farmers for UFO Truth group. He really knows his stuff.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Would you quit stomping around? I'm doing delicate chemistry here.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What the- you're not trying to make your own firecrackers, are you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm sorry, Monte, but not all of us have money to throw around on costly storebought firecrackers.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I just thought you should know that they aren't actually made out of crackers.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oooh, hey, that English muffin looks good.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I think you mean "Freedom muffin".

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, I'm pretty sure I don't.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What are you, some kind of King-lover? How dare you use my breakfast to pay tribute to the hated Redcoats? And on July 4, of all days!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, I love America as much as the next monkey. I just don't believe that political agendas - especially ones that are over 200 years old - should dictate changes to the English language.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I think you mean "the Freedomish language."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So I'm thinking about getting a dog.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Are you sure? Dogs are a big responsibility. You have to feed them, walk them and take them to the vet when they're sick.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Actually I …

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Plus, you can't even keep a plant alive. Remember the time I asked you to water mine while I was away?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I meant I was thinking about getting a hot dog. To eat. FOR LUNCH. But thanks for the vote of confidence, Monte.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So did you hear about Tom Cruise and Katie Holmes?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, I've got more important things to worry about than another failed celebrity marriage.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well for starters, the debt crisis, unemployment and homelessness.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What about love, Monte? If those two crazy kids can't make it, what hope is there for the rest of us?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You look like you've lost a little weight. Have you been working out?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nah, but I've been watching my carbs.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That must be tough. No bread. No pasta. Hey, is that a cupcake?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I didn't say I wasn't eating them. I said I was watching them ... watching them go right in to my mouth.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you see it's like 109° in some parts of the country? I bet some people are melting.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, people can't actually melt. Well, not because it's a little hot outside.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sure they can. I saw it happen once on the set of The Wizard of Oz. I was Flying Monkey #5. It was my big break!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You expect me to believe that? You'd have to be more than 70 years old.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What can I say? Good genes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Have you ever felt true heartbreak, Monte? Have you?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, great, this should be fun.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do you know the longing of true yearning? Or the yearning of true longing? Have you ever tasted an unrequited kiss?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Did the fountain soda at the gas station have too much water in it again?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I should have known you wouldn't understand.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Take a good look at me, Monte, because the next time you see me, I'll be wearing a top hat filled with champagne and smoking a diamond cigar.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're not driving another load of cans to Michigan again, are you? They're not really going to give you a dime for each one, you know.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, not that. I'm going to quit my job with no notice, then turn around and offer my services to Woot as a consultant at twice what I make now! They'll have no choice but to pay up!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And what services would those be, exactly?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know. Everything I do at my job now. All that stuff. Work stuff or whatever. Like, uh... OK, maybe I better think this through a little more.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I can consult on that for you. But I warn you, I'm not cheap.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, it's Woot's birthday! And they're celebrating with the Wootathon, a round-the-clock stream of Woot video classics old and new!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Don't remind me.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, right... still upset that they rejected your video submission?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Me? No way! I'm GLAD they didn't make me waste my awesome video idea on their dumb Wootathon! Now I can go sell it to HBO or something!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, I'm sure the networks will fall all over themselves to broadcast Baby Cops.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uh, excuse me? It's called Infant Patrol.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Here's a scary thought, Monte: what if Friday the 13th fell on Halloween?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Obviously, that could never happen.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's what the kids at Camp Crystal Lake thought, too.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know what really burns me up? I mean, really really gets on my last nerve?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, but -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: When I'm trying to do something urgent, like catch a bus, and some self-centered loudmouth just won't shut up about some stupid, petty little point he's trying to make. Do they ever stop to consider that maybe, maybe, people have more important things to do? Oh, no. Not them. It's all about me, me, me.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Funny you should say that -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's what's wrong with the world, if you ask me. Too many people with zero consideration for others. News flash, people: you're not the center of the universe! Oh, hey, you've got your backpack on. Where are you headed?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, just to the bus stop to wait 40 minutes for the next bus.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, I miss the days when Woot was all tech deals.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Funny you say that, Mortimer. Today we launch Tech.Woot! Nothing but gadgets, hardware, and such! Products without circuits need not apply! All tech deals, all the time!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's the point? I thought Woot was already all tech deals.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ugh! What reeks in here?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Reeks of deliciousness, you mean?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Blegh! No! It smells like - oh, wait a minute... you did it, didn't you? I can't believe you actually did it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yes, Monte. I'm not just talking about it any more. I've finally gone and created the world's first salt-and-vinegar shampoo. But I'm not just the inventor, I'm also -

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, a customer. So I smell.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's the worst shampoo idea I've ever heard. What on Earth makes you think anyone wants their hair to smell like salt and vinegar?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, every other shampoo on the market smells like some kind of fruit. I'm simply serving the untapped market of consumers who prefer savory to sweet.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's ridiculous. What's next? Barbecue-scented shampoo?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Who told you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ...so if you're talking in absolute terms, you gotta like the elephant's chances. If you're talking relative to its own weight, then my money's on the shrew.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, sure, great. Now can we wrap up the discussion about which animal would win a hot-dog eating contest?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Of course, if we're including aquatic animals, well, that changes everything. I mean, a blue whale's gotta put away, what, three tons of zooplankton a day? Let's see, at two ounces per hot dog, that comes to...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I've really got to learn to stay out of political arguments on Facebook. They never convince anyone of anything. They just make people hate each other.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What a crock of BS! That's exactly the kind of self-serving doubletalk I'd expect from a... hey, what are your political beliefs, anyway?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Does it matter?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Now that you mention it, I guess not.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Would you like a sun-dried tomato, Monte?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, are they supposed to be fuzzy?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What, that? It's just a little mold. It's a normal byproduct of sun-drying in my basement. Perfectly natural.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, second question: are they supposed to be magenta?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I ran out of tomatoes and had to use radishes, OK? Jeez... if I'd known you were this picky I never would have offered.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And I told him, "You'll get dinged if you pull funds out of a Roth IRA early. A 401(k) on the other hand..."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: This is boring. I challenge you to a race around the world starting NOW!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, wha--

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You, boy -- find me a hot air balloon and four stout barrels of kero-sene fuel. Quickly, while the wind holds westward!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, I never agreed to this...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Then the million dollars is mine! GOODBYE MONTEEEEEeeeeee

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I want to get in shape, but I don't want to do any work. Any ideas?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: There are no shortcuts to health, Mortimer. You've got to put in the effort.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, fine. So what's the easiest way to put in effort?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, you see, there is no easiest way. It takes dedication.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: "Dedication"? Is that the name of supplement or something? Sounds great, I'll order a whole case!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ahhhhh. That's better.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer! It's rude to pass gas at work.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's the silliest thing I've ever hear. What am I supposed to do with it?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, I don't know. Hold it in I guess.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: All day? That's impossible.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No it's not. I do it every day.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You do?? No wonder your face starts looking so serious in the late afternoon.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: BEFORE YOU CAME INTO MY LIFE I MISSED YOU SO BAD...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer! Stop singing at the top of your lungs.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But it's my Wednesday jam. I MISSED YOU SO BAD.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, your tastes are not reflected in others. Vis à vis: Me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Stop thinking and just let the music take over. I MISSED YOU SO, SO BAD.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ...Turns out I was confused. You'll get punished if you take out funds early from a 401(k), NOT a Roth --

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: -- Snapdragon! Eggplant! Burpee!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What the heck? Why did you interrupt me?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I figured that just saying interesting-sounding words would be more entertaining than whatever you were talking about. Calliope! Arugula!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: This is hopeless, you sybarite!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: There you go, Monte, that's a fun one!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: U.S.A! U.S.A! We're number 2, we're number 2!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Umm, Monte. I think you mean, "We're number 1!"

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Based on the latest economic projections, by 2019 the U.S. is going to have the second-largest economy. So I'm getting a jumpstart.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Are you saying our society has passed its golden age and is beginning its slow, irreversible decline?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You said it, Mortimer. Not me.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm serious. Read it again. "Ne-a-pol-i-tan." It's got nothing to do with Napoleon. Totally different word.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Pffft, whatever. It's obviously named after Napoleon.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: "Neapolitan" means "from Naples". That's not even in France.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh yeah? Well then why is Neapolitan ice cream the same colors as the French flag?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ...I'm really hoping you have some kind of blue-brown color-blindness. Otherwise, I just wasted five minutes reasoning with an imbecile.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Check out my rad new athletic gear! I've got London 2012 fever!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, my... I don't think that's how that sweatband is supposed to be worn. Doesn't that hurt?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Keep hatin', Monte. Keep hatin'. Every great athlete needs naysayers to overcome.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I know one athlete who might need surgery if he's not careful.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, gimme a hand and grab the other end of this desk, wouldja?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, OK... where are we taking it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My new office: the men's room.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It seems SOMEone in this office has issues with the simian body. I've been told to stop picking nits from my fur in the office due to complaints from an anonymous co-worker. So I'm moving somewhere where I can do as I please - that'll show 'em! Hey... you weren't the one who complained, were you?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, but if I'd known it would be this easy to get you out of the office...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How's your new "office" in the men's room working out?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Fantastic! You know, the other stall is still available. You might want to grab it before somebody else does.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, no thanks. I'm pretty happy here.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: See, that's your problem: you settle for too little. Me, I've got a space all my own, with a door I can close. What does this room have that the men's room doesn't?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: For one thing, it's relatively free of airborne human waste particles.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Take it from me: after a couple of hours, you forget you're even wearing the surgical mask.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm telling you, you're missing out, not having your desk in the men's room like I do. It's the nerve center of this whole office. The things I'm learning there will open the doors of the powerful.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do those doors go all the way to the floor?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Go ahead and scoff. It'll be small consolation when you're languishing here and I'm the one shaking hands with the captains of industry.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I hope they use hand sanitizer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte! I knew it! You've come by to check out that other stall, haven't you? I'm glad you're finally seeing the advantages of moving your desk into the men's room.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, actually I'm just here to, you know, use the restroom for its intended purpose.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh. Oh, I see. You're too good to work in my office, but you can just waltz in and befoul the place whenever you feel the need.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes, I do! Because it's the men's room!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Fine, yeah, sure. But I hope you know what this means. If you can do that in my office, what's stopping me from doing it in your office?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think I'll try the gas station.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I bet you're glad to get out of that "office" in the men's room for a couple of days, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Actually, I was thinking about getting a similar setup together at home, too.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? No.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Think about all the different places there are to sit in your house, for various purposes. Now imagine how much spare room you'd have if you combined all those seats into one.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, if you lived like that, you certainly wouldn't have to worry about accommodating any visitors.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ha! Ha! You said "commode".

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uh, hey, Monte. How's it going?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Fine, I guess. Is that all you have to say? Nothing about how great it is to live and work in a bathroom?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No. I, uh, I'm kinda done with that.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? I just assumed that if you thought it was a good idea in the first place, nothing would change your mind.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, well, I sat down to "dinner" last night, and - I, I just couldn't do it. It was... too gross. What's happened to me, Monte? Am I getting too soft? Too civilized?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, I think there's still plenty of room between you and "too civilized".

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, every time I turn the Olympics on, it's the same thing! Crying gymnasts! Bikini volleyball! And the swimming, my God, the swimming! How many different races do they need? IT'S ALL SWIMMING!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What would you prefer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Y'know, the weird sports! The ones everybody forgets about until the Olympics! Where's my water polo? My team handball? My shot-put? Do you realize that of all the swimmers I've seen in the past week, not two were synchronized?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, well, if you check the cable listings here, you can find all of that stuff. See, look. Here's some team handball on right now.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh. Uh, OK. Cool. I'll leave that on while I think up something else to complain about.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ...And so that's my interpretive dance piece about my feelings on Manhattan versus New England Clam Chowder.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Dance? All you did was yell for fifteen minutes about how bad Manhattan Clam Chowder is.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My vocal chords were doing most of the dancing.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My heat bill is going to be through the roof this month!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, it's August. Why are you running a heater?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Equilibrium. Won't feel hot outside if it's equally hot inside!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think you might have sweat out your sense of logic.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Just bought plane tickets home for Thanksgiving.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ha! Monte, that's like forever from now!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's saving me hundreds! You should do it too!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Better idea: I buy a suitcase with breathing holes and I fly home as YOUR luggage!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think “better” might be the wrong word there.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, what you eating there?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It’s a dönor kebab. And it’s dëliciöüs.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think you may be using those umlauts excessively.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whätëvër! Tëll thät tö Mötley Crüe.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte, pull my finger!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That is not something that sounds remotely appealing to me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, seriously. I jammed it. I need you to pop it back into place.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, if you really need me to …

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: :::pfffrrrttt:::

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't even know why I'm surprised.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Break my code, Monte: z,pmyr dyomld

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How did you know I took a remedial cryptology course? Let's see...got it!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Mp esu

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes way - you're speaking as though your hands were shifted one key to the right on a QWERTY keyboard. And I do not stink, thank you very much.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, don't the crazy low prices on back-to-school gear in Woot's Dealgebra 101 event remind you of that time we saw Mr. Fortner pulling a used coffee filter out of the teacher's lounge trashcan? He was so cheap!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: We didn't go to school together.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Or that time we saw him filling his pockets with those little plastic cups full of nondairy creamer at the gas station? What a skinflint!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't know who Mr. Fortner is. But if he was a teacher, I bet he didn't make that much money, and he was just trying to economize so he could afford to pursue his dream of inspiring young minds like yours.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, now you're all full of compassion? Where were you when we were all making fun of Mr. Fortner for bringing his baloney sandwiches to school wrapped in old McDonald's burger wrappers? You weren't too high and mighty to laugh then, were you?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I can't believe Woot is featuring all these back-to-school deals without anything for the rest of us! Their Dealgebra 101 event discriminates against the old and/or willfully ignorant!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How, exactly? You can use things like backpacks and laptops and vacuum cleaners whether you're in school or not.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But it's a back-to-school sale! And I'm not in school! That's a clear violation of my rights!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Woot can sell whatever it wants. They're not taking anything from you, or keeping you from doing anything. What "rights" are they violating, exactly?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My right to feel pandered to, Monte! Have you forgotten the stirring words of President Benjamin Franklin: "It's all about me"?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, our educational system owes you an apology, I'll give you that.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: There is a tide in the affairs of men, which, taken at the flood, lead on to fortune.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Shakespeare! It's from Julius Caesar, and it means...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No no, I mean THAT.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh. That's a shiny aluminum pie pan, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's up, Monte? You look stressed.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's my house. I couldn't sell it so I rented it, and now I have to pay for a bunch of repairs.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Bummer.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sometimes I wish it would just disappear.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: If you're serious about that, I know a guy. Just say the word.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Unless that guy is David Copperfield, no thanks.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So I'm trying to decide between two apartments. One is roomy and within my budget. The other is small and overpriced, but it has an amazing view.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What kind of view?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Blonde. 5'10". Looks like a dancer or something.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Let me guess: you're going with the view.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I am starting to lean that way, yeah.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You have any plans this weekend, Monte?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes, actually, barring inclement weather --

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm going to a water park tomorrow.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK...good to know. Why did you ask me a question if you just wanted to answer it yourself?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why else would you ask somebody a question?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Maaaaaahn-TEEEE... I'm boooooo-OORED...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, OK. Maybe we can pass the time by whining at each other.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sounds like fun! I'll go first: Maaaaaahn-TEEEE... I'm boooooo-OORED...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So Woot's running a Woot-Off called Last Call for Bacon. They say it's meant to put the whole "bacon thing" to rest. I don't know. It seems kinda -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wayh, whuhnm? Dshooh shey thehhsh behinh? Weh?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, sorry, my mouth was full.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh... let me guess: it was full of bacon.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, Swedish fish. But... there's bacon? Where? Who's got bacon? And how do I get it into my face?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: This Woot-Off looks fun. Too bad I don't eat bacon.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? Bacon seems exactly like something that you, specifically, would be annoyingly obsessed with.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nah. It bugs me that they make such a big deal about how it's "cured," but nobody ever says what disease it had in the first place.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer! Stop! Why are you smashing those Woot-Off lights?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because we just had our last Woot-Off, right?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: WRONG! We called it "Last Call for Bacon" because it was supposed to bury bacon humor. It was definitely NOT the last Woot-Off!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I am so tired of you not telling me these things BEFORE I do something stupid.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You ever wonder if the corn kernels hurt when they pop?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No. They're corn kernels. They have no feelings.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How does it feel, Monte? What's life like without a heart?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So what do you think of this whole Lance Armstrong debacle?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't get it. The guy is really good at riding bikes. So what if he's a little dopey?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, no. He's been accused of DOPING. You know, taking performance enhancing drugs.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I still don't see the problem.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, want to go to brunch?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sure, let me just make sure I have 2 hours and $23 to spend on overcooked poached eggs and tepid coffee.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow. Why do you hate brunch so much?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My estranged father is a Monte Cristo sandwich.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's not true.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No no no NO! You're still flat on the chorus, Monte!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? It sounded pretty good to me -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, because you're still clinging to the "traditional" idea of harmony! You clearly still haven't grasped the revolutionary concepts of Mortimelodics! Is that creaky old mind of yours still able to open, Lawrence Welk?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: One, Mortimelodics sounds terrible. Two, what's wrong with Lawrence Welk?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, have you "liked" Woot's Facebook page yet?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Me? No way. I don't do that Facebook stuff.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, I'm not sure about it either. I prefer real experiences over virtual ones. And I find the commercialization of online community space a little troubling.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, me too. Plus I was banned from Facebook for life for running a pyramid scheme promoting fraudulent diet pills. But mostly because of that other stuff, yeah.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Would you ever live overseas?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, totally! I studied abroad in college and it really changed the way I see the world!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, sorry! When I said "live overseas" I guess I meant, "live over the sea." Like, in a blimp.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Think anything scary is going to happen in the office tomorrow?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, not really. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because it'll be Friday the 31st.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? No, it's Friday the 13th that's the scary day, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, we can argue about the order if you want. All I know is, if there's a Friday, a 3, and a 1, stuff gets crazy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do you believe in unicorns?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Of course not. Unicorns are mythical creatures invented by the ancient Greeks.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well if they don't exist, then who ate the rainbow?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What rainbow?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Exactly.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Where do butterflies get their wings, Monte?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What a thoughtful question. Caterpillars enter a cocoon, self-phage much of their body, and slowly grow wings through a process called...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: This isn't nearly as full of unicorns and rainbows as I expected.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, that's science, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, science is baking soda volcanoes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Today I'm honoring the sacrifices and contributions of American workers with the ultimate gesture of solidarity: a one-day strike!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But you have the day off anyway.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: If this strike had consequences, it wouldn't be a gesture.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, did you see the Woot writers when you were at the big PAX gamer convention last weekend?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't want to talk about it. A pox on PAX, I say.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, I see... your costume didn't go over too well with the crowd, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: For such an important character in gaming history, you'd really expect the yellow piece from Sorry to get more respect.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Can I borrow your car to run a quick errand, Monte?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Jeez, I don't really feel comfortable with that. Like, what if you got into an accident?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Simple: I would just borrow your car insurance and take care of it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I wish I could bring my dog to work!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But wait, it's a dog friendly workplace. What's stopping you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I just need the American Kennel Club to recognize "Swarm of Bees" as a breed of dog.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, I'm so hungry I could eat a horse.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Are you referring to the great quantity of meat that a horse would provide, hence satisfying your enormous hunger? Or do you mean that you're so hungry you would even consider eating horse meat despite the social taboo?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm sorry, what? I couldn't hear you over all this chewing.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I really love cheese.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OMG me too!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: A nice brie tart or an herbed goat's milk. Superb. What's your favorite kind of cheese?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hmm. So many great choices. But I think I'm going to have to go with "melted."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Don't expect me to get too much work done today! I'll be playing Borg of Crap, the new casual game at Tech.Woot, all day long!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Have fun with that. I'll be busy all week trying to win a random piece of junk in the Pieces of Crap giveaways on Woot's Facebook page.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, great. Now I have two things I gotta do instead of work. I can't even relax when I'm wasting time.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh... this day always makes me a little sad.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know, me too. But I've learned how to handle it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How's that? By doing volunteer work or something?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not quite. By taking a deep breath, giving a moment's thought to everybody we lost, reflecting on how lucky I am to be alive, and then... playing video games! Eating junk food! Watching awful movies! Blasting obnoxious music! Doing all the dumb fun stuff I would miss doing if I was suddenly gone!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, for once, I'm not sure you're wrong.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, thanks. You want some of these Spicy Buffalo Cheddar 'n Chili Doritos or what? I'm about to finish the bag.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's easier to ask for forgiveness than permission, right?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, I think that depends on the transgression -- OW!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm so sorry, Monte. I should never have shot that rubber band at your face. Will you forgive me?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're abusing the system.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Today we celebrate the birth of America's finest actor.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Marlon Brando?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nope. Sam Neill.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I see two problems with that assessment.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: EEEK! Boardwalk Empire starts up again tomorrow night!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It might just be the best show on TV right now. Can you believe that at the end of last season they ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: LALALALALALALALALALALALALALALALALALALALALALA

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I haven't seen it. I don't want you to ruin it for me.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'll admit, this beer you homebrewed is pretty decent. What did you call it again?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What? Oh, that, right. Mortimerbrau. Sorry, I'm busy studying for my pilot's license.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm sorry, you're doing what, now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Studying for my pilot's license. Frank Zappa said "You can't be a real country unless you have a beer and an airline." One down, one to go - soon Mortimeria will finally be free! What do you think about that, yanqui?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think the world community is breathing a sigh of relief that Frank Zappa is not Secretary-General of the U.N.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, Monte! Tell the nice people all about The Search for Crapbeard's Treasure, our Woot-Off treasure hunt!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh... I'd really... rather not...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Go ahead! Explain how certain Woot-Off sales have pieces of a treasure map hidden inside them, and how they can assemble those pieces to discover a very special Woot treasure! Go on!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, it's... seriously, I just don't want to... you know... think about it anymore...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Aww, c'mon! You're not still grossed out by the name "Crapbeard", are you?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ugh! Don't say it! A beard of crap... I just can't get that... that picture out of my head...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So, why do leaves change color anyway?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do you want the science version or the Mortimer-friendly version?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Mortimer-friendly please!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, once upon a time, there was this dragon who said, "Green leaves are boring!"

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oooh! I like where this is going!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: UGH! So hung over! Why did they have to put "Drink Like a Pirate" day in the middle of the week?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, it was "Talk Like a Pirate" day!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, and let me guess: today's "POINT OUT MY MISTAKES LIKE A JERK" DAY!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So, who do you think was hotter? Dawson or Pacey?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hotter in what sense? Like physical attractiveness or like star power? Because while Joshua Jackson went on to moderate success, James Van Der Beek pretty much fell off the grid after Varsity Blues.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oooh, I'm sorry. That is incorrect. The answer we were looking for was, "I dunno, I'm a dude."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Today, the Autumnal Equinox ushers in the first day of fall! Don't you just love this time of year?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not really. All the leaves dry up and fall off the trees.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I suppose it is a little sad, how something once beautiful and full of life withers and fades until it is no more. But in death, it is offered back to the Earth as nourishment. And then in spring, reborn!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I have no idea what you are talking about. I just really hate raking.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What the heck are you doing with those peas, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Um, splitting them one at a time to make split pea soup, duh! Man, I've got a new found respect for chefs! This cooking stuff takes FOREVER!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But that's not how... You know what? Never mind. This'll be fun to watch.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So, wait. Should I halve them or quarter them?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Actually, for best results, I think you want to cut them into eights.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, then in the other cell, you enter the formula you need. In this case it would be SUM=(B6+C6)*A6, which should equal the total amount of pretax revenue for the montSURPRISE!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: AAAHHHH! Mortimer! What's the big idea of screaming like that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just trying to liven up a dull workday with the occasional surprise.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Great, except that it scares the heck out of me, and makes it hard to get any work done.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Those are just side benefits.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: AAAAAGH! What is this spilled all over my desk? Did you do this?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'll take those questions in reverse order. Yes, I did. And it's Fizz Zippo, the miraculous zero-calorie soda I drink constantly. Did you notice anything else about it?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's disgusting?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No - it's not sticky! That's right: a soda that isn't sticky! You can thank the space-age chemical sweeteners in Fizz Zippo for that!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: For what? My stuff being ruined?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I feel like you're missing my point here.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: From now on, I want you to call me "Morty B. Cool."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, what does the B stand for?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Be.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Then you don't need to abbreviate it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, I guess you're right, Monte B. Ruining-My-Fun.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh! The boss is all mad at me for taking a sick day!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? That's ridiculous!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know, right? And I gave him plenty of notice. I said, "I'm going to be out sick next Thursday," and he got all weird about it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh my Jeebus, have you heard about the Shapoopie? Best. Invention. Ever. I'll never have to touch the poopie again!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, I saw one at the pet store. Bet it makes walking your dog a lot more enjoyable, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait ... it's for dogs?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Have you seen these people who just stand on the side of the road and eat wild blackberries off the bushes? They don't even wash them. It's disgusting.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, I once saw you eat a stick of butter after dropping it in a pile of dog hair.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Um, 5-Second Rule! And it takes WAY longer than five seconds to grow a blackberry.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, Monte, I've always thought of us like Tim and Wilson from 'Home Improvement.'

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I didn't watch that show. How come?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, Tim was the headstrong goofball to Wilson's wise sage.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, I think I get where you're going with this.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Plus, Wilson's face was always partially obscured, just like mine.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait a second...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Boy, those replacement NFL referees were SO bad yesterday, my Aunt Gladys changed her will so-

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Whoa, whoa, the real refs have been back on the job for days now. If you're gonna write topical jokes, you should probably pay a little more attention to the news.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I was holed up all week writing this one. How long does the new contract last?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Eight years.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: All right! I can write five or six replacement-referee jokes by then!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Those 3D TVs on Woot today sure look amazing... but I don't know what I would watch on them in 3D.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: UH WUT? Avatar is just about to come out on 3D Blu-ray! In like two weeks! The biggest 3D blockbuster of all time! What else do you need?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, I don't know. I was hoping for something a little more to my taste.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Like what? My Dinner With Andre?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It would be like I was sitting right there at the table! Should I order the quail salad or the potato soup?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How would you feel if I got a perm?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm not even sure it's possible. I mean, you've got plenty of hair, but it's all really short.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why can't you ever just tell me how you feel, Monte? Why does it always have to be facts and reality with you?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Come on. Just try it. Just once. One little taste.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I told you, I don't like blue cheese! Lay off, OK?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But this is Point Reyes Blue Cheese. It will change everything you ever thought you felt about blue cheese.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No way! My rule is "when cheese is blue, no thank you"!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Are you sure? Because it's really incredibly pure and creamy and good. And I'm about to eat the last piece I bought from Wine.Woot...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK! Fine! If it'll shut you up... Hey, wow... That's amazing... Mmm-hmmm! Yes! I want more! But now it's all gone because YOU ate the rest of it! Thanks a lot, "pal"!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: UGH. I am so tired of moving! I've had to move, like, four times in the past two years!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why don't you buy a house or sign an extended lease? That way you can stay in one spot for awhile.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't know what that has to do with riding the bus. Seriously, EVERY TIME an old lady gets on, she just stands there and shames me until I give up my seat.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So I was thinking sushi tonight. You in?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No thanks. I just can't get past the whole raw fish thing.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait ... sushi is fish? And it isn't cooked? I had no idea!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, yeah. What did you think it was?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I guess I never really thought about it. Next you're gonna tell me that ball of green stuff isn't chewing gum.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I think my spam folder is getting full, Monte. What should I do?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You can just delete the contents. Does it smell weird in here to you, ?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's what I'm talking about. The filing cabinet where I store unwanted, opened cans of Spam processed meat overfloweth.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's disgusting. You definitely need to clean that out ASAP.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK fine, here's a bib and a bottle of hot sauce. Dig in!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've got a great new idea for a mobile restaurant app!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? Aren't there already, like, 10,000 restaurant apps?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not like Piehole (TM). When this new app detects your friends talking about restaurants, it plays deafening, abrasive noises until they change the subject to something more interesting.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow. Maybe there's a spark of genius inside you after all.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, I'll bite: why is there a pair of sunglasses suspended from fishing wire above your desk?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So I can say "Deal with it" and then push this button here and make the sunglasses descend onto my face.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, right. That dog GIF. Funny. I guess.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Analog memes are gonna be huge, dude! Oh, that reminds me, I need to throw one of these cheeseburgers up into the drop ceiling. That cat's probably getting hungry.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're keeping a cat up there? That's cruel, and probably against office regulations.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do I have to push the button again?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know those k-cup coffee machines? Why can't they make anything else? Like sandwiches?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Because you can't get a sandwich simply by running hot water through some grounds.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well then, maybe we need to start re-thinking sandwiches!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Remember that Brad Pitt movie from last year, Moneyball? Well, I say we make the sequel: Monkeyball! And it's about us!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How is that a sequel? Like, what does it add to the original story?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: A k. In the title.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Okay, let's add "sequel" to your list of vocabulary words to learn.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So, did you watch the Vice Presidential Debate last night?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nah, not really my thing. But you know who does love debates? De-FISHES! HA!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You stole that joke from Big Bird, didn't you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, imitation is the highest form of flattery.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Autumn is here. Time to rake the leaves!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Raking's for suckers, Monte. I've trained a team of field mice to retrieve all the leaves in the yard and dispose of them.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's amazing! How did you train them?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: With acorns.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How'd you get the acorns?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I spent the better part of the week raking and collecting them.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's not fair! Woot and Archie McPhee are giving away a bunch of awesome Halloween stuff this week in their Crap or Treat mini-sweepstakes, but you have to "Like" Woot's Facebook page to win any of it!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, you can't win anyway, because you work for Woot.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm just glad I found out about the Facebook thing before I turned in my resignation letter.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So, wait: you were willing to quit your job to try to win something in Crap or Treat, but you're not willing to join Facebook?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I'm banned from Facebook. I swear, you sell a few squid-gland supplements that send a couple of people to the hospital, and suddenly Facebook is judge, jury, and executioner.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I have decided to learn to play the wind chimes.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You mean the chimes? Because no one actually plays the wind chimes. They just get blown around by, well, the wind.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, if you can produce wind, you can play the wind chimes. And I think you and I both know I can produce a lot of wind.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You got me there.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Let's have a staring contest!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Umm, okay, I guess...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I win!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? But I didn't blink yet.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I was going for speed, not endurance.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So what are your thoughts on this whole NHL lock-out?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't know what everyone's getting so upset about. You get to stay up all night and hang out with your friends and play games and stuff.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're thinking of a lock-IN, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh. Well then maybe they should just try having one of those instead. I bet everyone would be a lot happier.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know what I hate? When you try to start a conversation with someone and they just sit there, staring at you silently.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OH, REAL FUNNY, MONTE!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's the worst kind of pizza?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You mean pizza restaurant, crust style, or recipe?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ERRRRRRRR. Wrong answer. All pizza is delicious.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't know about that. I'm not a fan of Hawaiian...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ERRRRRRR. DELICIOUS!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Come on, sign the petition! Every voice counts! Never doubt that a small group of committed citizens can change the world - indeed, it is the only thing that ever-

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, there is no way they're going to make pencil-break an official sport at the 2016 Olympics.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not with defeatist attitudes like yours.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, I'm really inspired by Kludge-O-Ween, the Woot-Off that explains how to hack together last-minute costumes from ordinary items!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I assume that has something to do with those duck carcasses piled on your desk.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yep! This year I'm going as a Chinatown butcher shop! How many of these do you think I can hang from my face?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm thinking about starting a new career as a DJ. My name: DJ Dijon.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: DJ Dijon?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah! And get this: all the songs I'll play will be about mustard!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Are there really that many songs about mustard?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's actually what I wanted to talk to you about: do you have time to write 300 songs this afternoon?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Can you believe it's already been a year since Home.Woot launched?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, that reminds me. I need to buy today's Home deal, the Woot logo pan by Lodge. I'm buying three, actually.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, it's a really solid pan and a cool piece of Woot merchandise.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's exactly what I'm hoping the collectors' market thinks in a few decades. I'm planning to retire on those babies.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm thinking of doing the Paleo thing.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh yeah? You getting into the whole CrossFit movement?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Heck no. I just thought it'd be cool to walk around eating giant turkey legs like a caveman.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm so excited. Movember is almost here!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, I'm thinking of doing it too. It's for a really good cause.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, any excuse to shave my entire body.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, actually, you're just supposed to grow a mustache.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Go big or go home, right?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's so hard for me to decide what to be for Halloween!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know what you mean! I'm torn between two options that would be equally great...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Heathcliff or Mr. Darcy? Me too!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uh, no. I meant The Iron Sheik or Sergeant Slaughter.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: At last! I found a pair of curly-toed wrestling boots to complete my Iron Sheik costume! Commence the AWESOMEST HALLOWEEN EVER!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do you really think it's wise in these times to dress up like a stereotyped caricature of a Muslim?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, the Iron Sheik is supposed to be Muslim?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, what do you think of my costume?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Let me see... the cape, the ruffles, the velvet... Prince?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, it's Heathcliff. You know, from Wuthering Heights.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whoa, you got ripped off. That's the worst cat costume I've ever seen.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, where's your basket?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Real men don't use a basket for Trick or Treating. They eat the candy as they get it!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're going to get sick eating that many sweets!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uh, duh! Why do you think I planned our route to end at the hospital?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, did you hear the news? Woot finally launched a mobile-optimized version of the site!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, just go to woot.com on an iOS or Android phone to see the all-new Woot Mobile!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wow! You know who's going to love this? My mom.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, does she do a lot of shopping on her smartphone?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No. My dad has been refusing to shave his beard until we release a mobile version, and it's like a foot-and-a-half long now.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ever read that book "Scary Stories to Tell in the Dark"?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, those illustrations were way too disturbing for my young eyes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Really? They cracked me up.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's not a normal response. I think there might be something wrong with you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You're just now figuring this out?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh. I am so sick of this whole Zombie Apocalypse mem.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think you mean "meme."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's what I said. Mem.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's actually pronounced "MEEM" not "MEM."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whatever. ToMEEMo, ToMEMo.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'd like to do something to help, but I'm so far away...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's very thoughtful of you, Mortimer. There are several organizations doing work on the ground that you could donate to...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: On the GROUND?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes, helping the victims of Hurricane Sandy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh no. I was talking about he victims of the Intergalactic Wars that I've reading about in this newspaper.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's a cheap sci-fi novel from the 70s, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I need to tell you something, but first you have to promise not to get mad.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? What now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just promise you won't get mad and I'll tell you.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No! I reserve the right to get mad if I have a good reason to! Now tell me whatever it is you did so I know why I'm getting mad!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: If that's your attiitude, forget it. I might as well just let you find out for yourself. Never mind.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: GGGGAAAAAHHHHHHHRRRRRRRRGGGGHHHH!!!!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's a great day, Monte!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Indeed it is, Mortimer! The citizens of the world's oldest living democracy are once again exercising their sacred and hard-won right to determine the course of the nation's-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Jeez, enough with the civics lecture. I just meant we wouldn't have to watch any more campaign commercials.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How about that yesterday, huh?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, wild. A lot of momentum swings, some things that I expected, some things that I didn't. Just absolutely wild.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, I had you wrong, Mortimer. I didn't think you followed politics so closely.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, politics? I was talking about the Nuggets game. Did I miss something?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You ever wonder how they get the peanut butter into the middle of a peanut butter cup?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No. I'm sure it's boring and industrial.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's the world like without magic, Monte? Is it as dark as it seems?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you know that narwhals are the unicorns of the sea?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I know that narwhals are real and unicorns are not.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So then I guess you could say that narwhals are the real narwhals of the sea.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You could.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm drinking a gallon of milk a day to bulk up.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's disgusting. And bulk up for what?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know...life.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Aren't you worried about the health implications?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's that, Monte? I couldn't hear that through all this bulk. Oh, no, sorry. That's just milk pouring out my ears.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Where'd you get that porridge, Monte?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That new Coen-Brother-themed place down the street, "True Grits." You want some?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No thanks. I prefer my usual place downtown, "No Country For Oatmeal."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Is that the one off 1st ave?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, that's "Intolerable Gruelty."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, it's not always easy to be so much smarter than everyone else.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, I'm sure it isn't easy to live with your level of intelligence.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It makes people uncomfortable. Nobody likes being reminded of their shortcomings. I don't try to make them feel inferior. I just can't help it.

lines.speaker: monte
