lines.text: Somehow I doubt that's your biggest problem.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I know. I'm also too good-looking. But I've learned to live with that.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: THEY DID IT! Almost every Shirt.Woot design is back in print and available on the All Designs page!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But we don't even wear shirts.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No. But if I was wearing one right now, it would say "I'm With Buzzkill."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm scared, Monte! I don't want to have to testify before a Congressional committee!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm sure the feeling is mutual. But what are you talking about?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: All this news about shirtless photos! I've been sending people shirtless photos for years! YEARS, Monte! If an FBI agent can't get away with it, what chance do I have?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But you're a monkey. Every photo you've ever taken is shirtless.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Remind me not to put you on the witness stand!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What if I wanna take off from work for a day, but keep workin' it, you know? Do I have to put in a special request?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No. I think that's allowed.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You THINK, Monte?! Because I need to KNOW! I take workin' it very seriously!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Can you believe that Hostess shut down? Breaks my heart. No more Swiss Cake Rolls!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Those are actually a Little Debbie product.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No more Nutty Bars! NO OATMEAL CREME PIES!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Also both Little Debbie.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Huh. Well then I guess there's no problem.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte: I'm dyeing.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh my gosh, Mortimer -- how?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: This Paas Easter egg kit.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? Is it toxic??

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: We seem to have had some kind of misunderstanding based on the homophonous nature of the English language.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know what our problem is? We never go out and do anything anymore. No wonder we're drifting apart.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, what do you want to do?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just, you know, get away from all this for a while. Get out there and really enjoy each other's company. Sort of a "date night".

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Is it OK if we don't call it that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You're ALREADY backing out of date night? Wow. It's even worse than I thought.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh... is my desk somewhere under all these stuffed animals?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just trying to make the office a little more cozy. Excuse me. Geez.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Then why don't you have any stuffed animals on YOUR desk?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, right! What do I look like, a six-year-old girl?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Alright, Mortimer - I'm going to prepare the ENTIRE meal tomorrow. All I need you to do is make mashed potatoes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Now, how do you define "mashed." Because I think of it more as a state-of-mind, you know?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Anything to get out of doing some work, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Pretty much.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: This Enduring Holidays pop-up shop is pretty great! I can spend all day shopping for holiday survival supplies rather than interacting with my insufferable family!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah... but don't you think it's sad that we, as a society, would rather bury our noses in our laptops than make a meaningful human connection?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, you sound just like my hippie brother-in-law. Hey, check this out, a foosball table!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, happy Thanksgiving anyway, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Happy Thanksgiving to you, too, old bu- whoa, is that a cake pop maker? Dude!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm glad I got up early and got my shopping done.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, did you buy some stuff from Woot's Enduring Holidays pop-up shop? Or their Cash Grab Black Friday event?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I went to the mall.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow! You're pretty brave to deal with those crowds.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What crowds? I just walked right in. By the way, if anyone shows up here asking about a brick through a window, you don't know me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I can't wait for the feast! You're bringing the turkey, right?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, Thanksgiving was two days ago.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So what, Monte? So what if I enjoy the company of with my family? So what if I enjoy filling my home with love, laughter and the spirit of togetherness.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're addicted to pumpkin pie, aren't you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: IT'S A DISEASE!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Did you remember that the white elephant gift exchange is this afternoon, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I sure did. I had to spend a fortune.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You shouldn't have -- the limit was $20!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, YOU tell me where you can get 400 pounds of animal feed and a dozen gallons of whitewash for that cheap.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: PENNY-PINCHING SERVOMOTOR ACTIVATED

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, I get it. "Cyber Monday", ha ha. You're a cheapskate robot.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: INITIATE DEAL-SEEKING CAPACITOR

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, you can find plenty of deals at Woot's Cyber Monday Cash Grab, not to mention our Enduring Holidays Pop-Up Shop.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: CONTRIVED, TRANSPARENT PROMOTIONAL BANTER DETECTED

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I am so glad Cyber Monday only comes once a year.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Woot's Enduring Holidays Pop-Up Shop just keeps going and going, huh?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, no. It ends at midnight Wednesday night.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: WHAT? Why didn't anybody tell me?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: We did. It says that all over the site. That's what a pop-up shop is.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Exactly - pop-UP! The name doesn't say anything about it popping DOWN!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Okay, I was thinking for Christmas, we cook a ham, then dress it in a little tuxedo and sunglasses and call it Gang-Ham Style!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, has anyone ever told you that you're everything that's wrong with the world?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm thinking of getting a vanity license plate. What about MORTIMERSAWESOMERACECAR? Or if that's taken, MORTIMERSAWESOMERACECAR2?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: They have character limits on those things. Like 6 or 7, I think.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Okay, Monte, here's one for you: FUNKILL. Or if that's taken, FUNKIL2.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I just don't think it's that impressive that someone could eat an entire wheel of cheese.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I guess it just depends on the size of the wheel.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But an entire whale of cheese, now that's a challenge!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Dream big, my friend.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm so hungry I could eat a horse.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What does that mean exactly? That you're so hungry you could eat something the size of a horse? Or that you're so hungry you could eat something that's deemed gross or taboo in our culture?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I dunno. Both, I guess? But now that I think about it, I wouldn't say no to a horse-sized pizza.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So, like, a pizza that is as big as a horse? Or a pizza that is big enough to feed a horse?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You're ruining this beautiful moment.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm afraid I must seek satisfaction for your effrontery, Monte.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: A duel! You questioned my application of mayonnaise to a hamburger, and I cannot let this public disgrace stand.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know that in a duel we try to kill each other, right Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I rescind my challenge. Good day, SIR.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, we're launching a second Woot pop-up shop with dozens of holiday deals? Isn't that a little excessive?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, I guess that's why we're calling it Jingle Binge. What's wrong with being a little excessive?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because 'tis the season to be a LOT excessive! We should have like fifty pop-up shops! Each with a thousand deals! If Santa's "Up On The House Top", Woot should be way OVER that housetop!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: There's always next year.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ughhh... mmmlegghh... man, this Jingle Binge pop-up shop is killing me... and it's only the second day... I don't know if I can keep up this lifestyle for an entire week...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, what? I know there are a lot of great deals, but I don't understand how they could be taking an actual physical toll on you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's not the deals per se... blegh... It's the buffalo-chili-Velveeta-anchovy dip I eat while I'm shopping online... I must have put away... a gallon of that stuff already... groan...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's actually not a mandatory part of the Jingle Binge experience.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, you keep your... ungh... holiday traditions and I'll... errrrpp... keep mine...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, I'm off to the mall to see Santa.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Aren't you a little old for that, Mortimer? I mean, sitting on Santa's lap and asking for presents is what children do.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I'm not going for that. I'm going to ask him why he didn't give me all that stuff I wanted when I was a kid. And I'm not settling for anything less than an itemized list of the exact naughty transgressions that impeded my ability to get what I desired.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Good to see you're handling this like an adult.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh man! My team, the Columbus Blue Jackets, is tied for the best record in the NHL!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, but isn't the NHL locked out right now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You better believe it is! Keep it up Bettman! This is the best we've done in years!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh my gahhhhh ... my tummy hurts soooo bad.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh oh. Maybe you have that stomach bug that's been going around.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, unless it hitched a ride on the five custard-filled donuts I just snarfed down, I'd say it's more likely I just overindulged.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Breakfast of champions.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's the name of that show that ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Family Matters.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No. It's the one about the ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Twin Peaks!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You don't even know what I'm ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: T.J. HOOKER!!!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I think I have Seasonal Affective Disorder, Monte. My joie de vivre is kaput.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm sorry, Mortimer. Have you tried anything to solve it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Solve the season? How? My knocking the Earth off its axis with an intense but brief electromagnetic pulse?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, like by getting one of those full-spectrum lamps.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's no fun. SIGH.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I can't stop following this Armagedd-Off 2012 Woot-Off!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, you're collecting the special trading cards hidden in certain Woot-Off sales, and trying to solve the puzzle?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I'm just buying stuff. I've gotten three of everything so far.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, Mortimer, can you really afford that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Who cares? The bill isn't due until after the end of the world. Hey, you want one of these nose-hair trimmers? I don't really need all three.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How are you doing on collecting those Armagedd-Off 2012 trading cards?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not good. I've called, like, ten collectibles shops looking for them and nobody knows what I'm talking about.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, well, they're not real trading cards. They're images hidden in select sales during the Woot-Off happening right now on the main Woot site.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Aw, man! Woot is always getting my hopes up with their stupid pranks! Who ever told those idiots they were funny? Oh, well, at least I've still got the Armagedd-Off 2012 movie to look forward to.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, you're not gonna like this either, but...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, glad that Woot-Offs over!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But it's not! It's still going! Don't you know what's going on on the site, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, to be honest, I never figured out if those were o's or zeros in the URL...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Is there an echo in here?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Is there an echo in here?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Shhh, Monte! Be quiet! I was trying to listen for an echo.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, I know, I was just playing along!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Echos aren't a game, Monte! They're serious!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: If you're not feeling well, maybe you should just go home.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Can't. I've got too much to do.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But you could be infecting everyone in the office, including me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Then you'll get a day off work!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think you're missing the point .

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You're welcome.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I just ate cookies for breakfast.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Breakfast is the most important meal of the day. You should probably eat something healthier.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, you're probably right. Next time I'll have pancakes.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: There you go.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: If you ask me, the world needs is some more rockin' Christmas music.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Nobody asked you, and for good reason.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And a one, and a two, and a...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: duhduhduhduh Can you feel that MANGER DANGER!?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I really wish I could ask the Woot writers a question.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You can! They'll be doing a live videochat thing at noon and 4 PM Central! It's called the Lamestreaming Media Live Wootcast. And they'll also be demonstrating the Star Wars lightsaber flashlights for sale on Kids.Woot.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But wait, it doesn't even start until noon? Never mind. It'll be too late by then.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why? What did you want to ask them?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: If they'd lend me ten bucks for lunch.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Holy cow! Scientists have found implements for cheese-making that are more than 7,000 years old!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uh, okay.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Isn't that amazing?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not really. I can make a room smell 7,000 years old just by cutting the cheese.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I have only myself to blame. I should know better than to bring up something like cheese with you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah. Too easy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why do they call Hollywood "Tinseltown"? Is it Christmas-y there all the time or something?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think it's just because it's so extravagant and shiny, you know?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No way! If that was it they woulda called it Glitterville. Or Light-Reflecting-Off-Glass City.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm going to close my eyes and just pretend it's a sunny spring day. That's how I'll get through this terrible weather.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's a good exercise, Mortimer. Visualize what you want for a few moments and you'll feel refreshed.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: A few moments?! Dude, I'm keeping my eyes closed UNTIL SPRING!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer! What is all this junk?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm emptying out my closet before the Apocalypse! Whatever happens today, I want to face it with a clean conscience!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Or at least a clean desk. But what are you going to do with all of this?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't know... give it away on Woot's Facebook page at random times throughout the day?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sounds cool. But if the world ends, you won't have time to send it to the winners.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But, officially, they'll own it. The afterlife people can't blame me for the mess.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So, you going to see Skyfall?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No. I'm still pretty cheesed they didn't pick me as the new Bond girl.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do you think it's because you're not actually a girl?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nah, I think they just didn't like the name.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Which was ... ?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ivanna Flickfleasanya.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How are you going to spend your Christmas bonus, Monte?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, WHAT?? You got a Christmas bonus? I was told nobody was receiving them this year!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The company doesn't GIVE you a bonus, Monte. You just strip the office of its copper wiring when everybody's gone, and sell it as scrap.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Let's just open all the presents right now! LET'S OPEN ALL THE PRESENTS OPEN ALL THE PRESENTS ALL THE PRESENTS!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm sorry, Mortimer. But we're going to wait until Christmas. Or at least a little later on Christmas Eve.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But why not right NOW? Who's gonna know? Let's just open the presents because WE SHOULD OPEN ALL THE PRESENTS!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No. The sooner we open the presents, the sooner your post-Christmas depression sets in. Let's wait. It's for your own good.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, really? Like when you wouldn't let me eat that meat until it was "fully cooked"? I suppose THAT was for my own good, too, huh?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So, uh, Merry Christmas, Mortimer, but... OK, like, um, here's the thing: I kind of, uh, don't technically exactly have your present just yet...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: WHAT? So... that means... you screwed up? I did the thoughtful thing for once and signed you up for a subscription to the Banana of the Month Club, and you didn't get me anything? This is you we're talking about! Monte! YOU screwed up and ruined Christmas! Not me!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah. I saw this really cool tree-swinging helmet online, and the thing said it would probably be here on time, but now it's late. I feel awful, Mortimer. I'm really sorry.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Don't be, pal. This feeling of moral superiority is gift enough. Merry Christmas, Monte.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Awww. Thanks, buddy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I do still want that helmet, though.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh! This is so lame! All I wanted for Christmas was a day off! And here we are at work.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But you did get a day off. Yesterday. Everyone got Christmas off.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh well, guess you're right. Looks like the Christmas genie fooled me again!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Christmas Genie?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Santa Claus, Christmas Genie, whatever you want to call him.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Counting down to the New Year! There's no law that says we have to start 10 seconds before the ball drops! But wait, where was I?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: 7,19... err, let's say 3, shall we?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wow! It felt like just a quick conversation, but we've actually been talking since last Thursday! Happy New Years, Monte!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Albert Finney, Richard Grieco, and William Shatner. Looker, Booker and Hooker. Get it? Get it?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? REALLY? No one under the age of forty is going to know what you're even talking about!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Listen, sometimes you've got to inspire people to do research. That's what being a role model is all about.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're not a role model, you work for an e-commerce site.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, Mortimer. I'll break through that tough unteachable exterior yet.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Knock knock.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh. Who's there?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Boo.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think I know this one. Boo who?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Boo Radley.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Nope, I guess I was expecting a punchline, not just the name of a character from an American literary classic.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm not too worried about this country falling off the "Fiscal Cliff."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? It's a pretty scary prospect.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm surprised you think so, since you also have a cape.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What does my cape have to do with the looming budget crisis?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because while the rest of the country plummets into the Fiscal Ravine, you and I shall soar away to safety.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Mortimer! Mortimer! You've got to help me!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Monte, calm down, what is it? What's wrong?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It just hit me! Tomorrow starts 2013! That means the song "Stacy's Mom" turns ten this year!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: This doesn't mean we have to get a cake, does it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Like I said last December, if I do it for Nelly, I've got to do it for everybody.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Happy New Year, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Um, Earth to Monte. New Years happened, like, a month ago!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Since it's been proven that Tuesdays don't exist, that means New Years happens way earlier than the so-called "calendar" would have you believe.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What do you mean, Tuesdays don't exist.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: DIDN'T YOU READ THOSE PAMPHLETS I GAVE YOU?!?!?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I think I'm going to start getting into shape.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's great, Mortimer! What are you thinking? Running? Biking? Swimming?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Those sound like pretty advanced shapes. I'll probably start with square and work my way up to circle.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm so glad I didn't make a New Year's resolution this year! Too much pressure!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But just yesterday you were talking about getting in...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm not going to think about yesterday, Monte. My New Year's resolution is to quit living in the past.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But you just said that you didn't...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What about "quit living in the past" don't you understand, Monte?!?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: On second thought, I think maybe I will make a New Year's Resolution this year.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh yeah? What's that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Diet. Exercise. You know, the usual.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But ... isn't that a pizza inside a hamburger inside a burrito you're eating right now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Meh. I'll start tomorrow.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Knock knock.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Who's there?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How am I supposed to know?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How am I supposed to know who?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't get it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: When's the Browns game on today?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ummm, Monte, it's the playoffs, and the Browns aren't in it this year. Or this decade.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Dang it! C'mon, Browns!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why are you a fan, Monte? You're not from the greater Cleveland metropolitan area.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The colors, Mortimer. They have my colors.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I only trust one source to give me the real news about the Consumer Electronics Show going on in Las Vegas this week.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You mean the Woot blog's CES coverage, now in its seventh year of capturing the weird and wondrous side of the massive industry hypefest?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I mean the Journal of Primate Dermatological Parasitology. I'm really hoping the rumors about Panasonic's new laser mite eradication system are true.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, I'm off to Las Vegas to press the flesh at the Consumer Electronics Show. You'll just have to follow along on the Woot blog's CES coverage.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, why do you get to go but not me? I thought we were a team!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I tried to get you on the list. But frankly, dude, some of the other Woot staffers thought you might be kind of a drag. You know, after hours.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Fine! I guess I won't set up that behind-the-scenes tour of the Museum of Arid Soil Geology!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, they might have a point.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, buddy! It's Mörtimer calling, from Vegas! As in Las Vegas, home of the 2013 Consumer Electronics Show!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, hi! Something sounds different about- hey, did you get an umlaut?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yep! After we saw Lemmy at CES, I guess I got a little carried away. But it suits me, wouldn't you say?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Totally. Maybe I'll get one, too. Just call me Mönte! Or should that be Montë? Ooh, or how about both, like Möntë? Is that too-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: STOP IT! LEAVE THE UMLAUT ALONE! YOU'RE SUCKING ALL THE COOL OUT OF IT!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I hope Mortimer's having a good time with the rest of the Woot crew out at the 2013 Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I hope he's meeting lots of nice Woot fans, and seeing all kinds of amazing gadgets, and having the time of his life in Sin City.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I hope he loves it so much, he wants to go next year, too.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I love the guy, but sometimes I need a week off.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uh, hi, Monte. This is Mortimer. Can you pick me up at the airport?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Back so soon? I thought you were supposed to be in Las Vegas for the final day of CES 2013.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: There was some unpleasantness.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, no. What did you do?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: All I'll say is, that robot threw the first punch.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You'll never believe what I heard this guy say in the lunchroom. Apparently, humans joke about kids running away to join the circus.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What? Everybody knows kids dream about running away from the circus to get a job in an office.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I know! It's like, just when I start to forget that I'm different around here, something reminds me that I don't fit in.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Awww. Does somebody need the nits picked from his fur?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Thanks, pal. At least I can count on you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, no problem. I was getting hungry anyway.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, Monte from Bakersfield, you're the next caller to enter the Reality Hole. Talk to me.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why are you talking like that? And I'm not from Bakersf-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: GIVE ME A BREAK, CALLER! That's exactly the kind of tired, stale thinking that's gotten this country into the mess it's in today!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, OK. You're playing talk radio.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you hear that story from your lamestream media puppetmasters? Don't buy it! They're just scared of the truth! They're scared of me! They're scared of the Reality Hole!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I can't say I blame them.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte, just an FYI, I'm going on sabbatical for a few minutes. I gotta use the restroom.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, the word "sabbatical" is usually used to describe longer periods of time than that.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Jeez, I can't believe I've wasted nearly half of my sabbatical here arguing with you! Ugh!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, you wanna join my rec league hockey team?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No way! Last time you invited me to join a rec league, it was just a bunch of people people taking turns hitting my car with a baseball bat.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That wasn't a rec league, Monte. It was a WRECK league. You gotta listen carefully before you agree to stuff.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, I just cannot figure out how to spell this word.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What word is that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'd love to tell you, but as I said, I cannot figure out how to spell it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Then why don't you just Google it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I would, but I don't know how to spell that word either!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Which cereal do you think makes the best cereal milk?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What is cereal milk?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know when you're eating, like, Cocoa Puffs and the milk is all brown and chocolatey at the end. And you drink it out of the bowl and it tastes just like chocolate milk.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh yeah. Well then, I guess I'd have to say ... Raisin Bran.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh. Why do I even bother?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte, do you want to participate in the office "Biggest Loser" competition?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sure! Sounds fun.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You win.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ha, Mortimer. Ha.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I notice you've been updating your LinkedIn page. Thinking about a career change?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uh, heh, no, of course not! What makes you say that?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: The fact that you list your occupation as "Boudoir Photographer".

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, look: keep this between us and I'll waive the sitting fee on your next session.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Here's my contribution to the '90s giveaways we're doing on Facebook today and tomorrow!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, five Beanie Babies and a Rusted Root CD?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, I thought that one song was catchy. And if I'd just sold those Beanie Babies at the right time-

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, I mean, don't you think we should give away things that are, you know, a little less crappy?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ...You remember who we work for, right?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Good point. Never mind.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I can't get that song by 4 Non Blondes, "What's Up?," out of my head. Except I keep putting in the lyrics from "Helter Skelter."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's an odd combination.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, but it works. Like, "When I get to the bottom I go back to the top."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Maybe a text-only comic is not the best place to try this...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why don't people drive their cars in reverse more often? Like, on the highway?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You mean, other than for the reason that they can't SEE that way?! Well, cars can't actually go very fast in reverse. See, the gear ratio is such that...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, I'd appreciate it if, for once, you wouldn't use logic and science against me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm thinking of getting my eyebrows waxed.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um ... but you don't have eyebrows.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You can be really hurtful sometimes, you know?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm sorry. If you had eyebrows, I'm sure they'd be expertly groomed.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's too late.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte, can I tell you a secret?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Of course you can, Pal.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, cool.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So ... what is it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I don't have one right now. It's just good to know I can talk to you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Happy Monkey Day!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? Since when is the 27th of January "Monkey Day"?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Since me! It's not fair that fathers, mothers, and independences get their own days, but not monkeys. So I have declared the fourth Sunday of January "Monkey Day"!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Did you clear it through the International Day Naming Council?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No. But they're probably closed for Monkey Day today, so I'm sure they'll cut me some slack.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You seem a little... down today, Monte.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, well, I guess I feel like I might be fighting off a cold or something.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nonsense! Your problem is, your brand is stale! The only person who can turn things around for you is a personal branding consultant!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sure, OK, but I don't know anybody like- oh, no. Wait a minute. No... you don't mean...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Tell me: what is the "Monte" brand's disruptive values-and-lifestyles proposition that wins the psychographical hierarchy-of-effects battle?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: At least now I know where my copy of MARKETING FOR MORONS went.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I have to tell you, my initial market research into your brand reveals some serious perception problems.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do tell.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Our focus groups did some wordfogging and valuestorming around the Monte brand, and terms like "uptight", "bland", "predictable", and "very stingy with his nachos" kept coming up.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, you're the only one who's ever asked me for nachos. Who was in these "focus groups", anyway?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm sorry, I can't reveal names. But the demographics skewed furry, highly intelligent, and irresistibly sexy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: If we're going to turn around the terminal decline of your personal brand, let's start at the foundation: your name.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's wrong with my name?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: "Monte". It just kind of sits there. No capital letters in the middle, no "L'il" or "Esq.", not even any Z's or X's.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So what's your idea? I should call myself "L'il MontExx, Esq."?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey! No fair peeking at my notes!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sorry, I'm not changing my name to something stupid because a self-appointed "brand advisor" says so. What else you got?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Tell me a little bit about how you draw your Alpha Influencers deeper into the Circles of Brand Engagement.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't think I do that.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What? You don't nurture brand advocates? You don't have any plan for turning customers into evangelists?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't even have customers.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wow. The rot runs even deeper than I thought.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No matter what a disaster your personal brand is, Monte, I can turn things around. Because this is my passion. I'm not one of those consultants who just wants to cash a paycheck. I'm in this because I have a deep commitment to-

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Whoa, whoa, paycheck? No way am I paying for this nonsense.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, then, forget it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What about all that talk about passion and commitment?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You can't buy a speedboat with passion.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Full disclosure. You ever do any of those Glamour Shots back in the day?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? No. Absolutely not.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Really? Why not? I did and it was a lot of fun!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Truth? I never did it because I look RIDICULOUS in feather boas.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So you excited for the Athletic Merriment today?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: The what?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, the Swine Dermis Extravaganza.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm not following.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh come on, Monte! I'm talking about the Mega Sport thing that everyone's talking about.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh! You mean the Sup[EDITED DUE TO COPYRIGHT INFRINGEMENT].

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You look a little down, pal.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, it's just a case of the end-of-the-season blues.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, until football starts up, you've got the rest of the basketball, and hockey's playing again, and baseball season isn't far-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Football? I'm talking about Moonshiners! Basic cable just won't be the same without Tickle, Jim Tom, and the rest of the gang. It's gonna be a long nine months, pal.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes, I'm sure it will be.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I got that song stuck in my head. You know, "Do you believe in miracles, where you're from, you text-y thing?"

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, Mortimer, I don't think the word is "text-y."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sure, not in the original. But in the version I recorded myself to appeal to the tweens, you better believe it says "text-y."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte, why is 6 afraid of 7?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Because 7 ate 9.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nooo. Because 7 will CUT you.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Have you heard about this whole Rent-A-Friend phenomenon?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, and I'm outraged. I don't believe in buying someone's affection.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Aren't you the guy who keeps a stocked candy jar at work so people will stop by and talk to you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's totally not even the same thing.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I finally cleaned my place this weekend.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Cleaning is a fool's errand. It's just going to get dirty again.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well...yes...but...what do you do??

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I move to a new apartment every time my old one gets messy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I figured out how I'm going to finally find acclaim and adoration as a pop star: find a boring little country where there's not much going on, and wow them with my authentic American pizzazz!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's like Frank Sinatra says, "If you can't make it here, you can maybe make it somewhere else."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Exactly! I've narrowed it down to Belize, Tonga, or Andorra. They'll be so grateful that a totally cool American like me noticed their Godforsaken little backwater, they'll buy any old slop I sell them!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You might want to keep that attitude to yourself.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Really? Those are lyrics from my debut single, "You People are Lucky to Have Me."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: If I could have one wish, it would be for a new way to enjoy fried potatoes.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What about French fries? Hash browns? Potato chips? Home fries? Tater tots? Potato pancakes? Seasoned fries? Waffle fries? Poutine? What else could possibly be left?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Unlike you, I refuse to accept any limits to what the fried potato is capable of.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm going to do it! I'm going to fulfill my dream as a child and become an astronaut!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's great, Mortimer. Just remember, it's going to take a lot of effort. You've got to stay strong and focused.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know what would be awesome? If there was a space-themed pizza place, and instead of having garlic knotts, they had "Astro-knotts." Get it?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You've already lost your focus, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My focus on what?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whoa, look Mortimer! My secret admirer sent me flowers!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: The card says, "Mortimer, pretend these are from a secret admirer. Love, Mortimer."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: He's revealed! And very attractive if I may say so myself!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Beware the ides of March, Monte.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, that's like an entire month away.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I just said to be aware. I didn't say "WATCH OUT THE IDES OF MARCH ARE RIGHT BEHIND YOU!" Jeez. Listen next time.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I like to ate ate ate aypples and baynaynays.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I like eat eat eat eepples and beeneenees.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I like to ite ite ite ipples and bininis.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I like to ote ote ote opples and bononos.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I like to ute ute ute upples and bununus.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That was fun.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wooooo! I'm excited to watch The Oscars today!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sorry to burst your bubble, Mortimer, but the Oscars are next Sunday.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, no, not the Academy Awards. "The Oscars" is an annual tradition in which I watch the Sylvester Stallone mafia comedy "Oscar" on repeat all day.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I've never seen it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You're not missing much.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's with this tape on the floor?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: In honor of President's Day, I've made our office into an oval. Make sure you don't step overstep the boundaries of the "Oval Office".

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, really? Well, let's just see what happens when- OW! What was that you threw at me? A rotten peach?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's right. Im-PEACH-ment. Get it?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So wait: you must have been saving that peach for weeks in preparation for this.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Impeachment is not something to be done in haste, Monte.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You gonna pick up anything in the Cubicle Fever Woot-Off to spruce up your desk?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know it! My productivity went up 375% after I got this desktop Zen rock garden.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, really?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Granted, most of those gains came in the areas of Pebble Grooming and Soulful Contemplation. But still, productivity is productivity.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Have you seen the pictures that Woot fans have submitted of their depressing cubicles to win a bundle of cubicle-enhancing stuff?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, no, I've been too busy trying to win actual stuff from Woot staffers' desks over on Facebook.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's all pretty sad, isn't it?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No sadder than having a blatantly contrived conversation for the sole purpose of directing readers to the events promoting our Cubicle Fever Woot-Off.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, duh, nothing's sadder than that.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh! I'm so hungover. Late night celebrating the second night after Presidents' Day last night.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I didn't realize that was a thing that people did.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wha??? Next you're going to tell me you've never celebrated Lincoln's Birthday Plus One Week!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The word of the day is "Eggcorn."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Can you please use that in a sentence?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I sat in the park and watched the squirrels gather eggcorns.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh I see what you did there.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You seem a little cranky today. Everything OK?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: meh

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, if there's anything you want to talk about ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Meh

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, I'll just leave you ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: MEH!!!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I need to come up with something to patent. Any ideas?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, what you need to do is sit down and come up with an idea for something that people need, but which doesn't exist yet.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Brilliant!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't know about that, Mortimer, but thank you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'll call it "A System For Coming Up With Inventions." I'll be rich!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's that you say? You wanna play Woot-opoly, the sensational new board game that crams the Woot experience into a box? Let's go!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, I didn't say anything, and I actually have a lot of work to do.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ooookaaaay... but one of the custom pewter pieces is a Woot monkey...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oooh! I get to be the monkey! Called it!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Fine. Then I get to be the Bag o' Crap!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh goody. Yet another way for me to lose out on the BOC.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Laptops. Laptops! Laptops LapTOPS LAPTOPS!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh-oh. Looks like our mind-blowing laptop sale on Sellout.Woot today has indeed blown Mortimer's mind. Good thing it won't last too long.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: LAPTOPS! LAPTOPS! Laptops laptops. Laptops! LAPTOPS!!!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Actually, I think he might be less obnoxious this way.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm going to become a motivational speaker. Here's what I'm calling my program: "The MonKEY to my Success!"

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's catchy. What's your advice?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Use puns to lure people in. Then, escape out a trapdoor. I'll be demonstrating both at the seminar.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, have you heard about this Common Year thing?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah! It's a really important cause. To do their part, Woot has a special shirt, a special plus sale, and some interesting and informative content.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So, it's not like The Year of the Rapper Common? Because if it isn't, then I have some CDs to return...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Did you know that all alligators are crocodiles, but that not all crocodiles are alligators?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I did.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh yeah?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sure. It's actually quite simple. Crocodiles and alligators are both part of the reptilian order Crocodylia. But they're part of different families, Crocodylidae and Alligatoridae respectively. Usually when people say "crocodile," they really mean "crocodilian." In total, there are some 23 unique species of crocodilians, including those in the lesser known family, Gavialidae.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, that's a surprise.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I read it on the internets.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: "I would walk 500 miles and I would walk 500 more ... "

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ... for a decent corndog right about now, yeah?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Totally.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What do you think about the debacle in D.C.?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's a very interesting question, Mortimer. We obviously need to reduce our deficit somehow, but these automatic cuts don't seem like the right way to do it. Our original spending plan seems better.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So you're saying that the original is always better than the sequester?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh. No.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Maybe we should just spend the sequester abroad.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't believe what I'm hearing! How can you say that? It's discrimination, plain and simple!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: All I mean is, I think the International Thumb-Wrestling Federation has good reasons to maintain a separate division for monkeys.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, what is this piece of construction paper doing here? It has "You Scored One-Love In My Heart" written on it in crayon.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've been looking all over for that! It's my birthday card for Russian tennis star Anna Chakvetadze, who turns 26 today!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, right, she was really good a few years ago. Whatever happened to her?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: She's currently ranked #302 in the world. Which means now I might have a shot.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't think there's a ranking number big enough for that.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, Monte, you're gonna have to take over for me. Tore my ACL. Out for the year.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, first of all, you're walking just fine. Second of all, this isn't a sport, so that doesn't get you out of work.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What is it with you and details these days? Jeez!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, you're never going to believe this! I saw a restaurant that advertised having "small plates." Seriously, they just ADMITTED it!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Actually, I can believe that. After all, some people like to have a few little things rather than one big pile of food. Nothing wrong with that.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Um, what?!? I'm sorry, Monte, but you're wrong. You're always supposed to eat as much as possible. That's why John Belt added those extra holes to the end of his invention, the belt.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: The guy who invented the belt wasn't named John Belt.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why does every conversation with you have to feel like a trivia game?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, I really need to schedule a grooming appointment.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: For your dog?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, for me! Things are getting a little unruly around the tail region.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: There are some things I would rather you never tell me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh believe me. There are plenty of things I don't tell you.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? Because it doesn't feel that way.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: UGH! I just stepped in a wet spot with my sock on.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Eww. That's the WORST.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know. I totally have the oogies now.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So why is there a wet spot on your floor?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I think it got there when I was wringing out my other sock.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, good, my business cards finally arrived!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I've never seen you give a business card away. You've still got five unopened boxes right there.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Those are obsolete! They have my old titles, like "Party Agent" and "Hug Doctor". I can't hand those out now that I'm a Certified Thrill Technician.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, that would be ridiculous.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm just glad our self-service business-card-ordering system makes it so easy to order new cards.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Enjoy it while you can. I have a feeling you won't be able to use it much longer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, good, my business cards finally arrived!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I've never seen you give a business card away. You've still got five unopened boxes right there.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Those are obsolete! They have my old titles, like "Party Agent" and "Hug Doctor". I can't hand those out now that I'm a Certified Thrill Technician.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, that would be ridiculous.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm just glad our self-service business-card-ordering system makes it so easy to order new cards.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Enjoy it while you can. I have a feeling you won't be able to use it much longer.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: EWWWW! Why did you show me that?!? There's nothing "heartwarming" about that picture!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You misheard me. I said "heart-wormy".

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: If I owned a Vietnamese Noodle shop, I'd call it Pho Money, Pho Problems.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's not how it's pronounced.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Um, I know how O's work, Monte.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm handing in my 17 year notice today, Monte. It's been great working with you.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? Why are you leaving in 17 years?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because if my calculations are correct, that's when the technology will be advanced enough for me to finally pursue my dream of opening a downloadable soda company.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Name one food that ranch dressing would not be good on. I bet you can't do it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ice cream.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh. Huh. OK then. Name one food that nacho cheese would not be good on.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ice cream.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You think you're so smart, don't you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't care what you say. It's weird.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Maybe she just really likes vanilla ice cream.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Thirty-one flavors, Monte! 3-1. And she gets ONE scoop of VANILLA in a BOWL. Not even a waffle cone or anything. Psssh.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, I see where you're coming from. But did you really have to break up with her?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: There was no future in it, Monte. We're just too different.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Happy St. Patrick's Day, Monte! What are your favorite Irish traditions?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, I love the corned beef and cabbage. And green always goes well with my fur.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My favorite tradition is the "Irish Goodbye."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Never heard of it. How does it work?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Aw, man. I'm feeling wrecked after the St. Patty's Day celebration yesterday.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, really? How much did you have to drink?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nothing. I'm just exhausted after chasing all those snakes out of the office. And I'm still not sure I got them all.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I thought I heard something moving around in my desk drawer.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Cool robes, dude.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Thanks! After years of hiding in the spiritual closet, I've decided to be open about my faith in the workplace!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So what level wizard are you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ha ha! We'll see who's laughing when you aren't invited to the Morquinox Festimer this week! You'll have to thank the Great Sky-Mortimer all by yourself!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: "Morquinox Festimer"? "The Great Sky-Mortimer"? Is your religion about worshipping yourself?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It seems like the least I can do for such an awesome guy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Dude! Duuuu-uuuuude! Remember the other day when you were telling me about those Harlem Shake videos?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, no. Me and my big mouth. I just had to open my big mouth and now you're going to be totally obnoxious about how we should make one and it's all my fault.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, but, see -

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And we'll do a lousy job at it and there'll be a lame thing on the Internet with my face in it weeks and weeks after it would have been even remotely interesting and everybody will see it and assume I think it's cool and again like I said it's all my fault.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Dude! I was gonna SAY I went looking for those but I got distracted by this video of a llama eating this guy's hat right off his head. AHAHAHA See? Watch this!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, OK. Whew! Saved by your short attention span.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know that book, "Eat, Love, Sleep"? I'm going to write a parody called, "Feet Rub, Cheap."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, that's not a book. It's called something else.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Huh, whoops. Say, Monte. Do me a favor?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Write a book called "Eat, Love, Sleep."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm thinking of going back to graduate school.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, you never graduated?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, grad school.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well I see why you didn't! You don't even use full words!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Saw that movie Zero Dark Thirty last night. And I gotta say, not impressed.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? Because it got great reviews, and it was nominated for a lot of awards.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nah. I thought it was boring. Thirty minutes of nothing but darkness. If I had known it was some artsy fartsy film, I wouldn't have bothered.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um ... pretty sure maybe you just forgot to hit "Play" and were watching a blank screen the whole time.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Huh. Well either way, I expected a lot more.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You and I are an endangered species, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You're telling me. Nobody else puts salt on their watermelon around here...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, I looked it up, and monkeys like us are on the brink of extinction.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh no! What can we do?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: We've got to start taking care of ourselves to ensure the species survives. First order of business: No more high-salt watermelons.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm telling you, I've GOT to KNOW which one of us is stronger! It's essential! I've GOT to! PLEASE can we arm-wrestle? PLEASE?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, fine, here. Ready? Go!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ungh... grrr... eh, never mind. I quit.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? But I thought you HAD to know.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: When I realized it wasn't going to be an effortless boost for my ego, I lost interest.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ugh! Not even the sweet 16 yet and my bracket is totally busted!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Gotta take that to a mechanic.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm not sure you were listening...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I'm pretty sure I wasn't.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What do you say me and you go toss the ol' ball around, eh son?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What are you...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: APRIL FOOLS! I'M NOT ACTUALLY YOUR DAD! I'M YOUR FRIEND, MORTIMER!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? It's not even April yet.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So you're saying I fooled you TWICE?!?!?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Does anybody ever buy scissors?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've got, like, four pairs of scissors here and I've never bought one.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, those are mine! I guess you don't have to buy scissors when you have a co-worker to steal them from.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oooh, who did you steal yours from?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man. I had no idea you were such a snorer.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I know. I've tried everything. Even those little strips you stick to the sides of your nose to open your nasal passages. Nothing works.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Maybe you should see a doctor about it. You're probably not getting very good sleep. It certainly kept me up all night!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, maybe. Hey wait a minute! What were you doing in my house?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wanna hear a joke?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh. Sure.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I have a blister on my foot.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How is that--

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Happy March Sincerity Day! (I actually have a blister on my foot. There is no joke.)

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why the long face, bud?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My test results came back positive.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? Test results? I didn't even know you were-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: POSITIVE you're gonna love the home-improvement bargains at Woot Tools & Garden, that is! Especially since I partially burned down that pergola in your yard.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I really hope at least part of what you just said is another April Fool's Day joke.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Um, uh, finding out is half the fun!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Where should I retire, assuming I'll be a billionaire by then?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's never too early to start- wait, what? A billionaire?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah. That's my goal, so that's what I'm basing all my plans on.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, you know, there might be some more short-term plans you should work out first.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You're right, you're right. I'll start with something more realistic: which NFL franchise should I buy?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just bought my summer ski pass! It was WAY cheaper than the winter ones!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But you can't ski in the summer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Typical Monte! Wants to give up at the first insurmountable obstacle!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, get in this barrel with me! We'll be a barrel of monkeys!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why would we do that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: For Halloween.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But it's April.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Preparedness, Monte. Preparedness.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's wrong? You look exhausted.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I saw the new Evil Dead movie last night. Couldn't sleep.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Pretty scary, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I wouldn't know. I fell for the old $0.25 more for the SUPER MEGA soda, so I was in and out of the bathroom all night.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I heard that the universe will keep expanding and expanding and expanding forever.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yikes. That's pretty heavy, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Not really. It means that nothing matters, so I can do whatever I want.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm not sure if that's --

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Now if you'll excuse me, I'm going to go watch G.I. Joe, drink some butterscotch, and cover myself with mink pelts.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's tragic to be so celebrated abroad, yet so unappreciated in my own country.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, what would you know about it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You should see all the emails I get from my Russian fans. They love me over there.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Can you read Russian?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No. But I've seen the pattern before. My fans in Nigeria have been trying to throw money at me for years.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whoa! How about that big game, huh?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, it was really something. Quite a game. All those, uh, points!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yes! The points that players scored! And then those times when the players prevented points from being scored. What a contrast! That's what I call a basketball game played by two competing teams.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You... you didn't watch it, did you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Whew. I thought I was the only one.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you know the human body is like 60 or 70 percent water? That means if there was a test, and the correct answers were stuff that's not water, I'd fail!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: First of all, you're not human. Second of all, it's a good thing they don't do that test.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But what if they do, Monte? WHAT IF THEY DO?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm gonna go to a clam bake this weekend. Interested?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nah. I don't go to those things. Scared of choking on a pearl.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think you're thinking of oysters.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Shells is shells, bro.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK. Let's put this argument to rest once and for all. Elephant ear or funnel cake?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, elephant ear for sure.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I dunno. I think I prefer funnel cake.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why's that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's an animal rights thing. I don't really want to get into it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Blah blah ...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ... if you keep doing that ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Blah blah blah blahblah blah ...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ... people are going to think we phoned this one in on purpose.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Blahblah blah blahblah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blahblah.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How much are you donating to the IRS this year, Monte?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, that's a pretty personal question, but -- wait, "donating"?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, you know, taxes are just like a fine art museum entrance fee. They're just a recommended donation.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's that sound? Are those helicopters?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Call me "Montgomery" for the next few days while the IRS blows over, OK?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, I'm updating my resume. Which sounds better: Book User for Various Used Bookstores or Scuffing Specialist for Used Bookstores Nationwide?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, resumes usually only include real work experience, not random stuff that you just do.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Thanks for the tip, Monte. Now, how would I sum up my role in this conversation? Information Receiver at Friendship sound okay?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh, I hate Mondays.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But it's Tuesday! You made it through!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know. I'm talking about NEXT Monday. It's right around the corner. I'm gonna be soooooo tired.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Here's my idea: we open a restaurant that sells weird stuff that foodies would want to try. Our signature dish: refried Mexican jumping beans.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, I don't know, Mortimer. Those things have larvae inside. In fact, the "jumping" is caused by the larvae spasming in an attempt to roll their beans to a cooler environment.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So you're saying we'd have to have a vegetarian option?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've decided I need to start acting more professional at work. And that means using more professional language.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think that's a great idea, Mortimer. Act like a professional and you'll feel like a professional.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I guess. It's kinda stressing me out, honestly! Is work over yet? I'm feeling mega overwhelmed by this new behavior.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, that didn't sound very professional...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh. You're right. How about this: what's the ETA on the EOD?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Dude. Have you seen the First Aid cabinet in the kitchen? It has LITERALLY everything in it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't think you mean "literally" literally.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yes I do! You name it, it's in there. Eye drops. Aspirin. Cough syrup.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How about unicorns? Are there unicorns in the First Aid cabinet?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I bet if you drank enough of that cough syrup there would be.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I spy with my little eye something that is ... weird.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Weird? You can't use "weird." You have to use something more concrete like "brown" or "furry."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK fine. I spy with my little eye something that is brown, furry and also weird.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: :::sigh::: You're talking about me, aren't you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Dang! You're good at this.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How's your fantasy-fantasy baseball team doing, Monte?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, Giancarlo Stanton has been a bust, but -- wait, "fantasy-fantasy"?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, you know, where you build a team of fantasy baseball players, and your performance is based on how THEY perform in their fantasy baseball leagues.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How is it that I always end our conversations with a headache, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm thinking about growing a mustache, you know, since mustaches are kinda cool now.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But you've always had hair on your upper lip. So you've always had a mustache, sort of.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh gross! Even when it wasn't cool? WHY DIDN'T ANYONE TELL ME?! Ugh! No wonder Heather Locklear never answered those emails!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oooh! This Woot-Off, we're playing Crapword! I can't wait to use my crossword skills to nab a Bag o' Crap!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Don't, Mortimer! Crapword is for customers, not staff.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And that's why I'm submitting my resignation, effective immediately. Hey, what's an eight-letter word meaning "foolhardy, reckless, impulsive"?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm looking at it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So how's Crapword going? Nab yourself a Bag o' Crap yet?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No. Not yet. But I'm still glad I quit my job at Woot just so I'd be eligible to play. I regret nothing. Everything's fine, OK? Everything's fine! So just get off my back and stop interfering in my life!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, but when your back was turned the other day, I deleted your resignation email from your Outbox. As far as Woot is concerned, you never quit.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Thank you for interfering in my life. You're the best friend I've ever had.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And the best co-worker you still have.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What are you up to tonight?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nah, I signed up for a 5k this weekend, so I'm getting started on that.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Getting started? You mean "training"?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No! I mean getting started! I'm going to run a little bit every night for the rest of this week, so that come race day, I'll only have a few steps left to do!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're not allowed to do that, Mortimer. You've gotta run the whole thing in one go.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh! What has happened to this country? You get NOTHING for showing initiative and working ahead anymore!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Happy Sexy Arbor Day, Monte!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? Sexy Arbor Day? Who calls it "Sexy Arbor Day"?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: We do! Woot.com!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I gotta start looking for a new job...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So, what do you think of my new cat shirt?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's good. But not as good as your Oprah shirt.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How do you think it compares to, say, my Great Wall of China shirt?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, WAY better. Cat shirt trumps Great Wall of China shirt every time.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte! Monte! I can't believe it! I won the lottery!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Holy cow! Congratulations, Mortimer. Now, the first thing you're going to want to do is take remaining money after tax, and invest it in --

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: More lottery tickets?? That way I can multiply my millions!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, that's the worst --

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Too late. Wish me luck!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hmmm.... Braunschweiger or Jellied Eel?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, no thanks. I'm not hungry.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, no. Which new potato chip flavor should I vote for?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Those are potato chip flavors? That's gross.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: We're talking about mass-produced slices of fried potato coated in salt, grease, and chemical flavoring. "Gross" isn't a criticism, it's a selling point.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's that new movie based on that Hemingway book?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think you're probably thinking of "The Great Gatsby" but that's by-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: -yeah, Fitzgerald. I DID go to high school, you know. But I'm talking about that other movie, with the guy who overcomes great trouble to defeat a sea creature and win back his worth as a man.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well that's the plot of The Old Man And The Sea, I guess, but... wait... are you talking about Pacific Rim?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: YES! Boy, nobody can write giant robot movies like the ol' Wax Puppy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm so psyched the NHL Playoffs are underway! I love hockey! It's my favorite sport! Like, ask me anything about the NHL! Anything!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Who plays goalie for the Blues?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: B.B. King!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I went to art school for four years, and no one will hire me as a graphic designer!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You don't have an art degree, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I know. But I DROVE to an art school every single day for four years. You'd think that would count for SOMETHING, right?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: As a matter of fact, I would not.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's the grossest thing you've ever eaten?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, I don't know. I'm a pretty adventurous eater. Probably bee larvae. It's a delicacy in China and Japan.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: SICK!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How about you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, I was going to say Nacho Cheese Corn Nuts, but forget it now.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm thinking about growing out my beard.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uhhh, Mortimer, you don't have a beard.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know -- that's why I have to grow it out.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, I mean, you're physically incapable of growing facial hair. You're covered in fur everywhere else on your body, why do you want a beard?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uniformity.

lines.speaker: mortimer
