lines.text: Hey, what do you think of this ad for my new business?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: "Hot monkey singles in your area want to talk now"? Let's say I called this number - who would I really be talking to?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Me. I'm the hot monkey single.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, OK. But it says "singles", plural.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's kinda what I want to talk to you about. How would you like to earn up to some dollars a day?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ...And so then after it happened a fifth time, I was like "Oh, I'm sorry, I didn't realize I was supposed to return CDs and DVDs in a different book drop!" The librarian and I laughed so hard over that one!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: AHAHA AHAHAHAHA HAAAA HAHA HOOO HA HOO HEH HEH AHHAHAHA

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, thanks, Mortimer, but even I didn't think it was that funny.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I wasn't laughing at the story. I was just picturing you wearing a muzzle.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, I don't know why everyone loves ice coffee so much. It just tastes like water to me!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's actually iced coffee, with a d.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I'm not talking about iced coffee; I'm talking about ice coffee which is when you use ground ice instead of ground coffee beans. Doesn't taste like anything!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've been watching the NBA playoffs, and boy are the games exciting! I'm thinking of getting in shape, maybe trying out for a team this summer.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: When's the last time you've even touched a basketball?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Last Saturday!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yep! I went to the mall and walked past the sporting goods store. They had a display of basketballs and stuff out in front. My shoulder grazed one of the balls. Boom!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: If you could smell like any pop star, who would it be?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, I dunno. Madonna? Or maybe Britney Spears. How about you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I was thinking the Dr. Pepper guy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Today I learned that I've been eating bananas the wrong way my entire life.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh yeah?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah. According to proper etiquette, you're supposed to cut off the ends, slice it lengthwise on both sides, peel off the top with your flatware, and then cut off bite-sized pieces with the side of your fork.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How have you been doing it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I usually just put the banana behind the tire of a car, and wait for the driver to back up until it shoots out of the peel.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I just calculated that if I keep saving, I'll be a millionaire by 2113!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Congratulations! But you're not going to be alive that long. And don't forget about Uncle Sam's share.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Don't worry. I'm pretty sure I can work my way around this whole "death and taxes" thing.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I just calculated that if I keep saving, I'll be a millionaire by 2113!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Congratulations! But you're not going to be alive that long. And don't forget about Uncle Sam's share.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Don't worry. I'm pretty sure I can work my way around this whole "death and taxes" thing.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, want to come to my outdoor movie party tonight?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sure thing! What's playing?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: "Frack This," an in-depth investigational documentary about the dangers of natural gas extraction.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ...ZZZzzzzzzz

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How did you fall asleep in two seconds?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Good afternoon, sir! Need your lawn moved?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, my lawn is at home, not at the office.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Duh! I know! But for a limited time, I'm selling these vouchers directly to my co-workers to get my name out there. $50 worth of lawnmowing for just $48! Redeemable anytime!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, but did you have to bring the lawnmower into the office?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, I tried selling these vouchers without it the other day, but I kept getting distracted and forgetting what I was doing.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, I got an idea: a pizza wrap! You take a whole pizza and roll it up! So it's like dough with pizza stuff in the middle!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Isn't that just a Stromboli?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, you're thinking of a kind of horn. And it's pronounced "trombone."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, did you hear? It's Woot writer Sean Adams's Birthday today!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Whoa! That's awesome! He's so cool! And handsome! And he has such big muscles!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And did you know he writes these little conversations on Thursdays?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow! That's so... wait a minute...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I SUDDENLY FEEL SO USED!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So this morning I woke up with a Backstreet Boys song in my head.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ugh. That sucks.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know. And I don't think I should have to suffer alone.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Don't you dare.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You are ... my fiiiiire. My one ... deeeesiiiiire.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I hate you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Unnnnnngggggg

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Did you eat too many donuts again?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Unnnnnngggggg

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm going to take that as a "yes."

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Unnnnnngggggg

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I quit.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, you can't quit...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know, I know. My services are invaluable. The company won't be the same without me. The median handsomeness of the office will drop precipitously...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ...Because we never actually paid you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's the problem? I got a suit pressed, a nice haircut, and some resume counseling, all for free! How is that a bad idea?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Because you're not actually unemployed! You're just pretending to be unemployed so you can get free stuff you haven't earned!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I resent the implication that I'm lazy. Pretending to be unemployed is hard work.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Woot-Off tonight! You excited?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Eh, Woot-Off's aren't what they used to be.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, yeah? Since when?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Since my millionaire uncle told me he'd have me arrested if I took out any more credit cards in his name.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Boy, some people, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know! Isn't family more important than a few tens of thousands of dollars?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I decree that we need a gong to sound whenever we make bold statements!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Umm... okay, I guess...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: See! That would've been a PERFECT TIME to sound a gong!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why do they charge money for sporting events? It's like, shouldn't that stuff just be free?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Because they have to pay the players and stadium workers and everyone.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: They pay the players? To PLAY a GAME?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, I could answer that question, but I feel like it would only give way to more questions, all of which you should know the answers to.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, what sound does a cow make?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Moooooooo!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's right. And what sound does a duck make?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Quack quack quack!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Very good! Now can you tell me what sound a monkey makes?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Like in what scenario? Could you be more specific?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know those photos of the dogs that they dress up like humans, and they're, like, fishing and whatnot?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Those really creep me out.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't know. I guess the idea of animals acting like humans is just really disturbing to me.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hmm.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm going to the big music festival this weekend, you coming?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, you mean the one where you spend $500 to camp in a crowded mud-pit to listen to 12 straight hours of live music, only 3 of which you're able to actually catch because you spend the rest of the time waiting in line for the bathrooms and overpriced food carts?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yup! So that's a soft yes?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, how's the grilling going? Wait, do you have the charcoal on top of the grill?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nope. Those are the brats. I like em well done.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Umm, okay. But they look totally burnt.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, you're right. They're probably ready. Here, help me light these buns on fire and then lunch is served!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, my plants keep dying. Like, they get all dry and crinkly.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're probably not watering them often enough.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No way! I water them several times a decade!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Man, it feels so nice to be right! Thanks for that, Mortimer!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Check it out, new snow shoes! It's always cheapest to buy this stuff out of season!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, I don't know if it's a good idea to wear them out of season, though, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What are you talking about? They're shoes! I'm breaking them in!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But snow shoes don't really work like that...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ha! Good one, Monte! Next thing you're going to tell me is that horse shoes are totally different than normal shoes too!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, you get the weirdest looks when you wear snow shoes in May.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, it is unconventional, and when you do anything unconventional in public, people are bound to look at you funny.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I was talking about my look. Like, I can't find any shorts that go well with snow shoes. Wait, people are looking at me funny?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What would you say if I told you I was going to move to the Moon and there's nothing you can do to stop me?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'd tell you that the Moon isn't really made of cheese.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh. Well what would you say if I told you I was going to move to Wisconsin, and there's nothing you can do to stop me?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's your turn to do the dishes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nuh uh. I did them last time.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No you didn't! I did!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, if you want to get technical ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sigh... Sunday. Boring, boring Sunday.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, the carpet really needs vacuuming. Or you could clean up all those cans and bottles you and your friends left on the lawn last night. You should probably scrub the barbecue grill before those bits of food start to mold. Or, hey, it's gonna be a big week at the office, maybe you could get a start on some of that work.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sigh... Sunday. Exhausting, exhausting Sunday.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Woot's looking good, but you know what would really tie our whole look together?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: A new Woot site featuring watches, sunglasses, and other accessories?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh. I was thinking more like an ocelot-print cravat. But that would be good, too.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Knock knock.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Who's there?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh wait, no. You're supposed to start.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Fine. Knock knock.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No no, say "A man walks into a bar..."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm done here.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Have we ever talked about hard pretzels vs. soft pretzels?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Okay, let me put that on the schedule. How about we discuss that Monday, September 9th...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, why can't we just talk about it now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Um, because I'm busy organizing conversation topics? Duh, Monte!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do you ever have that dream where you're eating sheet cake and then when you wake up, it turns out you're just eating your sheets?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Nope, never.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Okay, but if you did, what would that say about you?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It would say you have no idea what sheet cake tastes like.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte, help me settle an argument I'm having with a friend of mine.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Is it rude to poke your finger in a donut to see what's inside, and then not take that donut?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man! I really thought you'd be on my side.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why? I'm the friend you're having the argument with.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Where did you get that coat rack?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I found it in the trash.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, didn't you just throw away a coat rack?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah! So I was totally in the market for one. Can you believe the luck?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm halfway through this Dickens book and they still haven't started digging.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't remember one that involved digging...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: They just keep complaining about rich people and stuff. But the title is really misleading.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh no, I think I see where this is going...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why did he title it "Great Excavations?"

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, how are you... OH JEEZ! Are you okay? How'd you get trapped under that computer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I was trying to turn my desktop into a laptop and it backfired.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Turn your desktop into a laptop?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, by putting it in my lap. It didn't work, obviously. But the good news is I can sue the computer industry for confusing verbiage!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh man. I gotta start looking for summer jobs! Gosh, this is so stressful.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? No you don't. We have our jobs here for the summer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh! This place doesn't even give us summers off? How do they expect us to get work done when it's nice outside?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, but you were just talking about working for the summer...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Are you sure? Because that doesn't sound like me at all.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Why don't people carve jack-o-lanterns out of watermelons in the summer?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Too wet. They'd put out the candles.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So you're saying that pumpkins are SOO MUCH dryer, huh?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That is exactly what I'm saying.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, okay. Good to know.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Do you know any Russian?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Nyet.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, never mind ... hey wait a minute!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, I need to lay off the coffee. It's really making me feel weird.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How much did you drink?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I dunno. However much was in that old coffee can in the garage.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm pretty sure that was motor oil, not coffee.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I thought it tasted a little bitter.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've decided to start training for a marathon.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow! That's ambitious. Which one are you going to do?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Iron Man, I think.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Holy cow, Mortimer. That's not just a marathon, but also a 112-mile bike ride and a 2.4-mile swim! Are you sure your body can handle that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I meant I'm going to get myself prepared to watch all three Iron Man movies in one night. That other thing sounds sweaty.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, I've been on Sport.Woot all morning, taking advantage of their Woot Will Get You Ripped* In 5 Days program! Am I looking like an MMA fighter yet?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, no. What's the asterisk for, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Probably something awesome! Why else would they use an asterisk if not to indicate something being BETTER than you thought it would be?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wahooo! Go Blackhawks! Wahhhhhoooo!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, Mortimer. Are you really such a huge Blackhawks fan that you're still celebrating their victory a full three days after they won the Stanley Cup?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You bet! The Blackhawks are MY team! I've been a huge fan since way back on Monday!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, so you're a bandwagon fan...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, Monte. The Blackhawks aren't a band. They're a soccer team. Get it right!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Did you notice we forgot to chat yesterday?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, but it was intentional.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It was? How so?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I was having a moment of silence for bread.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Doing the gluten-free thing, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's so hot you could lay an egg on the sidewalk!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You mean "fry an egg," Mortimer

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Don't you dare fry my precious egg! That's my baby!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I can't believe it's July 1st already.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm glad you said that! You reminded me of something important I need to do.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Pay your rent?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, my lutefisk is finished soaking in lye. Can I have the key to your car trunk?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer! I just got your message about going to the hospital last night! Are you OK?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Fine, fine. And it gave me a great idea for a new product: edible phone cases!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Good, glad to hear it. But, uh, what about your trip to the hospital inspired that idea?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The fact that I was there because I ate my phone case. If only it had been actual food, right?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've reached out to Dwight Howard. Turns out he's not interested in signing with Woot. I wonder why...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Probably because Woot isn't a basketball team.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, he's a basketball player? I thought he was a model for shoulder implants!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, I'm so glad you're here! Can you help me unload all these fireworks from my trunk so we can set em off for the fourth of July?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Fireworks? Mortimer, this is just a palate of matchboxes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Uh, no! They're fireworks! Low-light, indoor-safe, candle-lighting fireworks! The guy selling them in the gas station parking lot told me so. Does that sound like the kind of guy who would lie to me?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you know that today is the day that the greatest number of pets are turned into animal shelters?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Is it because they got scared of last night's fireworks and ran away?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Huh. Maybe. I thought it was because they were all going on summer vacation at the same time.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You look great, Mortimer. Have you been going to the gym?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah. I've really been working on my French press.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You mean your bench press?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I mean French press. I like to watch people exercise while I have my morning coffee.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's no use talking to me until I've had my first cup of coffee.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sounds like you've got a bona fide chemical addiction. Did you know that --

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Are you speaking? I can't tell through this haze.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: DRINK SOME COFFEE SO I CAN EXPLAIN WHY YOU SHOULDN'T.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh. The older I get, the tougher these holiday weekends are to come back from.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Had a wild time for the 4th, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, was it the 4th this weekend? No, I'm still recovering from Memorial Day.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, come on! If a restaurant refuses to offer a certain item on the menu, it shouldn't be in their name!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I bet you're the only customer to ever make that mistake about Panda Express.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, why is there a mirror set up at my desk?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because it's good to be self-aware, Monte! By watching yourself in the mirror you get to know yourself better. And, by the way, what you see is just as important as what you don't see.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, is this all because you've been reading those vampire books?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: JUST TELL ME YOU SHOW UP, MONTE!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's your favorite kind of egg?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Like favorite way to prepare it? Scrambled, I guess.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I like mine poached.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh yeah?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, but don't tell anyone. I don't want to go to jail or get fined.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's that smell?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't smell anything.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Smells like teen angst and nag champa.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, OK. I bought some of that Drakkar Noir from Accessories.Woot.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I knew it!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ah, a summer Sunday. Nothing but hour after quiet, unhurried hour to relax, to unwind, to contemplate, to-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: PLAY WITH MY NEW JACKHAMMER! YOU WANT A TURN?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Baseball's biggest stars on one field tomorrow night! How exciting!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Huh, I would've thought you were one of those fans who wrote the game off as meaningless.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Who cares who wins? For me, it's all about the fashions!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you see this doggie DNA test on Home.Woot today? It's an outrage! It's not enough that people coddle the little beasts in their own homes, buy them special food, give them medals for trotting around an arena, clean up their droppings - now they get their own DNA tests, too? Where does it end?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, Mortimer, some people keep monkeys as pets.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ...Thanks for reminding me what a sick world we live in.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, have you submitted any suggestions for the Woot Libs Woot-Off? There's still time to get your very own word into an actual Woot product description!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: [Exclamation] yes, I have! Like [grossly exaggerated number] times! But those [plural insult] refuse to [verb] my brilliance! I swear to [deity], if this [noxious substance] goes on, I might just [rash, gratuitously violent act] myself and others!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think you might be getting a little too into the spirit of this.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What the [expletive] is that supposed to mean, you [deeply offensive personal insult]? Go [anatomically impossible act] yourself!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Look, Mortimer, just because something isn't explicitly prohibited doesn't mean you're supposed to do it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's what you say. I say, "free plastic utensils in the staff kitchen" means "free plastic utensils in the staff kitchen." Now are you going to help me back this truck up or what?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Come on, Monte! Everybody's doing it!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm just saying, if everyone jumped off a bridge, would you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Again? Yeah, totally.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I should be surprised, but I'm not.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte, what's happening?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think it's an earthquake! Quick! Get under that table and cover your head!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No I meant, like, what's going on ... what's up ... what's happening, you know?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh. Not much I guess ... oh wait! EARTHQUAKE!!!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sigh. My morale is pretty low, Monte.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How can that be? You never do any work in the first place.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Exactly, I'm having a hard time motivating myself to slack off.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hi, Monte! Hello! Good morning! Hi there!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ...So, I guess I'm not going to get away with not asking you about that hairnet you're wearing.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, THIS? I'm just getting into the spirit of Deal Cafeteria, the week-long buffet of back-to-school deals at Kids.Woot!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And I guess that explains this meatloaf smeared all over my desk chair?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, that was something else I was doing. Funny coincidence, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, I just thought, you know, "carte" was French for "cart", and a steam table is kind of like a cart, and -

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ahaha! I can't believe you thought "a la carte" meant "from the cart"! Maybe YOU should be going back to school with our weeklong Deal Cafeteria sale!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, making fun of me for a simple mistake is really mean. It's, it's... it's the kind of thing I would do!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ...Wow, that stings a lot more than it logically should.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte! We need to make Tetris: The Movie!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, why?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because it would the ultimate summer BLOCK BUSTER! Get it?!?!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I hate you.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ugh! Mortimer, your office is a mess. What is all this?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You mean my collection of CD jewel cases, Beanie Babies, and half-filled bags of potting soil?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes! You should sell some of this stuff over at the Sellout.Woot Woot-Off. It would fit in perfectly.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No way! This stuff is expected to depreciate in value by over 400% in the next 20 years.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You need to look up the definition of that word, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So ... hypothetically speaking, if someone were to borrow your car without asking, and then wreck it, would you be mad?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes! Mortimer! Of course I'd be mad!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, well it's a good thing it's only a hypothetical question then.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, can I please have my toothbrush back now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I didn't take your toothbrush. Maybe it was the ghost.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, I SAW you take it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wooooooo! WOOOOOOOOOO! Did you hear that? The ghost gets upset when you give me credit for his shenanigans.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I should probably complete my degree someday.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes you should! How many credits do you have left?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hmmmm, I'm not sure. How many gold stars make up a credit?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh. Mortimer, are you talking about finishing kindergarten?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whoa! I love your t-shirt!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Thanks! I got it from the Shirt.Woot Employee-Designed T-Shirt Contest!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've got to go buy it too!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You totally should! It's easily the best one in the whole collection! And check this out: it's designed by the same guy who wrote this monkey chat!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whoa! That must be why we love it so much!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes! Because we don't have any other choice!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, did you know that "panini" is actually a plural word?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, I did know that. It's "panino" if it's singular, right?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, you're supposed to say, 'I had pani-NO idea!'

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: We can't do these bits if you're not going to give me my part beforehand, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: This new Daft Punk song is catchy and all, but these guys gotta get a handle on their gambling addiction. "Stay up all night to get lucky"? C'mon, guys! It's not worth ruining your lives for!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Okay, first, that song came out months ago. Second, when they say, "get lucky," they don't mean gambling.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So... leprechaun hunting?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: On a scale of 1 to 10, how offended would you say you get?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: To answer that, I'm really gonna need more details. First of all, offended about what? I need a scenario to base my answer on. Secondly, how is the scale constructed? Does 1 represent not offended at all while 10 represents extremely offended, or vice versa?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm just going to put you down as an 11.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's your least favorite woman's name?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm not sure if I --

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's 'Amy', right? Doncha just hate Amys?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're doing that thing again where you assume that everybody has had exactly the same life experiences as yourself, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: First one to talk has to eat a pack of Necco Wafers!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You just talked, so does that --

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: -- you lose!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well good, because I happen to fancy Necco Wafers.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Aw jeez Monte, you're too lame to even prank.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Your momma's so dumb, when I said the college admissions board was scary, she asked if it had a nail sticking out of it!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I see you're getting into the spirit of Get Schooled, a whole week of funky fresh back-to-school deals at Sellout.Woot.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sure, let's go with that.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hmmm... I just have one question about the back-to-school deals in Woot's Get Schooled! event: why are home theater systems considered a back-to-school item?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The fact that you asked that answers another question: how come nobody ever wanted to hang out in your dorm room?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Do you have any threes?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Nope! Go fish!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, where are the cards?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: In the pond! Grab your fishing reel!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, Monte, if you agree to play Extreme Go Fish, you gotta be ready to get your feet wet!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Just sent a cease-and-desist to KRS-One! I'm tired of him using our site's name in that song! You know, "Woot! Woot! It's the sound of the police!"

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think it's more of a "Whoop! Whoop!" sound. Not to mention, that song came out in, like, 1993, way before Woot was even a website.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So wait, KRS-One founded Woot?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wanna play Paper, Rock, Scissors?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sure. Shoot on three. One ... two ... THREE.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ha! I win!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How do you figure? Paper COVERS rock.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, but my rock is flint. Flint makes fire, and fire BURNS paper. So I win.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, want to grab some lunch?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: || I AM OUT OF THE OFFICE THE WEEK OF AUGUST 11TH. PLEASE DIRECT ANY ISSUES TO MONTE, MY SUBORDINATE ||

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Was that an out-of-office reply?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: || I AM OUT OF THE OFFICE THE WEEK OF AUGUST 11TH. PLEASE DIRECT ANY ISSUES TO MONTE, MY SUBORDINATE ||

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Please, Monte? I promise I'll give it right back.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, there is no WAY I'm loaning you my credit card. Not with these incredible computer deals all week on Sellout.Woot. My card is going to stay right here in my... hey, where's my wallet?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, I was trying to do this the nice way. Whatever happens from here is on your head.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, I just remember: I had a dream about you last night!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really. Wow.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, I was riding this bicycle in, like, a park, but the park was actually my house, you know? It was like my house was the park. And I remember there were these three pizzas there, so I was like, yay, pizza, but then when I opened the boxes there was just this paper in there. I looked at the paper and it was sheet music. So I was like, aw, man, I wanted pizza, but then somebody was like "The pizza's on the piano" and I go "Where's the piano?" Or I tried to, but I couldn't get the words out. You know how that is in a dream, when you want to talk but you can't? So I'm looking for this piano when I see all these boxes of old comics, like from the '60s or whatever-

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: OK, OK, so where do I come in?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, at one point you started telling me about a really boring dream you'd had, and you just wouldn't shut up! That's so like you!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I made tortilla soup last night. It turned out really good!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Um, sorry to break this to you, Monte, but tortilla soup is a scientific impossibility. Tortilla is solid. Soup is liquid.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: By that logic, chicken noodle soup is also a scientific impossibility.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: FINALLY! SOMEONE WHO AGREES WITH ME!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Call me crazy, but...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You're crazy!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I wasn't done.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, it did seem weird, but then again, you're crazy, so...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I didn't say I'm crazy. I said, "call me crazy." It's a phrase people use.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh! I gotta stop having these conversations with crazies! They don't make any sense!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Who do you think should be the next Batman?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, I don't know. I heard they were maybe thinking about that guy from True Blood, or even Ryan Gosling. Who you do thi ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Russell Brand.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You really think that R ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Russell Brand. Yes. Or maybe Danny DeVito. I'm torn.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How do you prefer your calamari?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Like, fried or steamed, you mean? Fried, of course. How about you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I prefer my calamari eaten by someone else. BLEH.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Have you ever consumed Tristram Shandy?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I sure have! It's one of my favorite books -- or dare I say -- at risk of sounding highfalutin -- that given (and indeed because of) -- one of my favorite human works.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wow. I had no idea you would enjoy a combination of Keystone Light and Orange Slice.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Aw, man, I am gonna ace the Woot Ultimate Challenge! It's right in my wheelhouse!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So you think you're pretty good at watching Woot staffers compete doing stupid things, then shooting a video of yourself doing those things, and being one of the first five to post that video to the discussion thread every day for a chance at a Bag of Crap?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Jeez, is THAT what you're supposed to do? I stopped reading the announcement after the part about watching videos.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So just hypothetically, you'd be pretty upset if I'd accidentally let 100 scorpions loose in your car, right?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes. Extremely upset, yes.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So compared to that, like, one scorpion would be no big deal.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think it's time to revisit our carpooling arrangement.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Late for work again, huh?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes. I had to go to the hospital this morning after I found that scorpion you lost in my car.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, no! That's terrible!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes, it was the most painful thing I've ever -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Please tell me Colonel Stingz is gonna be OK!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, Mortimer did you say you got a Cadillac?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I said I had a caddy lack! Like, they refused to give me a caddy at the golf course!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You certainly have a way with words, Mortimer. Not a good way, but a way.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Waa waa waa. Waa waa waa waa. Waa waa.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't understand what you're trying to say.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Waa waa ... waa waa waa. Waa waa waa waa. Waa waa.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, I just ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: WAA WAA WAA WAA WAA!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Are you yelling at me? It's coming across loud but not very clear.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man! I missed Ghost Shark!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're really upset about that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Heck yeah! I loved Sharknado.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Seriously. These shark movies are getting out of control. What's next ... Sharkquake? Sharknami? Sharkenstein?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I feel like maybe that last one is a little too expected.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, I don't want to talk about it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, I didn't say anything. I don't even know what you're talking about.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: More like what I'm NOT talking about. I just said I didn't want to talk about it. Will you please respect my privacy?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sure. Great.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, fine, jeez! If you're gonna be that way about it, if you just HAVE to know, I'll tell you! So what happened was, the other day my sister-in-law called me about these wet cardboard boxes I left in their bathtub. And do you believe she had the nerve to actually give me an ultimatum to come pick them up? After I spent half of my Saturday cleaning up after her incontinent parakeets? Well, I wasn't about to sit there and take that from her, of all people. So I said...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Can I please respect your privacy? Please?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well? What do you think? How is it? How does it taste?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's... uh, it's OK... the crust and the icing are kind of weird together...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Curses! Back to the drawing board!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Listen, Mortimer, you should really get some sleep.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No! I will not rest - I cannot rest - until I've succeeded! I will be the first chef to ever fuse cake and pie!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, Monte! Is it true what they've been saying? That the Shirt-Off is actually just a Woot-Off on Shirt.Woot?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, why? What did you think it was?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ummm... Nothing...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What the heck? Why did "It's Raining Men" just start blasting over the PA system?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't know! It wasn't me! Bye!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Got a business idea: a bumper sticker company that only makes really sad bumper stickers. We call it... BUMMER STICKERS!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why would anyone want a sad bumper sticker?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Let's not get ahead of ourselves with the planning here, Monte. Let's just start making these sad bumper stickers and we'll answer that later.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Did you take the required sexual harassment training course?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't know what you're talking ab ... why ... why are you asking?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No seriously, you need to take it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did ... did someone say something, or ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: IT WAS AN HONEST MISTAKE!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You going to the Shirt.Woot party tomorrow?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh. I didn't know there was a party. Guess I wasn't invited.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well this is awkward.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Welcome to September, Monte. PREPARE TO DIE!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait -- wha -- what's happening in September?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh nothing in particular. It's just important to meditate on your mortality every once in a while.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What is all this mess?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: As a salute to American workers, I decided to spend Labor Day building my own car.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I've never been inside an auto plant, but I don't think they use this much marshmallow fluff and Styrofoam.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: My therapist says I need to stop berating myself for mistakes I made in the past.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I agree. I never feel sorry for anything I've done, and look how happy I am.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't think that's exactly what he meant...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: WELL I'M NOT TAKING IT BACK!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte, have you seen my deodorant? I left it in the freezer.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, I threw it away. Why did you put it in the freezer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because I bought the gel by accident but really I wanted the solid. DUH!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ugh! This coffee tastes terrible!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What? I made it myself! Ground the beans and everything!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, what kind of coffee beans did you use?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait... do they have to be COFFEE beans?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: How much money would you give me to eat this whole jar of jalapenos in under 30 seconds?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Zero. I would give you zero dollars to do that.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: OK, how much would you give me to NOT eat this jar of jalapenos in under 30 seconds?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hmm. Well, I guess I'd have to say ... also zero dollars.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't get the whole "no shirt, no shoes, no service" thing.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I don't know for sure, but I think maybe it's a health code issue.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Have you SEEN me after work? I'm pretty sure it'd be more of a health hazard if I leave my clothes on.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Brain...isn't...working...super...help...Monte...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Are you OK? What happened?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Bad...vienna...sausages...hurt...Mortimer...brain

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, these aren't Vienna sausages, they're horse tranquilizers!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Mortimer...forever...sleep...now

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, this isn't the bagel I ordered. What kind of bagel is this? What am I supposed to do, Mortimer? WHAT AM I SUPPOSED TO -

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Calm down! It's OK! In fact, I think it's good for you to be forced to embrace a little uncertainty in your life. Like that time I mixed up your rubber bands and your paper clips.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That bad?!? I'm still finding stray paper clips in my rubber band cup! How could you do that to me? WHY ARE YOU ALWAYS TRYING TO RUIN MY PERFECT LIFE?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You're going to find them somewhere even less desirable if you don't shut up and eat that bagel.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: "Would I rather punch a chipmunk or a rabbit?" What kind of question is that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's just a fun hypothetical thing. So which would you?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I've really never given any thought to punching any cute woodland animals.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: C'mon, it's not supposed to be some huge deal. You're overthinking it. Just answer: which would you rather punch, a chipmunk or a rabbit?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh... a chipmunk?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: YOU WOULD PUNCH A CHIPMUNK? Wow. You think you know a guy...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, I'm brewing fresh bubble tea. You want some?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, not if it's just tea that you've blown bubbles into with a straw.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So, that's a no then.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I went to a bar last night where they said they had a "rotating tap," but get this: all the taps were stock still, not rotating at all.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, that's because "rotating tap" doesn't mean the tap physically rotates. It means that there's a different beer on that tap every week or month or whatever.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What?! Really?! I better go back to that bar and confirm that you're correct this very instant!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But it's noon. On a Thursday. And there's work to do.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Knowledge cares not about such petty concerns, Monte. To the bar!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So, I couldn't help but notice that SOMEONE forgot to include a monkey chat in yesterday's email.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You wouldn't talk to me because you were still mad that I wouldn't take swing dancing lessons with you. Remember?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I just remembered that I'm not talking to you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm thinking about getting a dog.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's exciting! What breeds are you thinking of?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Maybe a malamute or mastiff.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, those dogs would be five times your size!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well yeah, how else could I ride it to work?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What a Sunday! Did you see that hit that one guy laid on that quarterback? How about that catch - was that a thing of beauty or what? And all those upsets, man! Lotta unhappy losers in Vegas wondering where their money went this morning!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You didn't actually watch any football games, did you?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, but I'm pretty sure I got the gist of it.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I keep thinking it's Tuesday.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It is Tuesday.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You too, huh?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wahooo! Go Seahawks!!!!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, that games been over for a while!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: But we're trying to set the record! For loudest fans!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: They did set the record!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's some bunk, man! Loudness isn't a one night thing. It's a lifestyle!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What kind of sandwich are you eating, Mortimer? It's so crunchy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Onion and breath mint! All that onion taste! None of that onion breath!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That sounds disgusting.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well then, I guess than you think efficiency is disgusting!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's your biggest pet peeve?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Talking animals.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah. Like in movies, or mascots in commercials. It's just unnatural. Really off-putting.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I guess this is a little awkward, huh?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah. Just a little.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Maybe you should lock the door from now on. Just a suggestion.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: MAYBE YOU SHOULD LEARN TO KNOCK.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, what's it gonna take to get you to sign up for my softball team?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Nothing. I'm not signing up, Mortimer. Please stop asking me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ah, playing hardball, I see.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: And this year's Morty Award for Best Performance by a Male goes to... MONTE! Congratulations, Monte!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, thanks, Mortimer. It's nice to be recognized once in a while. But what are the Morty Awards?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The nation's top accolades for charismatically impaired individuals.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, then I don't accept.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Too late! And you're already an early favorite for next year!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, look, here's somebody excited about the Woot-Off! Woot member GyorgisPizza says "YES!!! The Woot-Off is the high point of my month!!!"

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, Gyorgi's Pizza is the place the warehouse guys always order dinner from when they pull late nights.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh. Well, uh, maybe it's... a different Gyorgi's Pizza? Who's actually a Woot customer who's really excited about buying a ton of stuff from the Woot-Off?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sure, I can agree to believe that if you can.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've got a lot of emails to write. Job stuff.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're applying to new jobs?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No. LinkedIn emailed me with some positions I might be interested in. I gotta let them know thanks but I'm happy where I am.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, that's an automated email, Mortimer. you don't have to reply.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sure I do! Now, should I address my message to Linked or, should I go more formal and use Mr. In?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, did you bring stuff from home for Woot's Bags of Our Crap sale today?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, Bags of Our Crap is a sale of our STUFF FROM HOME?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah. What did you think it is?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, nothing. Hey, you wanna trade lunches? I brought prunes and coffee.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte. Monte. Monte. MONTE!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: WHAT?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Have you played Cookie Clicker yet?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: DANGIT, MORTIMER! YOU JUST MADE ME MISS A GOLDEN COOKIE!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So that's a yes, then.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer. Do you know if ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Please press or say the first three letters of the name of the person you're trying to reach.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? I just want to ask you about the ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Please press or say the first three letters of the name of the person you're trying to reach.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: :::SIGH::: Fine. M-O-R ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That extension is busy. Please try again later. Good bye.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey Mortimer, did you watch --

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: DON'T SAY ANYTHING

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm not going to spoil anything --

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: LA LA LA I'VE NEVER WATCHED TV BEFORE AND I DON'T WANT YOU TO RUIN THE CONCEPT

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Whoa! BEST SERIES FINALE EVER! So many jaw-dropping surprises, but it all made total sense!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, I can't remember when I've ever been so gripped by a TV show.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I know! The way the main characters all ended up in prison, infamous and hated around the world - just because Kramer started jumping around on that plane! Amazing!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Uh, isn't that the Seinfeld finale?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, I just finished watching the DVDs. What show are you talking about?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Tired of "Rocktober"? Then take my hand and join me for "Hocktober"! I'll celebrate the season with visits to 31 pawnshops in 31 days!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That sounds really depressing. Why would you want to do that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It'll be fun! Kitschy! Ironic!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Also, I made some really bad spending decisions over the last few months. Ol' Mortimer's gotta try to get his hands on a little cash.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh! The mechanic says it's going to cost TONS of money to fix my car.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Jeez, what happened?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I don't know. Something with the oil intake system.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Did you use the wrong oil?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, that's just the thing! I only ever buy the best stuff I can find. I'm talking extra virgin, infused with garlic and herbs, the works!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm thinking of going back to school for art history.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, they still don't recognize pizza as a study-able art.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: BUT IT'S SO BEAUTIFUL!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What do you think about company sponsored Happy Hours?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think you should proceed with caution.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah. Me too.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Heck yeah! If you just run in there arms a-flailin', you might spill your drink, and that would be a real shame.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I have to pee.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You should probably go.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Are ... are you going, like, right now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You specifically said that I should go!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: See ya, Monte! I'm headed for the land of the rising sun.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You're taking a trip to Japan? Wonderful! Make sure to try fresh yakisoba --

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, no. I'm going to walk in the direction that the sun rises until I find where it goes at night. Wish me luck!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sigh. Good luck.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've done it! Presenting the world's first zero-calorie donut! Enjoy!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Thanks, Mortimer, I'll give it a- wait a minute. Is this just tissue paper and sawdust twisted into a ring shape?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I didn't say "the world's first edible zero-calorie donut."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, don't forget to watch Late Morning, the new Woot talk show, today at 1 PM Central/10 AM Pacific right there on the Woot front page!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Are you kidding me? After they kicked me off the show during rehearsals? No way.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why did you get kicked off the show?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ...Let's just say "creative differences".

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, so that's what all those fire trucks were doing here yesterday...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I can't believe I've had to work through all these major holidays!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, Monday was Taco Tuesday Eve, Tuesday was Taco Tuesday, and today is the Post Taco Tuesday Day of Rest and Digestion.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh yeah! Last day of the week! Gettin' ready for the weekend!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's only Thursday, Mortimer. One more day.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Friday is a mindset, dude!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, it's a day on the calendar.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Look, Monte, I'd love to sit here and argue with ya, but I gotta get all my stuff wrapped up before the weekend, okay?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: If someone told you that you smelled like soup, would you consider it an insult?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes. Definitely.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I dunno. I think it really depends on the soup. Are we talking chicken and rice or chowder? Because chowder is delicious.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: New England or Manhattan style?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ooh, I never thought of that. That jerk!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Got any plans for the weekend?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, I was thinking of going pumpkin picking.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That is horrible. How could you even think of doing such a thing? Stealing that poor kid's pumpkin off his front porch ...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, no! Of course I'd never do that. I'll just go to the pumpkin patch and take one from there.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: YOU MONSTER.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer! You left the sink full of dirty dishes

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well yeah: Why would I wash the dishes?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So ... so they are clean and you can use them again!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You mean make them dirty again? No thanks.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sometimes your idiocy is profoundly wise, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, today's the first day of Pet Week over on Sellout.Woot.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I know. It raises unsettling questions about where you and I fall on that spectrum. Are we de facto humans? Pets? Something else?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Doesn't matter to me, as long as I can pee wherever I want.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's Woot-Off time again! Boy, is my F5 key going to get a workout!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Really? You work here. You can just look at the queue and see what's coming up for sale.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, I didn't mean I'd be refreshing Woot. I stayed up all night setting up the Woot-Off, so I have my F5 key hooked up to an intravenous caffeine drip.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey Monte, will you read me a bedtime story?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's like 11:30am.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Good call. Hey Monte, will you read me a powernap-at-my-desktime story?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, help me out here: is "farce" just old-timey speak for "fart"?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, if you read a book every now and then, you might know the answers to questions like these.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Read a book?! HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA! Oh, Monte! You just made me laugh so hard I farced!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What state fair food do you like better? Elephant ears or funnel cake?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh, funnel cake. No contest.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think I'm more of an elephant ear kinda guy.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, well, some of us have morals. Those poor elephants!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I love you, Mortimer.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh. Well thanks, Monte. Everything OK?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah. It's just that, you're a real good pal, and I just don't tell you that enough.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's real nice, buddy. Thank you. So ... can I have your vintage Woot shirt collection when you're gone?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? No! Jeez, Mortimer, I'm not dying!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Monte, I'm thinking about starting a 401k.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That's great news, Mortimer! With tax benefits and employee matching, a 401(k) can become very lucrative over the long term.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Long term?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yes, you have to slowly save for retirement.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: The 401k plan I'm going to do involves winning $401,000 on scratch tickets. But that other one sounds nice too.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What do you think of my costume?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hmm... football helmet, prison jumpsuit, bloody glove... O.J. Simpson?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You got it!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Is this for Awful Costumes of Awful People, where we're giving away bad costumes of bad human beings all week on the Woot Facebook page?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, we're doing what, now?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Smell that in the air? It's the smell of renewal, of rebirth, of awakening, of the circle of life making another slow turn toward eternity.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, most people think of fall as a time when things start dying. I have to say, that's a really poetic, philosophical way to look at it, to accept that death is as essential a stage of life as any other-

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I was talking about the SCENT OF SPRINGTIME candle I just lit to brighten things up in here. Fall is creepy and gross.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Oh man, how good was that World Series game last night? Go Seattle Mariners! It's like we can't lose!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? Seattle didn't even make the playoffs.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Exactly! That means there's no chance of us possibly losing! Go Mariners!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Got your Halloween costume yet?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yep. I'm going as you!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh. Well that's kind of flattering ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Only I'm going to wear this hat.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: So ... basically you're just going as you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's what I said!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: This is delicious! I should record it in "CakeBook."

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: It's a social media app that keeps track of all the cake you eat. You should friend me so you get alerts any time I eat cake.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Come on, Mortimer, how often do you --

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Thrice daily.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh ...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm involved in a never-ending, circular argument on Facebook wherein the other person refuses to hear reason and consistently contradicts himself. It's exhausting.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Then why don't you just stop posting?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: BECAUSE I HAVE TO WIN!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Inhale my rich musk while you can, Monte, because as soon as my script makes its way around, I'll be on my way to Hollywood!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I admire your optimism, Mortimer, but it's harder than you think to come up with a movie idea that's stupid in just the right way to get Hollywood's attention at the moment.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: TIC TAC TOE: THE MOTION PICTURE.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: ...Uh, wow. Can I have your desk fan when you move?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's the last time I order pizza from Maurizio's! Enough!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What happened? Was your pizza late? Did it arrive cold? Was the crust undercooked?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Worse! The mashed banana is clearly from a can, and the palm nuts are barely even pre-chewed!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: When you're the customer, Mortimer, the customer is always weird.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know how there are no-bake cookies? Why don't they do that with more stuff? Like, no-bake ziti?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, because the ziti would be hard and the cheese and sauce would be cold.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Maybe they should make a no-imagination Monte. Oh, wait, that's right. IT'S YOU!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm working on some poems. You want to tell me what you think of them?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Sure! I'd love to read your poetry and give you some feedback!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Wait, did you say "read"? Maybe I've got my words wrong. Poems are the things where there's bread and then stuff in between it, right?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ughhhhhh ... I feel sooooo sick.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Did you eat ALL of your Halloween candy in one sitting again?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Don't be ridiculous ...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh good. I was gonna say ...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ... I left one candy corn for you.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You want a peanut butter cup?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No thanks. I started my resolution early.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wow, good for you.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah. That way I'll fall off the wagon just in time for all the yummy holiday foods!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm worried about my See-What-Ya sauce!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Come again?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I've never known how it's pronounced. Siri Rasta sauce? Cree Wacha sauce? You know, the spicy red rooster sauce.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Ohhhhhh, Sriracha sauce.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's what I said!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ...Anyway, long story short, they decided to drop the charges, but I am banned from that particular nail salon for life. Then, wouldn't you know it, as I'm riding the bus home, I come across this bag of something that looked like smushed vanilla cake under my seat. But you're never gonna guess what it actually tasted like...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: You know, "fine" is a perfectly acceptable answer to the question "how was your weekend?"

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Hey, it's day two of the Champunship on Woot's Facebook page, with the top punsters from our community locked in a brutal pun-off all week long!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm boycotting it, until Woot gives this "boy" an explanation about why I "cott" left out of the contestant list.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I bet I could explain it.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Can you believe that a ChamPUN will be chosen today on our Facebook page, and that the winner will go face-to-face with the Woot Writers tomorrow?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I can't. Because that's PUNbelievable!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: We talked about this yesterday, Mortimer. You didn't make the cut.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Congratulations on winning the ChamPUNship and good luck going up against the Woot Writers today on Facebook, Barkwoot, if that is your real name...

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's not his real name. It's his user name.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: He's always one step ahead of me!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Can you believe Barkwoot won the ChamPunship?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yes! The Woot writers' punmanship proved punferior to the puntender.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I bet we can drop the wordplay schtick now.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: MY PUNMITMENT TO PUNS IS PUNENDING.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Sssssqqqqrrrrggggrrrruuuurrrr

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What was that??

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: My stomach is talking to my sandwich.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Where's your sandwich?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: In the fridge.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Are you more of a cat or dog monkey, Mortimer?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, it depends.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I know what you mean, it depends on their personality, needs...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: ...Seasoning, doneness, gravy thickness...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Ugh. My meeting with my armadillo-hormones connection didn't go well at all.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Let me guess: he finally convinced you that injecting armadillo hormones will not help you grow body armor?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, I'll never be convinced of that. THIS guy was trying to pass off ordinary weasel hormones as armadillo hormones! Can you believe that? I'm starting to think buying armadillo hormones on Craigslist is a really stupid idea.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Well, it's part of a really stupid idea, at least.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: The newest episode of Late Morning, the Woot talk show, is on today at 10 AM Pacific/Noon Central!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That show is dead to me ever since they rejected my application to be the bandleader.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: But there's no room in the podcast studio for a band. Seems pretty reasonable for them to say "no thanks".

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: "Unreasonable" is my primary personal brand trait.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Did you hear? This morning on our Late Morning talk show that long-time Woot writer Jason Toon is leaving?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah, I asked him to pick me up a sandwich while he's out.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, Mortimer, not leaving for a little while. Like, he's actually leaving.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You mean I'll never get my sandwich?! Oh no! I can feel the tears coming now!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Wait, about Jason or the sandwich? Wait, no. Don't answer that. On behalf of all of us here, thanks for everything, Jason!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Why do you keep looking from the floor to the ceiling really quick?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Gotta be sure there are no banana peels or anvils around.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, how many times do I have to tell you: they're just cartoons.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: BUT WHAT IF THAT'S WHAT THEY WANT ME TO THINK?!

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you know that today is National Clean Your Refrigerator Day?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: No, I didn't. Is that really a thing?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Yeah. But it's also National America Recycles Day.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And ... ?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I mean, CONSPIRACY MUCH?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Have you heard about the Like-Monkeying sweepstakes on Woot's Facebook page?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I think it's Like-MONGERING. Not Like-Monkeying.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Man, they're always ignoring the primate market.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Marley was dead: to begin with...

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Huh? What are you talking about, Monte? Who's Marley?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I'm reading "A Christmas Carol" aloud for half an hour every day between now and December 25th. Want to join me?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Heck no! Ain't nobody got time for heartwarming Victorian parables on the enduring spirit of empathy and love alive in even the cruelest breast.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Heh heh. Breast.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I think I've come down with a Monday morning bacterial infection.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, you mean "a case of the Mondays"?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Maybe... do "the Mondays" come with a really disgusting armpit rash?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: The hijinks of some of these elected officials are pretty depressing.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, they make me feel much more optimistic.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What? Smoking drugs in office? Knocking old ladies over on the City Council floor? Nude texting? How can that make you feel optimistic?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Because it means there's nothing keeping me from a career in politics.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: And now I feel even worse.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You can be my campaign manager, as long as you can handle the occasional insane rampage.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Have you been following the Shameless Like Mongering sweepstakes on our Facebook page, Mortimer? We're really giving away some cool stuff.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Facebook? PFFT! All the cool kids use Friendster.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Yeah, maybe the cool kids, like, eight years ago.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know, I'm getting a negative vibe here, so I think I'll leave. You can page me when you're ready to apologize.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey, what's with this thing I've been seeing on the site, the Deal Bloatus or something?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: It's the Deal Krampus. It's a quick deal, once a day, at a random time.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: So I don't need to take any over-the-counter medicines to get rid of it?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Did you know that interest rates are still at an all-time low?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Oh, are you considering purchasing a home?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What? No. I just meant that the amount of interest I have in what you have to say is pretty much non-existent.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Nice.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: I'm thinking of getting a baby.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: A baby what?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: A baby baby.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Mortimer, you can't just go out and get a baby.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: That's not what the guy at the baby store told me.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: What's your kink, Monte?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Um, excuse me?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Your kink! You know, like a kink in a rope or cord. The thing that makes you snag on the rest of society.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: I can see how you came to that definition, but I'm not sure if you've got it quite right.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Well, sounds like SOMEBODY is feeling a little kinky today.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Hey! I've got an axe to grind!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Huh? What's the problem?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: No, seriously, I just bought an axe and it could use a good grinding.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: You know what would make this week totally awesome?

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: A social media-driven sweepstakes whereby I only have to click a button and I could win sweet prizes!

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: How would you go about doing that?

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Clicking here: https://www.facebook.com/woot?v=app_194975693850063&rest=1

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: Pfft! I can't believe all these sheep. Celebrating "Thanksgiving" as if it's not some ploy by Big Turkey to get us a day off so we can eat and drink all day with our family.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: That doesn't sound like a ploy. That just sounds nice.

lines.speaker: mortimer
lines.text: They've gotten to you, huh, Monte? That's a real shame.

lines.speaker: monte
lines.text: Look, do you want a drumstick or not?

lines.speaker: mortimer
